typst.markdown_text: 我们希望预测某个价格区域是否可能成为突破或反转发生的水平。为此，我们尝试理解在何种价格区域会有足够多的参与者介入，从而改变供需平衡。AVWAP使我们能够更好地找到这一潜在价格区域，因为它允许我们将瞬时价格与趋势价格进行比较。

samples.source_text: Once we identify a key level of Support or Resistance, we wait for price confirmation (prices begin to move higher) on a shorter timeframe before putting hard earned money to risk.
samples.translated_text: 一旦识别出关键支撑或阻力位，我们会在较短时间框架内等待价格确认（价格开始走高），然后再将辛苦赚来的资金投入风险。
samples.fit_text: 一旦识别出关键支撑或阻力位，我们会在较短时间框架内等待价格确认（价格开始走高），然后再将辛苦赚来的资金投入风险。

samples.source_text: We want to be involved in an emerging move at the onset, not after a trend campaign is well underway and the risks are higher. Do not chase price action. If you miss out, it exposes you to too much risk.
samples.translated_text: 我们希望在趋势启动初期参与，而非在趋势运动已全面展开、风险较高时介入。不要追逐价格走势。若错过时机，将面临过高风险。
samples.fit_text: 我们希望在趋势启动初期参与，而非在趋势运动已全面展开、风险较高时介入。不要追逐价格走势。若错过时机，将面临过高风险。

samples.source_text: Be aware that the more obvious a level seems to be, the more likely it is that other traders will also know that level and they will use it as an area for a stop loss or to exit a portion of their position. This can make the obvious level a place where failed moves are likely, so be careful with stops in these areas.

samples.source_text: We want to anticipate whether a price zone will be a level where a breakout or reversal is likely to occur. To do that we try to understand at what price zone enough participants will get involved to shift the balance of supply and demand. The AVWAP allows us the ability to better find this potential price zone because it allows us to compare the instantaneous prices with the trending price.

samples.source_text: Market Psychology: Self-Reinforcing Role
samples.translated_text: 一个自然的问题是，为什么某些价格区域能够吸引足够的买家和卖家，从而为价格运动提供支撑或阻力？支撑和阻力区域本质上往往是“自我实现”的。当你初次学习技术分析时，可能听过类似这样的说法：多头活跃于50日移动平均线（DMA）之上，而空头则活跃于50日移动平均线之下。

samples.source_text: The natural question is, why do certain price areas attract enough buyers and sellers to provide Support or Resistance to price movement? Support and Resistance areas are often “self-fulfilling” in nature. When you first learn about technical analysis, you likely heard something like, the bulls live above the 50-day moving average (DMA) and bears live below the 50-DMA.

typst.markdown_text: • 持有现金的观望者现在可能会入场，以他们认为的廉价价格买入股票。

samples.source_text: Institutions that own a lot of the stock will sell some of it as it becomes “extended” from the 50-DMA in order to reduce their position, and then rebid for those shares at lower prices as the stock drops back down toward the 50-DMA. This allows them to sell some shares at “high” prices relative to the 50-DMA and replace them at cheaper prices. As they replace the quantity of shares they sold (at higher at the prices) with shares (at the now-lower prices) this could help to create Support at the 50-DMA. The stability from their purchases encourages others to participate at the 50-DMA which helps further strengthen Support.
samples.translated_text: 持有大量股票的机构会在股价偏离50日移动平均线（50-DMA）时卖出部分股票以降低仓位，并在股价回落至50-DMA附近时以更低价格重新买入。这使得他们能够以相对于50-DMA的“较高”价格卖出部分股票，并以更便宜的价格补回。通过以当前较低价格买入的股票替换先前以较高价格卖出的等量股票，这一操作有助于在50-DMA处形成支撑。其买入行为带来的稳定性会吸引其他参与者在50-DMA附近交易，从而进一步强化支撑作用。

typst.markdown_text: 对于图表$2.10 \\& 2.11$左侧的日线图显示一只股票处于上升趋势，但请注意价格两次跌破50日移动平均线。这表明50日移动平均线是一个值得进一步研究的关注区域，而不一定是自动买入或卖出的点位。同样，右侧图表显示下降的50日移动平均线是不要轻信下跌趋势中反弹尝试的理由。正如我们稍后将看到的，我们将以同样的方式使用5日移动平均线进行更短期的分析。

samples.source_text: For charts 2 . 1 0 \& 2 . 1 1 the daily chart on the left shows a stock in an uptrend, but note that the price drops below the 50-DMA twice. This shows that the 50-DMA is an area of interest for further study, not necessarily a point where you should automatically buy or sell. Likewise, the chart on the right shows the declining 50-DMA as a reason not to trust rally attempts in a downtrend. As we will see later, we will make a shorter-term analysis using the 5-DMA in the same way.

samples.source_text: Of course, the 50-DMA is just one example of where and how a price level may attract enough activity to bring about a shift in supply and demand and create a level of Support or Resistance. The supply and demand battles for control of a trend can, and often do, occur at other levels of technical significance you may be familiar with, such as Fibonacci retracement or extension levels, a prior consolidation area, or widely watched moving averages. Our focus here is AVWAP, which complements all the other analysis techniques.
samples.translated_text: 当然，50日移动平均线仅是价格水平可能吸引足够活动、从而引发供需转变并形成支撑或阻力位的一个示例。争夺趋势控制权的供需博弈，可以且经常发生在您可能熟悉的其他技术性重要水平，例如斐波那契回撤或扩展位、先前的盘整区域，或受到广泛关注的移动平均线。我们在此关注的是AVWAP，它是对所有其他分析技术的补充。
samples.fit_text: 当然，50日移动平均线仅是价格水平可能吸引足够活动、从而引发供需转变并形成支撑或阻力位的一个示例。争夺趋势控制权的供需博弈，可以且经常发生在您可能熟悉的其他技术性重要水平，例如斐波那契回撤或扩展位、先前的盘整区域，或受到广泛关注的移动平均线。我们在此关注的是AVWAP，它是对所有其他分析技术的补充。

samples.source_text: It is also important to recognize that if one or more other technical factors show up in the same general area, it can reinforce that area’s level of significance because more participants may be forming their decision to buy, sell, or stand aside in the same area, but each for different reasons. The more people coming to the same price conclusion, in the same area, increases the likelihood it will become Support or Resistance.
samples.translated_text: 同样重要的是要认识到，如果一个或多个其他技术因素出现在同一大致区域，可能会增强该区域的重要性水平，因为更多参与者可能基于不同原因在同一区域形成买入、卖出或观望的决策。在同一区域得出相同价格结论的人越多，该区域成为支撑或阻力的可能性就越大。
samples.fit_text: 同样重要的是要认识到，如果一个或多个其他技术因素出现在同一大致区域，可能会增强该区域的重要性水平，因为更多参与者可能基于不同原因在同一区域形成买入、卖出或观望的决策。在同一区域得出相同价格结论的人越多，该区域成为支撑或阻力的可能性就越大。

samples.source_text: How Would You Feel?
samples.fit_text: How Would You Feel?

samples.source_text: Beyond mere recognition of Support and Resistance zones, true market understanding comes from anticipation of how other participants will act in different scenarios. When you look at a chart, try to imagine how you would feel, and possibly act, if you were long, short, or in cash.
samples.translated_text: 除了识别支撑与阻力区域，真正的市场理解源于预测其他参与者在不同情境下的行为。观察图表时，尝试设想如果你是持有多头、空头或持有现金，你会作何感受，并可能采取何种行动。
samples.fit_text: 除了识别支撑与阻力区域，真正的市场理解源于预测其他参与者在不同情境下的行为。观察图表时，尝试设想如果你是持有多头、空头或持有现金，你会作何感受，并可能采取何种行动。

typst.markdown_text: 除了识别支撑与阻力区域，真正的市场理解源于预测其他参与者在不同情境下的行为。观察图表时，尝试设想如果你是持有多头、空头或持有现金，你会作何感受，并可能采取何种行动。

samples.source_text: If Support breaks, who will buy, sell, or cover? How about when Resistance is broken? Understand the psychology of why Support and Resistance develop, and you will have a significant advantage over most participants who simply look at patterns.
samples.translated_text: 若支撑位被突破，谁会买入、卖出或回补？阻力位被突破时又如何？理解支撑与阻力形成的心理机制，你将比仅关注形态的大多数参与者拥有显著优势。
samples.fit_text: 若支撑位被突破，谁会买入、卖出或回补？阻力位被突破时又如何？理解支撑与阻力形成的心理机制，你将比仅关注形态的大多数参与者拥有显著优势。

typst.markdown_text: 若支撑位被突破，谁会买入、卖出或回补？阻力位被突破时又如何？理解支撑与阻力形成的心理机制，你将比仅关注形态的大多数参与者拥有显著优势。

samples.source_text: Going Deeper: Slope of the AVWAP
samples.fit_text: Going Deeper: Slope of the AVWAP

samples.source_text: One of the most common questions about the volume of trading activity is whether the volume was in buying or selling? Well, of course, it is both. For every buyer, there is a seller and vice versa. What we really want to know is who is more aggressive and who is in control, the buyers or sellers? The direction and slope of the AVWAP allows us to answer that question easily and accurately.
samples.translated_text: 关于交易活动量最常见的问题之一是，交易量是来自买入还是卖出？当然，两者皆有。有买方必有卖方，反之亦然。我们真正想知道的是，买方还是卖方更具主动性并占据主导地位？AVWAP的方向和斜率使我们能够轻松且准确地回答这个问题。
samples.fit_text: 关于交易活动量最常见的问题之一是，交易量是来自买入还是卖出？当然，两者皆有。有买方必有卖方，反之亦然。我们真正想知道的是，买方还是卖方更具主动性并占据主导地位？AVWAP的方向和斜率使我们能够轻松且准确地回答这个问题。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 434.15000000000003pt, height: 26.319999999999993pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p54_item_4_2_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.76pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.54em, fit_height: 26.319999999999993pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 关于交易活动量最常见的问题之一是，交易量是来自买入还是卖出？当然，两者皆有。有买方必有卖方，反之亦然。我们真正想知道的是，买方还是卖方更具主动性并占据主导地位？AVWAP的方向和斜率使我们能够轻松且准确地回答这个问题。

samples.source_text: AVWAP allows us to quantify who has control at any point, buyers or sellers. The Chart 2.12 demonstrates how to determine the directional bias by the slope of the AVWAP. The slope of AVWAP (higher or lower) shows whether the dollar average paid per share for the period studied is rising or declining. When the slope rises, buyers are in control from the anchor point. When the direction of the AVWAP is lower, we know sellers are in control from the anchor point.
samples.translated_text: AVWAP使我们能够量化在任何时点买方或卖方谁占据主导。图2.12展示了如何通过AVWAP的斜率判断方向性偏向。AVWAP的斜率（上升或下降）表明所研究期间每股支付的平均美元价格是上升还是下降。当斜率上升时，买方自锚定点起占据主导。当AVWAP方向向下时，可知卖方自锚定点起占据主导。
samples.fit_text: AVWAP使我们能够量化在任何时点买方或卖方谁占据主导。图2.12展示了如何通过AVWAP的斜率判断方向性偏向。AVWAP的斜率（上升或下降）表明所研究期间每股支付的平均美元价格是上升还是下降。当斜率上升时，买方自锚定点起占据主导。当AVWAP方向向下时，可知卖方自锚定点起占据主导。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 444.59999999999997pt, height: 34.77999999999997pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p54_item_5_3_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.42pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.53em, fit_height: 34.77999999999997pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: AVWAP使我们能够量化在任何时点买方或卖方谁占据主导。图2.12展示了如何通过AVWAP的斜率判断方向性偏向。AVWAP的斜率（上升或下降）表明所研究期间每股支付的平均美元价格是上升还是下降。当斜率上升时，买方自锚定点起占据主导。当AVWAP方向向下时，可知卖方自锚定点起占据主导。

samples.source_text: The direction and rate of change of the AVWAP is often more important than simply being above or below it. The steeper the slope of AVWAP, the more aggressive the trend and higher the likelihood the AVWAP will act as Support on pullbacks and the stock will experience a bounce. The AVWAP can be the reason Support is found as sellers become less aggressive and buyers place bids closer to “fair value.” The AVWAP becomes and reinforces Support levels. The reverse of this takes place when the trend is in the other direction; the AVWAP becomes and reinforces Resistance levels as sellers become more aggressive relative to buyers. We see this below in chart 2.12.
samples.translated_text: AVWAP的方向和变化速率通常比单纯位于其上方或下方更为重要。AVWAP的斜率越陡峭，趋势越强劲，AVWAP在回调时充当支撑、股价出现反弹的可能性就越高。AVWAP可能成为支撑位形成的原因，因为卖方积极性降低，买方出价更接近“公允价值”。AVWAP形成并强化支撑位。当趋势向相反方向发展时，情况则相反：随着卖方相对于买方更为积极，AVWAP形成并强化阻力位。我们在图2.12中可以看到这一点。
samples.fit_text: AVWAP的方向和变化速率通常比单纯位于其上方或下方更为重要。AVWAP的斜率越陡峭，趋势越强劲，AVWAP在回调时充当支撑、股价出现反弹的可能性就越高。AVWAP可能成为支撑位形成的原因，因为卖方积极性降低，买方出价更接近“公允价值”。AVWAP形成并强化支撑位。当趋势向相反方向发展时，情况则相反：随着卖方相对于买方更为积极，AVWAP形成并强化阻力位。我们在图2.12中可以看到这一点。

typst.markdown_text: AVWAP的方向和变化速率通常比单纯位于其上方或下方更为重要。AVWAP的斜率越陡峭，趋势越强劲，AVWAP在回调时充当支撑、股价出现反弹的可能性就越高。AVWAP可能成为支撑位形成的原因，因为卖方积极性降低，买方出价更接近“公允价值”。AVWAP形成并强化支撑位。当趋势向相反方向发展时，情况则相反：随着卖方相对于买方更为积极，AVWAP形成并强化阻力位。我们在图2.12中可以看到这一点。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 431.29999999999995pt, height: 17.8599999999999pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p54_item_9_7_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.69pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 17.8599999999999pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 当平坦的AVWAP被更频繁地测试时，其从支撑转为阻力或从阻力转为支撑的可能性更大。目标是利用该信息来识别低风险、高概率的交易机会。

samples.source_text: As the slope of the AVWAP flattens out, it shows that there is greater market acceptance of the value and the market likely needs more time (and perhaps a price shakeout) before the original trend resumes.
samples.translated_text: 随着AVWAP斜率趋于平缓，表明市场对当前价值的接受度提高，市场可能需要更多时间（或许还需要一次价格调整）才能恢复原有趋势。
samples.fit_text: 随着AVWAP斜率趋于平缓，表明市场对当前价值的接受度提高，市场可能需要更多时间（或许还需要一次价格调整）才能恢复原有趋势。

samples.source_text: When a flat AVWAP gets tested more frequently, it is more likely to flip from Support to Resistance and Resistance to Support. The goal is to use that information to identify low risk, high probability trades.
samples.translated_text: 当平坦的AVWAP被更频繁地测试时，其从支撑转为阻力或从阻力转为支撑的可能性更大。目标是利用该信息来识别低风险、高概率的交易机会。
samples.fit_text: 当平坦的AVWAP被更频繁地测试时，其从支撑转为阻力或从阻力转为支撑的可能性更大。目标是利用该信息来识别低风险、高概率的交易机会。

samples.source_text: A stock with a rising AVWAP shows that buyers are more aggressive than sellers during the timeframe in question. The market must rotate to higher prices in search of liquidity (supply) to satisfy the quantity of buy orders (demand). In Chart 2.13 we set the anchor to the gap on the 27th on this 65-minute timeframe (1). Notice how the AVWAP provided support (green arrows) as buyers stepped up their purchases near the AVWAP on the pullbacks in its uptrend.
samples.translated_text: 当一只股票的AVWAP呈上升趋势时，表明在所考察的时间段内，买方的积极性高于卖方。市场必须转向更高的价格以寻求流动性（供给），从而满足买单（需求）的数量。在图2.13中，我们将锚点设在此65分钟时间框架内27日的缺口处（1）。请注意，当价格在其上升趋势的回调中接近AVWAP时，买家加大了购买力度，AVWAP如何提供了支撑（绿色箭头）。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 442.70000000000005pt, height: 33.84000000000003pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p55_item_3_3_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.34pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.52em, fit_height: 33.84pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 作为市场反转上涨趋势的证据，成交量加权平均价格（VWAP）的方向通常会趋于平缓，价格会向下穿越并受困于VWAP之下，随后开始下跌。在交易日较早阶段，或在所研究的任何时间框架内，由于参与平均计算的数据周期数较少，VWAP的斜率通常处于最极端水平。随着交易日推进，每笔交易对平均值的影响逐渐减弱，VWAP斜率自然趋于平缓。

samples.source_text: Chart 2.14 emphasizes why one should not automatically change from bullish to bearish on a short-term break of the VWAP/AVWAP.
samples.translated_text: 图2.14强调了为何不应因VWAP/AVWAP的短期突破而自动从看涨转为看跌。
samples.fit_text: 图2.14强调了为何不应因VWAP/AVWAP的短期突破而自动从看涨转为看跌。

samples.source_text: As evidence that the market is reversing an advance, the direction of the VWAP will typically flatten out, prices will cross below, get trapped under the VWAP, and then decline. Earlier in the day, or during whatever the timeframe being studied, the slope of the VWAP is typically at its most extreme level because the number of data periods being averaged is lower. As the day progresses, and each transaction has less impact on the average, it is natural to see the slope of the VWAP become less pronounced.

samples.source_text: GENERAL NOTE: All moving averages should be used as a reference point to compare price trends. They should not be considered as automatic levels to do business when they are touched, but as a reason to observe the price action on a shorter-term timeframe, where we formulate a plan. We generally give the benefit of doubt to buyers when the 50-DMA trend is higher, and to sellers when the slope is lower.
samples.translated_text: 总体说明：所有移动平均线应作为比较价格趋势的参考点。它们不应被视为触及即可自动交易的价位，而应作为在更短期时间框架内观察价格行为并制定计划的依据。当50日移动平均线趋势向上时，我们通常倾向于买方；当斜率向下时，则倾向于卖方。

typst.markdown_text: 重要AVWAP的首次或第二次触及更可能出现强劲反弹。这一趋势源于场外观望的买家，他们自认为错过了行情，急于进场买入股票。

samples.source_text: The increase in price shows on the chart as a bullish trend above the rising AVWAP. Consider these stocks innocent until proven guilty during the timeframe that this occurs.

samples.fit_text: 价格上涨在图表上表现为高于上升AVWAP的看涨趋势。在此现象发生的时间段内，应视这些股票为“无罪推定”。

samples.source_text: Buyers should seek opportunities to buy after pullbacks toward the AVWAP, but first wait for evidence of actual support. If there is, then prices will likely rise and we have a logical place for our stop.
samples.translated_text: 买家应在价格回调至AVWAP时寻找买入机会，但首先需等待实际支撑的证据。若支撑成立，价格很可能上涨，我们便有了合理的止损位。
samples.fit_text: 买家应在价格回调至AVWAP时寻找买入机会，但首先需等待实际支撑的证据。若支撑成立，价格很可能上涨，我们便有了合理的止损位。

samples.source_text: The first one or two touches of an important AVWAP are more likely to experience a strong bounce. This tendency occurs as sidelined buyers, who thought they missed the move, rush in to purchase shares.

samples.source_text: As the life of the trend stretches out, the bounces occur with less enthusiasm, which raises our level of caution and our defensive posture. When a bounce is less vigorous it’s because buyers are a less motivated source of demand; thus, the trend more vulnerable to failure.
samples.translated_text: 随着趋势持续时间的延长，反弹力度逐渐减弱，这提高了我们的警惕性并促使我们采取防御姿态。反弹力度减弱是因为买方的需求动机不足，从而导致趋势更易失效。
samples.fit_text: 随着趋势持续时间的延长，反弹力度逐渐减弱，这提高了我们的警惕性并促使我们采取防御姿态。反弹力度减弱是因为买方的需求动机不足，从而导致趋势更易失效。

typst.markdown_text: 与任何支撑位一样，支撑位在未偏离AVWAP的情况下被测试的次数越多、频率越高，其在短期内失效的可能性就越大。

samples.source_text: As with any level of Support, the more times and more frequently Support gets tested without a bounce away from the AVWAP, the more likely it is to fail in the near-term.

typst.markdown_text: 当股价低于下降的平均成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP）时，表明卖方比买方更具动力。交易以更低的价格进行，这在图表上表现为低于下降AVWAP的看跌趋势。

samples.source_text: When a stock is below a declining AVWAP, it tells us that sellers are more motivated than buyers. Transactions occur at lower prices, which show on a chart as a bearish trend, below the declining AVWAP.

typst.markdown_text: 在下跌行情中，市场无法在当前价位承接供给，因此价格会下跌至需求足以吸收供给的水平。当AVWAP处于下降趋势时，应采取偏空的立场，并对开立多头头寸保持谨慎。在此时间框架内，应将这些股票视为有罪，直至其被证明清白。

samples.source_text: In a decline, the market does not accept the supply at current levels, so prices fall to levels where there is sufficient demand to absorb the supply. When the AVWAP is in a downtrend, the stance to take is a short bias and use caution on open long positions. Consider these stocks guilty until proven innocent on the timeframe where this occurs.

typst.markdown_text: 若持有多头头寸并希望平仓，股价反弹至AVWAP附近通常是理想的离场时机，可避免后续回落。若欲建立空头头寸，可在价格从下降的AVWAP回落时开仓，并将止损设在当前时间框架内最近且相关的较低高点之上。

samples.source_text: If you have a long position that you want to get out of, rallies up to the AVWAP are often an excellent point to exit before the stock heads back lower. If you want to enter a short position, you can start new positions as prices fall away from the declining AVWAP, with a stop above the most recent and relevant lower-high for your timeframe.

typst.markdown_text: 图2.15展示了卖方将在下降的AVWAP处提供阻力。我们将初始锚点设定在大幅下跌走势的起点，该走势始于每股$\\$ 60$附近（1）。注意观察随着价格持续下跌，卖方是如何在触及AVWAP时出现的（红色箭头位于3、5、7和9处）。在从60美元（1）测试AVWAP后，卖方已积聚了动能。

samples.source_text: Chart 2.15 shows how sellers will provide Resistance at a declining AVWAP. We set the initial anchor to the start of the large move lower, which began near \$ 60 per share (1). Notice how the sellers showed up on the touches of the AVWAP (red arrows at 3, 5, 7, and 9) as the prices continued their decline. Sellers have picked up momentum after tests of the AVWAP from $60 (1).

typst.body_expr: block(width: 447.45000000000005pt, height: 26.32000000000005pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p57_item_7_6_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.65pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 26.32pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 当价格跌破下降的AVWAP时，我们进行卖空操作（绿色圆圈），预期趋势将继续下行。若股价反转回升并突破AVWAP，我们需设置止损以控制亏损。该止损位设置在近期低点高点之上，以红色箭头（3、5、7、9）标示。例如，若我们在（2）处卖空，止损位将设于前高（3）之上，并随股价持续下跌依此类推。

samples.source_text: When prices fall away from the declining AVWAP, we sell short (green circles) in expectation that the trend will continue lower. If the stock reverses back up through the AVWAP, we want to have a stop in place to minimize any loss. This stop is placed above the recent lower-high, represented with red arrows (3,5,7,9). For instance, if we sell (2) our stop would go above the previous high (3), and so on as the stock continues its decline.

typst.markdown_text: 大型机构通常在事件开始时设定为期数周的平均成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP）计划。随着价格从事件开始接近AVWAP，这些计划会变得更加激进，并提供压倒性的供应，从而形成阻力。

samples.source_text: It is common for large institutions to set multi-week AVWAP programs at the start of an event. Those programs become more aggressive as prices approach the AVWAP from the start of the event and provide overwhelming supply which forms resistance.

samples.source_text: Takeaways on Declining AVWAP
samples.fit_text: Takeaways on Declining AVWAP

samples.source_text: • When the direction of the AVWAP is lower, we should have a bias toward short positions and avoid new long positions.
samples.translated_text: • 当AVWAP方向向下时，我们应偏向空头头寸，并避免新建多头头寸。
samples.fit_text: • 当AVWAP方向向下时，我们应偏向空头头寸，并避免新建多头头寸。

typst.markdown_text: • 卖空者应在价格反弹至AVWAP、遇阻回落时寻求卖出机会。

typst.markdown_text: • 随着趋势持续延伸，下跌动能逐渐减弱，这促使我们提高警惕，并对新的空头头寸采取更为防御性的姿态。

samples.source_text: • As the life of the trend stretches out, the declines occur with less vigor, which raises our level of caution and our defensive posture towards new short sales.

typst.markdown_text: 与任何阻力位类似，阻力被测试的次数越多、频率越高且价格未出现下跌，则其在短期内失效的可能性就越大。

samples.source_text: As with any level of Resistance, the more times and more frequently Resistance gets tested without a decline in price, the more likely it is to fail in the near term.
samples.translated_text: 与任何阻力位类似，阻力被测试的次数越多、频率越高且价格未出现下跌，则其在短期内失效的可能性就越大。
samples.fit_text: 与任何阻力位类似，阻力被测试的次数越多、频率越高且价格未出现下跌，则其在短期内失效的可能性就越大。

samples.source_text: When you observe a flat AVWAP with prices crossing above and below it, my advice is to avoid the stock on that timeframe. This action is neutral and there is no edge in being long or short.
samples.translated_text: 当你观察到一条平坦的AVWAP且价格在其上下交叉时，我的建议是在该时间框架内避开该股票。这一行为是中性的，做多或做空均无优势。
samples.fit_text: 当你观察到一条平坦的AVWAP且价格在其上下交叉时，我的建议是在该时间框架内避开该股票。这一行为是中性的，做多或做空均无优势。

samples.source_text: Rather than complain about choppy action or lack of opportunity, we should interpret the message of the market as meaning one of three things to us; cash is a good position for now, find another market or stock to trade, or switch to a different timeframe to find a better opportunity to get involved.

samples.source_text: In Chart 2.17 we anchored the AVWAP to an important low (1) on the stock above. Initially, the buyers regained control (2), but as the stock traded above and below the AVWAP (3). The stock needed more time to let the buyers and sellers battle it out for control. Trend traders should avoid these stocks until there is greater clarity of who has control.

typst.markdown_text: 对于希望在上涨趋势中大量买入某只股票的机构交易者而言，当股价回调至AVWAP时挂出买单是合理的，因为这为他们提供了一个以相对于开盘价和他们预期收盘价的“公允价值”吸纳股票的机会。

samples.source_text: Market Psychology: Institutions’ Role
samples.fit_text: Market Psychology: Institutions’ Role

samples.source_text: For an institutional trader who wants to buy a large amount of a stock in an uptrend, it makes sense to place bids for the stock as it pulls back down to AVWAP since it is an opportunity for them to scoop up shares at the “fair value” relative to the open and the price they might expect it to close.
samples.translated_text: 对于希望在上涨趋势中大量买入某只股票的机构交易者而言，当股价回调至AVWAP时挂出买单是合理的，因为这为他们提供了一个以相对于开盘价和他们预期收盘价的“公允价值”吸纳股票的机会。
samples.fit_text: 对于希望在上涨趋势中大量买入某只股票的机构交易者而言，当股价回调至AVWAP时挂出买单是合理的，因为这为他们提供了一个以相对于开盘价和他们预期收盘价的“公允价值”吸纳股票的机会。

typst.markdown_text: 当我们预期某一兴趣水平可能演变为支撑或阻力时，我们的任务便是在更短的时间框架内观察该区域，以判断是买方还是卖方正在取得控制权。

samples.source_text: Risk Management
samples.translated_text: 风险最低的交易是那些我们事先有明确计划的交易。明确的计划会预见到一个过渡区域，在该区域中，我们在介入之前会在较短的时间框架内观察价格行为。一些最常见的交易错误源于反应性的FOMO决策。
samples.fit_text: 风险最低的交易是那些我们事先有明确计划的交易。明确的计划会预见到一个过渡区域，在该区域中，我们在介入之前会在较短的时间框架内观察价格行为。一些最常见的交易错误源于反应性的FOMO决策。

samples.source_text: When we anticipate a level of interest which has the potential to become Support or Resistance, it becomes our job to observe that area on a shorter timeframe for evidence of whether it is the buyers or sellers that are gaining control.

typst.markdown_text: • AVWAP能够揭示传统技术分析工具无法识别的市场秩序。

samples.source_text: • AVWAPs can reveal order in the market that traditional technical analysis tools cannot.
samples.translated_text: • AVWAP能够揭示传统技术分析工具无法识别的市场秩序。
samples.fit_text: • AVWAP能够揭示传统技术分析工具无法识别的市场秩序。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 357.20000000000005pt, height: 9.399999999999977pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p60_item_3_2_md, max_size: 10.56pt, min_size: 8.75pt, max_leading: 0.6em, min_leading: 0.5em, fit_height: 9.399999999999977pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: • AVWAP最简单且最有效的用途是发现“隐藏”的支撑位与阻力位。

samples.source_text: • The simplest and most effective use of the AVWAP is to find “hidden” levels of Support and Resistance.
samples.translated_text: • AVWAP最简单且最有效的用途是发现“隐藏”的支撑位与阻力位。
samples.fit_text: • AVWAP最简单且最有效的用途是发现“隐藏”的支撑位与阻力位。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 416.1pt, height: 17.860000000000014pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p60_item_4_3_md, max_size: 10.46pt, min_size: 8.59pt, max_leading: 0.58em, min_leading: 0.47em, fit_height: 17.86pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 与更常见的被视为水平价格盘整的支撑位和阻力位不同，AVWAP的支撑位和阻力位是动态的。它们会随着每一笔交易进行调整。

samples.source_text: Unlike the more common Support and Resistance levels which are seen as horizontal price consolidations, the Support and Resistance levels of an AVWAP are dynamic. They adjust with each trade.
samples.translated_text: 与更常见的被视为水平价格盘整的支撑位和阻力位不同，AVWAP的支撑位和阻力位是动态的。它们会随着每一笔交易进行调整。
samples.fit_text: 与更常见的被视为水平价格盘整的支撑位和阻力位不同，AVWAP的支撑位和阻力位是动态的。它们会随着每一笔交易进行调整。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 277.4pt, height: 10.339999999999975pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p60_item_5_4_md, max_size: 10.56pt, min_size: 9.01pt, max_leading: 0.6em, min_leading: 0.51em, fit_height: 10.339999999999975pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: • AVWAP 使我们能够从任意时点量化买方或卖方谁占据主导。

samples.source_text: • AVWAP allows us to quantify who has control from any point, buyers or sellers.
samples.translated_text: • AVWAP 使我们能够从任意时点量化买方或卖方谁占据主导。
samples.fit_text: • AVWAP 使我们能够从任意时点量化买方或卖方谁占据主导。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 414.20000000000005pt, height: 18.799999999999983pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p60_item_6_5_md, max_size: 10.46pt, min_size: 8.72pt, max_leading: 0.58em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 18.799999999999983pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: • AVWAP的斜率可直观识别方向动量。当斜率上升时，买方自锚点起始即占据主导；反之，若斜率下降，则卖方自锚点起始即占据主导。

samples.source_text: • The slope of the AVWAP easily identifies directional momentum. When it rises, buyers are in control from the start of the anchor. Conversely, if the slope falls, sellers are in control from the start of the anchor.
samples.translated_text: • AVWAP的斜率可直观识别方向动量。当斜率上升时，买方自锚点起始即占据主导；反之，若斜率下降，则卖方自锚点起始即占据主导。
samples.fit_text: • AVWAP的斜率可直观识别方向动量。当斜率上升时，买方自锚点起始即占据主导；反之，若斜率下降，则卖方自锚点起始即占据主导。

typst.markdown_text: • 支撑与阻力区域具有自我强化的作用，这使得理解参与者动机的心理因素至关重要。

samples.source_text: • Support and Resistance areas have a self-reinforcing role, which makes the psychology of the participants’ motivations important to understand.
samples.translated_text: • 支撑与阻力区域具有自我强化的作用，这使得理解参与者动机的心理因素至关重要。
samples.fit_text: • 支撑与阻力区域具有自我强化的作用，这使得理解参与者动机的心理因素至关重要。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 418.0pt, height: 17.860000000000014pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p60_item_8_7_md, max_size: 10.56pt, min_size: 10.04pt, max_leading: 0.6em, min_leading: 0.57em, fit_height: 17.86pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 我们只能在事后识别支撑位和阻力位。在后续价格走势确认之前，它们仅是“关注水平”。

samples.source_text: We only know Support and Resistance levels in hindsight. They are “levels of interest” until confirmed by subsequent price action.
samples.translated_text: 我们只能在事后识别支撑位和阻力位。在后续价格走势确认之前，它们仅是“关注水平”。
samples.fit_text: 我们只能在事后识别支撑位和阻力位。在后续价格走势确认之前，它们仅是“关注水平”。

typst.markdown_text: 交易者应利用AVWAP识别支撑位或阻力位的能力来获取优势。一种方法是等待AVWAP水平被测试，并在股价首次触及AVWAP时买入，期望其能守住该水平。另一种选择是观察AVWAP附近的价格行为，并在较短时间框架内将其视为“关注水平”。

typst.markdown_text: 若选择观望，下一步需等待买方行动坚决且将捍卫AVWAP的证据。一旦明确股价在AVWAP处获得支撑或遇到阻力，即可入场并设置止损。接下来我们将对这两种选择进行更详细的探讨。

samples.source_text: raders should use the AVWAP’s ability to identify levels of Support or Resistance to their advantage. One way to do that is to wait for a test of an AVWAP level and to purchase the stock on the first touch of the AVWAP and hope that it holds. The other option is to observe the action around the AVWAP and treat it as a “level of interest” on a shorter timeframe.
samples.translated_text: 交易者应利用AVWAP识别支撑位或阻力位的能力来获取优势。一种方法是等待AVWAP水平被测试，并在股价首次触及AVWAP时买入，期望其能守住该水平。另一种选择是观察AVWAP附近的价格行为，并在较短时间框架内将其视为“关注水平”。
samples.fit_text: 交易者应利用AVWAP识别支撑位或阻力位的能力来获取优势。一种方法是等待AVWAP水平被测试，并在股价首次触及AVWAP时买入，期望其能守住该水平。另一种选择是观察AVWAP附近的价格行为，并在较短时间框架内将其视为“关注水平”。

samples.source_text: If your choice is to observe, the next step is to wait for evidence that the buyers are serious about their actions and will defend AVWAP. Once it is clear the stock has found Support or Resistance at the AVWAP, we can enter the stock and place a stop. Now we will explore both options in more detail.
samples.translated_text: 若选择观望，下一步需等待买方行动坚决且将捍卫AVWAP的证据。一旦明确股价在AVWAP处获得支撑或遇到阻力，即可入场并设置止损。接下来我们将对这两种选择进行更详细的探讨。
samples.fit_text: 若选择观望，下一步需等待买方行动坚决且将捍卫AVWAP的证据。一旦明确股价在AVWAP处获得支撑或遇到阻力，即可入场并设置止损。接下来我们将对这两种选择进行更详细的探讨。

typst.markdown_text: 当股价触及AVWAP时买入（或卖空）。这并非我的首选策略，因为我更倾向于规避风险，且无法保证股价会从AVWAP反弹。

samples.source_text: Option A: Buy the Touch
samples.translated_text: Paul Levine博士是AVWAP分析的先驱，他提出“孔隙度”这一术语来描述AVWAP是广义区域而非精确数值。尽管如此，有时股票会精确触及AVWAP价位并立即反转。

samples.source_text: Purchase (or sell short) just as the stock touches AVWAP. This is not my preference as I am more risk averse and there is no assurance the stock will bounce from the AVWAP.

typst.markdown_text: 当某只股票价格远高于AVWAP（图表3.1中的点2）时，我对此进行直观判断，而其他人可能使用标准差带。图表3.0展示了AVWAP以及标准差带。

samples.source_text: Paul Levine, PhD was a pioneer in AVWAP analysis and gave the term “porosity” to describe how AVWAPs are general areas, not precise numbers. That said, there are times when a stock will touch the AVWAP to the penny and reverse immediately.
samples.translated_text: Paul Levine博士是AVWAP分析的先驱，他提出“孔隙度”这一术语来描述AVWAP是广义区域而非精确数值。尽管如此，有时股票会精确触及AVWAP价位并立即反转。

samples.source_text: When a stock is far beyond the AVWAP (Point 2 in Chart 3.1), I view this intuitively, while others may use standard deviation bands. Chart 3.0 shows the AVWAP as well as standard deviation bands.

samples.source_text: Chart 3.0 This stock shows the standard AVWAP (purple) along with a 2 standard deviation band of the AVWAP. Chart: ThinkorSwim.com
samples.translated_text: 图3.0 该股票显示了标准AVWAP（紫色）及其2倍标准差带。图表来源：ThinkorSwim.com
samples.fit_text: 图3.0 该股票显示了标准AVWAP（紫色）及其2倍标准差带。图表来源：ThinkorSwim.com

samples.source_text: As always, any trade setup is more likely to be successful when trends are aligned with longer timeframes of the stock and the trend of the overall market. If you buy at each touch of AVWAP, it is sometimes possible to purchase at the exact turning point, but you run the risk the stock may continue lower (Point 4 in Chart 3.1). If the stock does not bounce from the AVWAP, it’s difficult to find a price-based level for your stop because there is often a lack of a lower-high nearby to place the stop under.
samples.translated_text: 与往常一样，当股票趋势与较长时间框架及整体市场趋势一致时，任何交易设置的成功概率更高。如果在每次触及AVWAP时买入，有时可能恰好买在转折点，但也存在股票继续下跌的风险（见图3.1中的点4）。如果股票未从AVWAP反弹，则难以找到基于价格的止损位，因为附近通常缺乏可作为止损参考的次高点。
samples.fit_text: 与往常一样，当股票趋势与较长时间框架及整体市场趋势一致时，任何交易设置的成功概率更高。如果在每次触及AVWAP时买入，有时可能恰好买在转折点，但也存在股票继续下跌的风险（见图3.1中的点4）。如果股票未从AVWAP反弹，则难以找到基于价格的止损位，因为附近通常缺乏可作为止损参考的次高点。

samples.source_text: The stock in Chart 3.1 made an important near-term low on October 24th (1). That is where we set our anchor. Note, that large distance between price and the AVWAP (2). When prices extend far from the AVWAP, new purchases are considered to be riskier.
samples.translated_text: 图3.1中的股票在10月24日形成了一个重要的近期低点（1）。我们将此点设为锚点。注意价格与AVWAP之间存在较大距离（2）。当价格大幅偏离AVWAP时，新买入操作的风险被认为更高。
samples.fit_text: 图3.1中的股票在10月24日形成了一个重要的近期低点（1）。我们将此点设为锚点。注意价格与AVWAP之间存在较大距离（2）。当价格大幅偏离AVWAP时，新买入操作的风险被认为更高。

samples.source_text: On November 2, the stock pulled back to the AVWAP (3) from that low and bounced precisely from that level. Two days later, the stock came back to the AVWAP (4) and this time failed to bounce.
samples.translated_text: 11月2日，股价从该低点回撤至AVWAP（3）并恰好从该水平反弹。两天后，股价再次回到AVWAP（4），但此次未能反弹。

typst.markdown_text: AVWAP水平并非总是自动交易点位，您必须通过试错来决定是倾向于在触及该水平时买入，还是等待股价从AVWAP反弹后再行买入。我将在第7章“买入回调与突破？”中对此进行详细阐述。

samples.source_text: This example shows one instance where the trader would have made money immediately if he purchased the touch of the AVWAP (3). It also shows where the stock continued to decline through the AVWAP, which would have resulted in a loss (4).
samples.translated_text: 该示例展示了一种情况：若交易者买入AVWAP（3）的触碰点，即可立即获利。同时，它也显示了股价持续跌破AVWAP的情形，这将导致亏损（4）。
samples.fit_text: 该示例展示了一种情况：若交易者买入AVWAP（3）的触碰点，即可立即获利。同时，它也显示了股价持续跌破AVWAP的情形，这将导致亏损（4）。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 438.90000000000003pt, height: 17.8599999999999pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p62_item_6_6_md, max_size: 8.61pt, min_size: 8.43pt, max_leading: 0.6em, min_leading: 0.57em, fit_height: 17.8599999999999pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 图3.2中的股票自2月初的锚点起延续了上升趋势，但自低点起在AVWAP（绿色箭头）处的买入操作并未全部立即实现盈利。

samples.source_text: AVWAP levels are not always an automatic place to do business and you must decide through trial and error if you prefer to buy the touch or wait for the stock to bounce from the AVWAP before you make a purchase. I cover this at length in Chapter 7, “Buy Pullbacks and Breakouts?”

samples.source_text: The stock in Chart 3.2 continued in its uptrend from the early February anchor point, but the purchases right at the AVWAP (green arrows) from the low didn’t all immediately result in a gain.
samples.translated_text: 图3.2中的股票自2月初的锚点起延续了上升趋势，但自低点起在AVWAP（绿色箭头）处的买入操作并未全部立即实现盈利。
samples.fit_text: 图3.2中的股票自2月初的锚点起延续了上升趋势，但自低点起在AVWAP（绿色箭头）处的买入操作并未全部立即实现盈利。

samples.source_text: Chart 3.2 Not all touches of the AVWAP will result in an immediate bounce. Chart: TC2000.com
samples.translated_text: 图3.2 并非所有触及AVWAP的情况都会立即反弹。图表来源：TC2000.com
samples.fit_text: 图3.2 并非所有触及AVWAP的情况都会立即反弹。图表来源：TC2000.com

samples.source_text: It can be better to purchase when the stock rises again (the bounce). The green dots show these points. When we purchase with momentum, we take part as the new trend begins. We can place a stop below the preceding higher-low, to protect us in the event that the price action does not follow through. A purchase right at the AVWAP touch does not allow us to have a consistent low-risk stop level, based on price action.
samples.translated_text: 当股价再次上涨（反弹）时买入可能更为有利。绿点标示了这些点位。采用动量策略买入时，我们在新趋势启动时参与。我们可以在前期更高的低点下方设置止损，以在价格走势未能延续时提供保护。若恰好在AVWAP接触点买入，则无法基于价格行为设定一致的低风险止损位。
samples.fit_text: 当股价再次上涨（反弹）时买入可能更为有利。绿点标示了这些点位。采用动量策略买入时，我们在新趋势启动时参与。我们可以在前期更高的低点下方设置止损，以在价格走势未能延续时提供保护。若恰好在AVWAP接触点买入，则无法基于价格行为设定一致的低风险止损位。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 441.75pt, height: 26.32000000000005pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p63_item_3_2_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.76pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.54em, fit_height: 26.32pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 在股价测试AVWAP后反弹时买入是风险较低的做多入场点。在测试AVWAP后下跌时做空是风险较低的做空入场点。在此类设定中，我们有证据表明，在我们投入资金之前，买方已成功守住AVWAP。通过这些做多和做空入场点，动量站在我们这边。

samples.source_text: Option B: Enter with Momentum
samples.translated_text: AVWAP使我们能够精确识别卖方力量被压倒、买方重新掌控局面的时空位置。此类入场方式使我们能在新动量启动之初介入，并轻松界定风险。
samples.fit_text: AVWAP使我们能够精确识别卖方力量被压倒、买方重新掌控局面的时空位置。此类入场方式使我们能在新动量启动之初介入，并轻松界定风险。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 443.65000000000003pt, height: 17.860000000000014pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p63_item_4_3_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.69pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 17.86pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: AVWAP使我们能够精确识别卖方力量被压倒、买方重新掌控局面的时空位置。此类入场方式使我们能在新动量启动之初介入，并轻松界定风险。

samples.source_text: Purchasing the stock after it tests the AVWAP and bounces away from it is the lower-risk entry. A short sale after a test of AVWAP followed by a decline is the lower-risk short entry. In this type of set-up we have evidence that buyers have defended the AVWAP successfully before we put our money at risk. Momentum is on our side with these entries, long and short.
samples.translated_text: 在股价测试AVWAP后反弹时买入是风险较低的做多入场点。在测试AVWAP后下跌时做空是风险较低的做空入场点。在此类设定中，我们有证据表明，在我们投入资金之前，买方已成功守住AVWAP。通过这些做多和做空入场点，动量站在我们这边。
samples.fit_text: 在股价测试AVWAP后反弹时买入是风险较低的做多入场点。在测试AVWAP后下跌时做空是风险较低的做空入场点。在此类设定中，我们有证据表明，在我们投入资金之前，买方已成功守住AVWAP。通过这些做多和做空入场点，动量站在我们这边。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 437.0pt, height: 17.859999999999957pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p63_item_5_4_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.69pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 17.859999999999957pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 我倾向于从一开始就把握正确方向，在趋势形成时入场交易。让他人抢先行动、承担“脏活累活”并在支撑形成过程中承受更大风险。若判断方向正确，仍将有充足的盈利机会，且风险更低。

samples.source_text: The AVWAP allows us to find exactly where and when the sellers are overwhelmed and buyers have regained control. This type of entry allows us to enter at the onset of new momentum and easily define our risk.
samples.translated_text: AVWAP使我们能够精确识别卖方力量被压倒、买方重新掌控局面的时空位置。此类入场方式使我们能在新动量启动之初介入，并轻松界定风险。
samples.fit_text: AVWAP使我们能够精确识别卖方力量被压倒、买方重新掌控局面的时空位置。此类入场方式使我们能在新动量启动之初介入，并轻松界定风险。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 438.90000000000003pt, height: 17.8599999999999pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p63_item_6_5_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.69pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 17.8599999999999pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 耐心等待信号（一般准则是在你所处的时间框架内出现3-5根K线）再入场，以便获得一个可供交易的价位水平。我们希望**在买方或卖方重新掌控局面之后**入场。在下跌后买入强势，在上涨后做空弱势。

samples.source_text: I like to be right from the start and enter a trade with momentum. Let others jump the gun, do the “dirty work,” and take the larger associated risks while Support forms. If you are in the right neighborhood, there will still be plenty of opportunity for profit and with less risk.
samples.translated_text: 我倾向于从一开始就把握正确方向，在趋势形成时入场交易。让他人抢先行动、承担“脏活累活”并在支撑形成过程中承受更大风险。若判断方向正确，仍将有充足的盈利机会，且风险更低。
samples.fit_text: 我倾向于从一开始就把握正确方向，在趋势形成时入场交易。让他人抢先行动、承担“脏活累活”并在支撑形成过程中承受更大风险。若判断方向正确，仍将有充足的盈利机会，且风险更低。

samples.source_text: Wait patiently for the signal (a general guideline would be 3-5 bars for the timeframe you are on) to enter so you have a level to trade against. We want to enter AFTER buyers or sellers have regained control. Buy strength after the dip and short weakness after the rip.
samples.translated_text: 耐心等待信号（一般准则是在你所处的时间框架内出现3-5根K线）再入场，以便获得一个可供交易的价位水平。我们希望**在买方或卖方重新掌控局面之后**入场。在下跌后买入强势，在上涨后做空弱势。
samples.fit_text: 耐心等待信号（一般准则是在你所处的时间框架内出现3-5根K线）再入场，以便获得一个可供交易的价位水平。我们希望**在买方或卖方重新掌控局面之后**入场。在下跌后买入强势，在上涨后做空弱势。

samples.source_text: Chart 3.3 The lower-risk short entries occur after the AVWAP has been tested. Chart: TC2000.com

samples.fit_text: 图3.3 低风险空头入场点出现在AVWAP被测试之后。图表来源：TC2000.com

typst.body_expr: block(width: 438.90000000000003pt, height: 26.32000000000005pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p64_item_2_2_md, max_size: 8.77pt, min_size: 7.6pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 26.32pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: AVWAP（红色箭头）处存在多个触及点，这些位置本可以以最小的初始亏损做空股票，这使得在AVWAP处做空颇具吸引力。事后看来，我们知道这些交易本应获利，但在当时这些交易可能被触发时，其动量与止损价位并不明确。这使得这些入场点具有风险。

samples.source_text: In Chart 3.3, the stock made an important high in early January. Once the stock dropped for a few days, that high became the logical place to set our AVWAP (blue arrow).
samples.translated_text: 在图3.3中，该股票于1月初创下重要高点。当股价随后下跌数日后，该高点便成为设置AVWAP（蓝色箭头）的合理位置。
samples.fit_text: 在图3.3中，该股票于1月初创下重要高点。当股价随后下跌数日后，该高点便成为设置AVWAP（蓝色箭头）的合理位置。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 443.65000000000003pt, height: 17.860000000000014pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p64_item_3_3_md, max_size: 8.61pt, min_size: 8.43pt, max_leading: 0.6em, min_leading: 0.57em, fit_height: 17.86pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 另一种选择是等待股价偏离AVWAP后下跌，并在下跌动能增强时做空（绿点）。随后，我们可以在前高之上设置止损位（红箭头）。

samples.source_text: There were several touches of the AVWAP (red arrows) where the stock could have been shorted with minimal initial loss and that makes it tempting to short at the AVWAP. With the benefit of hindsight, we know these trades would have been profitable, but at that moment, when these trades might have been initiated, the momentum and stop placement price was not clear. That makes these entries risky.

typst.body_expr: block(width: 441.75pt, height: 17.860000000000014pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p64_item_4_4_md, max_size: 8.61pt, min_size: 8.43pt, max_leading: 0.6em, min_leading: 0.57em, fit_height: 17.86pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 后一种入场点确保股票在入场前已朝预期方向移动，同时也为我们提供了一个易于识别的止损位，以防股价反转回升。

samples.source_text: The other option is to wait for the stock to drop away from the AVWAP and short as the downward momentum builds (green dots). We could then place a stop above the preceding high (red arrows).
samples.translated_text: 另一种选择是等待股价偏离AVWAP后下跌，并在下跌动能增强时做空（绿点）。随后，我们可以在前高之上设置止损位（红箭头）。
samples.fit_text: 另一种选择是等待股价偏离AVWAP后下跌，并在下跌动能增强时做空（绿点）。随后，我们可以在前高之上设置止损位（红箭头）。

typst.markdown_text: 在图3.4中，我们可以看到，当股价触及AVWAP时进行卖空操作（红色箭头所示）可能导致震荡交易，甚至更糟，造成亏损。

samples.source_text: This latter entry point assures the stock is moving in the desired direction before our entry. It also provides us with an easily identifiable level for our stop loss in case the stock reverses back higher.
samples.translated_text: 后一种入场点确保股票在入场前已朝预期方向移动，同时也为我们提供了一个易于识别的止损位，以防股价反转回升。
samples.fit_text: 后一种入场点确保股票在入场前已朝预期方向移动，同时也为我们提供了一个易于识别的止损位，以防股价反转回升。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 439.84999999999997pt, height: 26.319999999999936pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p64_item_6_6_md, max_size: 8.77pt, min_size: 8.71pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.54em, fit_height: 26.319999999999936pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 如前所述，我倾向于在股价从AVWAP（由绿点标示）回落时建立空头头寸，此时下行动量得到确认。随后，我们可将止损设置在最近的相关次高点上方。当基于价格行为设定止损位时，交易从一开始就具备了更高的确定性和信心。

samples.source_text: In Chart 3.4 we can see how selling short as the stock touches AVWAP (red arrows) would have resulted in choppy trading or, worse, losses.
samples.translated_text: 在图3.4中，我们可以看到，当股价触及AVWAP时进行卖空操作（红色箭头所示）可能导致震荡交易，甚至更糟，造成亏损。
samples.fit_text: 在图3.4中，我们可以看到，当股价触及AVWAP时进行卖空操作（红色箭头所示）可能导致震荡交易，甚至更糟，造成亏损。

samples.source_text: As noted, I prefer to start a short position as the stock drops away from the AVWAP (indicated by the green dots), where the downward momentum is confirmed. Then we can place a stop above the most recent relevant lower-high. When you set your stop level based on price action, it allows for greater certainty and confidence in the trade from the start.
samples.translated_text: 如前所述，我倾向于在股价从AVWAP（由绿点标示）回落时建立空头头寸，此时下行动量得到确认。随后，我们可将止损设置在最近的相关次高点上方。当基于价格行为设定止损位时，交易从一开始就具备了更高的确定性和信心。

samples.translated_text: 图3.4 当价格频繁上下穿越AVWAP时，风险难以控制。图表来源：TC2000.com
samples.fit_text: 图3.4 当价格频繁上下穿越AVWAP时，风险难以控制。图表来源：TC2000.com

typst.body_expr: block(width: 444.59999999999997pt, height: 26.32000000000005pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p65_item_2_1_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.76pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.54em, fit_height: 26.32pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 凡事并无唯一正确之法，唯有适合你自身之道。无论你选择在股价触及AVWAP时立即买入，还是等待更多证据再行入场，大多数人会倾向于其中一种方式。你可能对入场位置有天然的偏好，也可能需要通过尝试两种方法来逐步形成自己的偏好。

samples.source_text: Which is Right for You?
samples.translated_text: 有些人可能更倾向于采用混合策略，即在首次触及AVWAP时建立头寸，待其确认为动量支撑或阻力位后再加仓。一如既往，选择适合自己的方式，让交易符合个人风格。
samples.fit_text: 有些人可能更倾向于采用混合策略，即在首次触及AVWAP时建立头寸，待其确认为动量支撑或阻力位后再加仓。一如既往，选择适合自己的方式，让交易符合个人风格。

typst.markdown_text: 有些人可能更倾向于采用混合策略，即在首次触及AVWAP时建立头寸，待其确认为动量支撑或阻力位后再加仓。一如既往，选择适合自己的方式，让交易符合个人风格。

samples.source_text: There is no one right way for everything, only the right way for you. Whether you decide your approach is to buy just as the stock touches the AVWAP, or if you wait for further evidence before you make an entry. Most people will gravitate toward one or the other. You might have a natural preference about the entry location, or it might take time to develop your preference as you try both methods.
samples.translated_text: 凡事并无唯一正确之法，唯有适合你自身之道。无论你选择在股价触及AVWAP时立即买入，还是等待更多证据再行入场，大多数人会倾向于其中一种方式。你可能对入场位置有天然的偏好，也可能需要通过尝试两种方法来逐步形成自己的偏好。
samples.fit_text: 凡事并无唯一正确之法，唯有适合你自身之道。无论你选择在股价触及AVWAP时立即买入，还是等待更多证据再行入场，大多数人会倾向于其中一种方式。你可能对入场位置有天然的偏好，也可能需要通过尝试两种方法来逐步形成自己的偏好。

samples.source_text: Some people might even prefer to take a hybrid approach where they start their position on the first touch of the AVWAP and then add to the position after it proves to be momentum Support or Resistance. As always, do what is right for you. Make the trade your own.

typst.markdown_text: 价格历史研究的一个基本原则是“跌破的支撑位会转变为阻力位”。反之亦然，“突破的阻力位会转变为支撑位”。

samples.source_text: Market Psychology: Price Has Memory, so Use It
samples.fit_text: Market Psychology: Price Has Memory, so Use It

samples.source_text: One of the basic tenets of the study of price history is that “broken support acts as resistance.” The opposite is also true, “broken resistance acts as support.”
samples.translated_text: 价格历史研究的一个基本原则是“跌破的支撑位会转变为阻力位”。反之亦然，“突破的阻力位会转变为支撑位”。
samples.fit_text: 价格历史研究的一个基本原则是“跌破的支撑位会转变为阻力位”。反之亦然，“突破的阻力位会转变为支撑位”。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 438.90000000000003pt, height: 17.8599999999999pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p65_item_7_4_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.69pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 17.8599999999999pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 你是否曾买入一只股票，看着其价格下跌，并希望能在盈亏平衡点离场？我也有过类似经历。这种买家的悔恨情绪在图表上体现为阻力位。

samples.source_text: Support Becomes Resistance
samples.translated_text: 当股价回升至买入价并挂出卖单时，会增加供给，从而对价格上行形成阻碍，尽管这种阻碍可能是暂时的。显然，单个人的股票卖出通常不会构成明显的阻力。然而，当卖出发生在许多市场参与者共同认可的价位（价格记忆）时，例如重要的移动平均线或AVWAP平均水平，股票在进一步上涨前需要消化大量供给，这使得突破变得更为困难。
samples.fit_text: 当股价回升至买入价并挂出卖单时，会增加供给，从而对价格上行形成阻碍，尽管这种阻碍可能是暂时的。显然，单个人的股票卖出通常不会构成明显的阻力。然而，当卖出发生在许多市场参与者共同认可的价位（价格记忆）时，例如重要的移动平均线或AVWAP平均水平，股票在进一步上涨前需要消化大量供给，这使得突破变得更为困难。

typst.markdown_text: 当股价回升至买入价并挂出卖单时，会增加供给，从而对价格上行形成阻碍，尽管这种阻碍可能是暂时的。显然，单个人的股票卖出通常不会构成明显的阻力。然而，当卖出发生在许多市场参与者共同认可的价位（价格记忆）时，例如重要的移动平均线或AVWAP平均水平，股票在进一步上涨前需要消化大量供给，这使得突破变得更为困难。

samples.source_text: Have you ever purchased a stock, watched it decline in price and wish you could just get out at a breakeven? Me too. This type of buyer’s remorse shows up on a chart as Resistance.
samples.translated_text: 你是否曾买入一只股票，看着其价格下跌，并希望能在盈亏平衡点离场？我也有过类似经历。这种买家的悔恨情绪在图表上体现为阻力位。
samples.fit_text: 你是否曾买入一只股票，看着其价格下跌，并希望能在盈亏平衡点离场？我也有过类似经历。这种买家的悔恨情绪在图表上体现为阻力位。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 437.0pt, height: 26.319999999999936pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p65_item_9_6_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.76pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.54em, fit_height: 26.319999999999936pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 此处也别忘了空头。当支撑位被跌破时，空头便掌控了该股票，他们会试图通过增加卖盘来捍卫其空头头寸，不仅希望增持空仓，还意图恐吓多头持有者因担心更大亏损而平仓，这有利于他们的空头押注。

samples.source_text: When the stock rallies back to the purchase price and you place your sell order, it adds supply, which becomes a block, however temporary, to the price advancement. Obviously, one person’s sale of stock won’t typically offer much Resistance. When sales occur at a widely recognized level (price memory) shared by many participants, such as a significant moving average or average of AVWAP level, it becomes much harder for the stock to work through the large supply being offered before it can move higher.
samples.translated_text: 当股价回升至买入价并挂出卖单时，会增加供给，从而对价格上行形成阻碍，尽管这种阻碍可能是暂时的。显然，单个人的股票卖出通常不会构成明显的阻力。然而，当卖出发生在许多市场参与者共同认可的价位（价格记忆）时，例如重要的移动平均线或AVWAP平均水平，股票在进一步上涨前需要消化大量供给，这使得突破变得更为困难。

samples.source_text: Don’t forget about short sellers here, too. When Support breaks, short sellers are in control of the stock and they will try to defend their short position by adding more supply in hopes of not only adding to their short position, but also to scare long holders to liquidate their positions in fear of larger losses, which benefits their short bet.

typst.markdown_text: 图3.6是一个更直接的示例，展示了AVWAP如何从提供支撑（绿色箭头）转变为阻力（红色箭头）。

samples.source_text: Notice that in Chart 3.5 there were no actual tests of the rising AVWAP. This is an example of a “non-textbook example.” We often give analysis of AVWAP, or any market approach, an improbable expectation of success. In real trading, it isn’t as perfect as some books would lead you to believe. Remember, there is the science of analysis and the more difficult art of trading.
samples.translated_text: 注意，图表3.5中并未对上升的AVWAP进行实际测试。这是一个“非教科书式示例”。我们常常对AVWAP分析或任何市场方法抱有不切实际的成功期望。在实际交易中，它并不像某些书籍所描述的那样完美。请记住，分析是科学，而交易则是更为困难的艺术。
samples.fit_text: 注意，图表3.5中并未对上升的AVWAP进行实际测试。这是一个“非教科书式示例”。我们常常对AVWAP分析或任何市场方法抱有不切实际的成功期望。在实际交易中，它并不像某些书籍所描述的那样完美。请记住，分析是科学，而交易则是更为困难的艺术。

samples.source_text: Chart 3.6 is a more straightforward example of how the AVWAP, which once provided Support (green arrows) became Resistance (red arrows).

samples.source_text: Chart 3.7 This stock demonstrates how Support (green arrows) when broken, turns to Resistance (red arrows). This can occur on more than one instance on the same chart. Chart: TC2000.com
samples.translated_text: 图3.7 该股票展示了支撑位（绿色箭头）被突破后如何转变为阻力位（红色箭头）。同一图表上可能出现多个此类实例。图表来源：TC2000.com
samples.fit_text: 图3.7 该股票展示了支撑位（绿色箭头）被突破后如何转变为阻力位（红色箭头）。同一图表上可能出现多个此类实例。图表来源：TC2000.com

samples.source_text: In Chart 3.7, the AVWAP from the October low (blue arrow on left) showed Support twice (1) and (2) before it flipped to Resistance in early January (3) and then again in early February (4). If you find me on Twitter in a situation like this, you might hear me refer to points (3) and (4) as “AVWAP Tap and Crap” or “AVWAP Thump and Dump.”
samples.translated_text: 在图3.7中，从10月低点（左侧蓝色箭头）开始的AVWAP在1月初（3）和2月初（4）两次显示为支撑（1）和（2），随后转为阻力。如果你在Twitter上关注我，遇到类似情况，可能会听到我将点（3）和（4）称为“AVWAP轻触即跌”或“AVWAP重击即抛”。
samples.fit_text: 在图3.7中，从10月低点（左侧蓝色箭头）开始的AVWAP在1月初（3）和2月初（4）两次显示为支撑（1）和（2），随后转为阻力。如果你在Twitter上关注我，遇到类似情况，可能会听到我将点（3）和（4）称为“AVWAP轻触即跌”或“AVWAP重击即抛”。

samples.source_text: When the green AVWAP is anchored from the January low (5), notice how the AVWAP acted as Resistance twice after the start of the decline: (6) in early February, and (7) in mid-February.
samples.translated_text: 当绿色AVWAP以1月低点(5)为锚点时，可观察到AVWAP在下跌开始后两次充当阻力位：(6)在2月初，以及(7)在2月中旬。
samples.fit_text: 当绿色AVWAP以1月低点(5)为锚点时，可观察到AVWAP在下跌开始后两次充当阻力位：(6)在2月初，以及(7)在2月中旬。

typst.markdown_text: 当绿色AVWAP以1月低点(5)为锚点时，可观察到AVWAP在下跌开始后两次充当阻力位：(6)在2月初，以及(7)在2月中旬。

samples.source_text: We use price charts to understand the collective reasoning and psychology of market participants so we can objectively prepare our trade plan. When traditional Support breaks and then acts as Resistance, it means that there has been a greater number of participants anchored at that price level which, in turn can lead to a larger opposite move when the price breaks.
samples.translated_text: 我们利用价格图表来理解市场参与者的集体推理与心理，从而客观地制定交易计划。当传统支撑位被突破后转而充当阻力位时，意味着有更多参与者锚定在该价格水平，这进而可能导致价格突破时产生更大幅度的反向运动。
samples.fit_text: 我们利用价格图表来理解市场参与者的集体推理与心理，从而客观地制定交易计划。当传统支撑位被突破后转而充当阻力位时，意味着有更多参与者锚定在该价格水平，这进而可能导致价格突破时产生更大幅度的反向运动。

typst.markdown_text: 我们利用价格图表来理解市场参与者的集体推理与心理，从而客观地制定交易计划。当传统支撑位被突破后转而充当阻力位时，意味着有更多参与者锚定在该价格水平，这进而可能导致价格突破时产生更大幅度的反向运动。

samples.source_text: When price breaks a major level of Support, the trapped longs will have a propensity to get out at breakeven and as they sell shares, the supply from these participants helps to form Resistance.
samples.translated_text: 当价格跌破重要支撑位时，被套的多头倾向于在盈亏平衡点离场，他们卖出股票的行为所形成的供给有助于形成阻力。
samples.fit_text: 当价格跌破重要支撑位时，被套的多头倾向于在盈亏平衡点离场，他们卖出股票的行为所形成的供给有助于形成阻力。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 438.90000000000003pt, height: 17.860000000000014pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p67_item_2_2_md, max_size: 8.83pt, min_size: 8.65pt, max_leading: 0.6em, min_leading: 0.57em, fit_height: 17.86pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 当价格跌破重要支撑位时，被套的多头倾向于在盈亏平衡点离场，他们卖出股票的行为所形成的供给有助于形成阻力。

samples.source_text: Resistance Becomes Support
samples.fit_text: Resistance Becomes Support

samples.source_text: The same emotions come into play when a stock forms Support on a chart. Have you ever sold a stock after it has done nothing for months and then see it almost immediately turn higher without you? It is as if “they” know you are out and agree to take the stock higher without you.
samples.translated_text: 当一只股票在图表上形成支撑位时，同样的情绪也会发挥作用。你是否曾在一只股票数月毫无表现后将其卖出，随后却几乎立即看到它在你离场后开始上涨？这仿佛“他们”知道你已离场，并一致决定在你缺席时推高股价。
samples.fit_text: 当一只股票在图表上形成支撑位时，同样的情绪也会发挥作用。你是否曾在一只股票数月毫无表现后将其卖出，随后却几乎立即看到它在你离场后开始上涨？这仿佛“他们”知道你已离场，并一致决定在你缺席时推高股价。

samples.source_text: Of course this doesn’t really happen, but you probably wish you could buy your shares back at your original cost. As sellers start to take advantage of the rising price, causing the stock to pull back toward the breakout level, you may decide to get back in. Others, with sidelined cash may also be motivated to join in on what appears to be a new uptrend.
samples.translated_text: 当然，这种情况实际上并不会发生，但你可能希望以原始成本回购你的股份。随着卖家开始利用价格上涨获利，导致股价回落至突破位附近，你可能会决定重新入场。其他持有闲置资金的投资者也可能受到激励，加入这看似新形成的上升趋势。

samples.source_text: Don’t forget about those short sellers. If you were shorting the stock and it breaks out, it may motivate you to purchase your stock on the pullback before it begins a move higher. These short-covering purchases are strictly about limiting losses, which can quickly spiral out of control if the stock follows through on the breakout. These actions, repeated by many buyers and sellers, form Support for a stock’s price.
samples.translated_text: 不要忽视那些卖空者。如果你正在做空该股票，而它突破了阻力位，这可能会促使你在股价回调时买入股票，以防其开始上涨。这些空头回补行为纯粹是为了限制损失，如果股价在突破后继续上涨，损失可能迅速失控。这些由众多买卖双方重复进行的操作，共同构成了股票价格的支撑。

samples.source_text: Both techniques of measuring Support and Resistance are valuable, but by using AVWAP, pricing activity is revealed to be more granular and you can see subtle changes in trends. This makes it easier for you to more precisely time a purchase or sale.

samples.source_text: Active traders who use traditional measures of Support would miss a number of opportunities to buy or sell. For example, based on what you’ve read so far, can you see in Chart 3.11 where the two green arrows show areas of interest for setting up trades? These areas are not so easily identified in Chart 3.10. By using AVWAP, traders can see the transition from Support to Resistance more quickly and precisely.
samples.translated_text: 使用传统支撑位指标的交易者会错失许多买卖机会。例如，根据你目前所读内容，能否在图3.11中看到两个绿色箭头标示的交易设置关注区域？这些区域在图3.10中不易识别。通过使用AVWAP，交易者能更快速、更精确地观察到支撑位向阻力位的转换。

samples.source_text: The more times Support or Resistance is tested, the more likely it is to fail. This is a simple, but important concept. Traders’ impatience is demonstrated by the frequency of tests made to Support and Resistance. Monitor this impatience carefully for signs of potential for the price to move out
samples.translated_text: 支撑或阻力被测试的次数越多，其失效的可能性就越大。这是一个简单但重要的概念。交易者的急躁情绪通过支撑和阻力被测试的频率得以体现。需密切关注这种急躁情绪，以寻找价格可能突破的迹象。

typst.markdown_text: 在当前区间内，价格水平的突破可能引发普通交易者交易活动的重要转折，并且，正如我们将看到的，这些突破点是锚定AVWAP的绝佳位置。

samples.source_text: When traditional Resistance breaks and then acts as Support, larger moves often occur on the upside. When prices break a major level of Resistance, the trapped shorts will look to get out at break even by purchasing shares to cover their positions and the demand from these participants helps to form Support. The two charts below show the concept of how broken Resistance becomes Support. I came up with a couple of corny ways to remember them, “AVWAP Kiss and Run” or “AVWAP Tap and Snap.”
samples.translated_text: 当传统阻力位被突破并随后转为支撑位时，价格往往会出现较大的上行波动。当价格突破主要阻力位时，被套的空头会寻求在盈亏平衡点平仓，通过买入股票来回补头寸，这些参与者的需求有助于形成支撑。下方两张图表展示了被突破的阻力位如何转变为支撑位的概念。我想出了几个通俗的说法来帮助记忆，即“AVWAP 一触即离”或“AVWAP 轻触即弹”。

samples.source_text: In Chart 3.13, the daily chart saw the AVWAP from the early peak act as Resistance (red arrows) before buyers took control and then defended pullbacks to the AVWAP, where it formed Support (green arrows). Eventually, the buyers soaked up all the supply offered, and the stock experienced a great run higher.

samples.source_text: The longer a consolidation zone (period of relative stability in price) has taken time to develop, the greater the move away from it will be as more energy has built up. There is a larger group of traders who will benefit from a momentum push away from the consolidation. A good way to remember this is: the bigger the top, the bigger the drop and, the bigger the base, the higher in space.
samples.translated_text: 盘整区（价格相对稳定的时期）形成的时间越长，其后的突破幅度就越大，因为积累了更多能量。将有更大规模的交易者群体从盘整后的动量突破中获利。一个便于记忆的方法是：顶部越大，跌幅越大；底部越宽，上行空间越高。

typst.markdown_text: 当我们买入一只股票时，了解其潜在走势是有益的，而来自先前重要峰值的AVWAP通常会成为该股票的阻力位。当股票出现大幅波动，且图表上找不到传统的支撑或阻力位（除了AVWAP）时，参考先前的重要高点和低点作为锚定点是一个好方法。

samples.source_text: Key Levels and Price Targets
samples.fit_text: Key Levels and Price Targets

samples.source_text: When we purchase a stock, it is good to know where it has the potential to go and an AVWAP from a prior important peak will often become Resistance for the stock. When a stock makes a large move and there are no levels of traditional Support or Resistance to be found on the chart (except for the AVWAP), it is a good idea to look at prior important highs and lows as anchor points.
samples.translated_text: 当我们买入一只股票时，了解其潜在走势是有益的，而来自先前重要峰值的AVWAP通常会成为该股票的阻力位。当股票出现大幅波动，且图表上找不到传统的支撑或阻力位（除了AVWAP）时，参考先前的重要高点和低点作为锚定点是一个好方法。

typst.markdown_text: 如果您基于较短时间框架的分析买入该股票（3.16），明智的做法是了解AVWAP相对于峰值（绿点）的位置。该股票曾两次快速上涨至AVWAP，随后回落。这在经历向AVWAP反弹的股票中是常见现象。

samples.source_text: It is uncanny how often you will see a stock halt at an AVWAP from a prior high or low. Knowledge of these levels in advance allow us to approximate a “target” for the price move. Price targets are not necessarily a place to take profit, but to manage risk with tighter stops, so that if the stock does reverse, we can lock in large gains.
samples.translated_text: 令人惊讶的是，股票价格经常会在前期高点或低点的AVWAP处停滞。提前了解这些价位使我们能够近似估算价格波动的“目标”。价格目标不一定是获利了结的位置，而是用于通过更紧密的止损来管理风险，这样如果股票确实反转，我们就能锁定较大的收益。

typst.markdown_text: 支撑位与阻力位并非绝对。它们无法保证一定有效。市场的基调最终会从看涨转为看跌，反之亦然。当关键的支撑位与阻力位被突破时，应准备好采取行动以保护已累积的收益，因为相反方向的剧烈波动可能迅速出现。接下来的四张图表展示了两个看涨（图3.18与3.19）和两个看跌（图3.20与3.21）的示例。

samples.source_text: Going Deeper: Support and Resistance Failure
samples.fit_text: Going Deeper: Support and Resistance Failure

samples.source_text: Support and Resistance levels are not concrete. They are not guaranteed to hold. Eventually the tone of the market will change from bullish to bearish and vice versa. When key Support and Resistance levels break, be ready to act to protect your accumulated gains because large moves in the opposite direction can come quickly. The next four charts illustrate two bullish (3.18 and 3.19) and two bearish (3.20 and 3.21) examples.
samples.translated_text: 支撑位与阻力位并非绝对。它们无法保证一定有效。市场的基调最终会从看涨转为看跌，反之亦然。当关键的支撑位与阻力位被突破时，应准备好采取行动以保护已累积的收益，因为相反方向的剧烈波动可能迅速出现。接下来的四张图表展示了两个看涨（图3.18与3.19）和两个看跌（图3.20与3.21）的示例。
samples.fit_text: 支撑位与阻力位并非绝对。它们无法保证一定有效。市场的基调最终会从看涨转为看跌，反之亦然。当关键的支撑位与阻力位被突破时，应准备好采取行动以保护已累积的收益，因为相反方向的剧烈波动可能迅速出现。接下来的四张图表展示了两个看涨（图3.18与3.19）和两个看跌（图3.20与3.21）的示例。

samples.source_text: Chart 3.18 On this daily chart, we see how the more frequently AVWAP Support is tested the more likely it is to fail.
samples.translated_text: 图3.18 该日线图显示，AVWAP支撑位被测试得越频繁，其失效的可能性越大。
samples.fit_text: 图3.18 该日线图显示，AVWAP支撑位被测试得越频繁，其失效的可能性越大。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 437.95000000000005pt, height: 35.72000000000003pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p76_item_2_2_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.5pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.53em, fit_height: 35.72pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 图3.18和3.19展示了在趋势反转下跌前，股价反复测试前期显著低点AVWAP的案例。随着股价经历可观的上涨，AVWAP的上升速率放缓，导致市场对AVWAP的测试更加频繁。价格最终跌破AVWAP支撑并开始快速下跌。认识到这种倾向，可以使你在反转发生前，带着大部分利润了结盈利头寸。对于更激进的交易者而言，价格从AVWAP回落也可为反转交易提供早期入场机会。

samples.source_text: Chart 3.19 On this 65-minute chart, we see how the more frequently AVWAP Support is tested the more likely it is to fail. Charts: TC2000.com
samples.translated_text: 图3.19 在这张65分钟图上，我们看到AVWAP支撑被测试得越频繁，其失效的可能性就越大。图表来源：TC2000.com
samples.fit_text: 图3.19 在这张65分钟图上，我们看到AVWAP支撑被测试得越频繁，其失效的可能性就越大。图表来源：TC2000.com

samples.source_text: Charts 3.18 and 3.19 show stocks which repetitively tested the AVWAP from the prior significant low before the trend reversed lower. As the stocks experienced decent rallies, the rate of ascent in the AVWAPs slowed, resulting in even more frequent market testing of the AVWAPs. Prices eventually broke the AVWAP Support and began quick declines. Recognition of this tendency will allow you to exit winners with the bulk of your profits intact before reversals take place. For more aggressive traders, the drop away from AVWAP can also provide an early entry into a reversal.

typst.markdown_text: 图3.20和3.21中的两只股票处于下跌趋势，并持续在AVWAP（红色箭头指示处）遇到卖盘，这些AVWAP是以下跌前的重要高点为锚点计算的。随着股价进一步下跌，AVWAP被反复测试，最终消耗了卖盘压力，股价反转进入上升趋势。对AVWAP的反复测试消化了供应，因为持续买入的买家变得更加积极。

samples.fit_text: 图3.21 在日线时间框架上可见，阻力位被测试得越频繁，其失效的可能性越大。图表来源：TC2000.com

samples.source_text: The two stocks in Charts 3.20 and 3.21 were in downtrends and consistently found sellers at the AVWAPs (red arrows), which were anchored to the significant high prior to the decline. As the stocks declined further, the AVWAPs were tested repeatedly, which eventually overwhelmed sellers and the stock reversed to an upward trend. The repeated tests of AVWAP chewed through supply as the persistent buyers became more aggressive.

samples.source_text: You can use the concepts of AVWAP Support and Resistance on a stand-alone basis for a trade system, and you will probably achieve positive results. Of
samples.translated_text: 您可以将AVWAP支撑与阻力概念独立用于交易系统，并可能获得积极结果。
samples.fit_text: 您可以将AVWAP支撑与阻力概念独立用于交易系统，并可能获得积极结果。

samples.source_text: course, you also must manage risk effectively. Do not confuse simple with ineffective. All too many people want a complicated approach, but simplicity is the market’s greatest disguise. After all, stock prices move because of supply and demand. We don’t have to be obsessed with the “why” behind the moves. The strategies in the chapters ahead will assume you have read and understand the concepts discussed here. They are the building blocks of the layers we add on top of basic Support and Resistance.

samples.source_text: Risk Management
samples.fit_text: Risk Management

samples.source_text: It can be difficult to wait patiently for conditions to develop into your ideal time to make a trade and to resist the temptation to get involved early. Too many times, traders give into FOMO and buy before AVWAP provides evidence confirming who has regained control. Thus, they suffer needless losses. For longer than I care to admit, this was a problem for me. I would “be sure” a stock was ready to go, so I would get involved before the price action confirmed momentum. Once in a while, I would get lucky on this type of entry, but over the long-term, these trades ended up costing me money.
samples.translated_text: 耐心等待市场条件发展至理想的交易时机，并抵制过早介入的诱惑，可能颇具挑战。交易者常常因害怕错过（FOMO）而屈服，在AVWAP尚未提供确认哪一方重获控制权的证据之前便买入，从而遭受不必要的损失。在相当长的一段时间里，这对我而言也是一个问题。我曾“确信”某只股票即将启动，因此在价格走势确认动量之前便介入。偶尔，这类入场会带来幸运，但从长期来看，这些交易最终导致了资金亏损。

typst.markdown_text: 据说，你可以给一位优秀的交易者展示一个平庸的交易机会，他仍能获利；而给一位平庸的交易者展示一个绝佳的机会，他依然会亏损。努力成为多时间框架下价格与AVWAP关系的客观分析者，你将成为一个更出色的交易者。成功的交易者不断磨练技艺，并投入时间将基于经验的直觉叠加到他们的技术分析之上。

samples.source_text: he goal of this chapter is to identify the most valuable places to set the anchor. With that, we can measure supply and demand forces objectively and gain an edge in our buy and sell decisions. When we begin our VWAP measurement from any point other than the beginning of one day, it becomes an “anchored” VWAP (AVWAP) and it is cumulative from that point forward. Our interpretation of price action is relative to the direction and slope of the AVWAP.

typst.markdown_text: 特定价格水平的交易量越大，与该价格水平存在情感关联的参与者数量就越多。因此，高成交量事件附近的价格水平可作为设置AVWAP锚点的有效参考。异常高成交量（通常定义为正常成交量的1.5倍）的价格事件可能“冲击市场”并“重置”群体心理。无论是新闻事件还是技术信息，新信息引入市场可能引发或产生大量交易，使市场脱离盘整区间。这种现象称为突破。突破会引发紧迫感，因为当前估值迅速受到众多参与者的质疑和重新评估。

samples.source_text: Now it is time to address the subjective “art” of where to start the anchored VWAP to assist in buy and sell decisions. This helps us better manage risk and ultimately make more money.

samples.source_text: Anchor to High-Volume Events
samples.fit_text: Anchor to High-Volume Events

samples.source_text: The greater the volume of trading at a particular price level, the larger the number of participants with an emotional connection to that price level. Therefore, the price levels around high-volume events can be useful levels to set your AVWAP anchor. Unusually high-volume (loosely defined as 1.5x normal volume) price events can “shock the market” and “reset” the psychology of the crowd. The introduction of new information to a market, whether news events or technical information, can trigger or create a large volume in trading that shakes the market loose from a level of consolidation. This is called a breakout. The breakout creates a sense of urgency as the current valuation is quickly questioned and reevaluated by many participants.
samples.translated_text: 特定价格水平的交易量越大，与该价格水平存在情感关联的参与者数量就越多。因此，高成交量事件附近的价格水平可作为设置AVWAP锚点的有效参考。异常高成交量（通常定义为正常成交量的1.5倍）的价格事件可能“冲击市场”并“重置”群体心理。无论是新闻事件还是技术信息，新信息引入市场可能引发或产生大量交易，使市场脱离盘整区间。这种现象称为突破。突破会引发紧迫感，因为当前估值迅速受到众多参与者的质疑和重新评估。
samples.fit_text: 特定价格水平的交易量越大，与该价格水平存在情感关联的参与者数量就越多。因此，高成交量事件附近的价格水平可作为设置AVWAP锚点的有效参考。异常高成交量（通常定义为正常成交量的1.5倍）的价格事件可能“冲击市场”并“重置”群体心理。无论是新闻事件还是技术信息，新信息引入市场可能引发或产生大量交易，使市场脱离盘整区间。这种现象称为突破。突破会引发紧迫感，因为当前估值迅速受到众多参与者的质疑和重新评估。

samples.source_text: When we set the AVWAP from high-volume areas, it helps us to see clearly if buyers or sellers maintain control from the event. Unusually large volume at, or associated with, a breakout increases the likelihood of the price trending in the direction (higher or lower) of the price from the break. If the AVWAP from the event offers Support or Resistance, price will typically experience a tradeable continuation as price moves away from AVWAP. If, instead, the AVWAP is undercut and the price remains under AVWAP, the stock is likely to reverse lower. Tests of the AVWAP from a high-volume event will often become a battleground between buyers and sellers, and the emerging trend can set the tone for the stock for days, weeks and even months.
samples.translated_text: 当我们从高成交量区域设定AVWAP时，它有助于我们清晰地判断买方或卖方是否在事件后保持主导。突破点处或与之相关的异常大成交量，增加了价格沿突破方向（上涨或下跌）趋势延续的可能性。若事件AVWAP提供支撑或阻力，价格在远离AVWAP时通常会出现可交易的延续走势。反之，若AVWAP被跌破且价格持续位于其下方，则股价很可能反转下跌。对高成交量事件AVWAP的测试常成为多空双方的争夺焦点，而由此形成的趋势可为该股未来数日、数周乃至数月的走势奠定基调。

samples.source_text: Large price moves, accompanied by a surge in volume are excellent anchor points because they are where innumerable “price memories” live. The price action and development of AVWAP starting at these anchor points becomes an objective reference point where we can make an unbiased analysis of price action. From these points, the AVWAP helps us observe the real time changes in market values caused by the constant push and pull between bulls and bears.
samples.translated_text: 大幅价格波动伴随成交量激增是极佳的锚点，因为此处承载着无数的“价格记忆”。从这些锚点出发的AVWAP价格走势与发展，为我们提供了客观的参考基准，使我们能够对价格行为进行无偏分析。基于这些点，AVWAP有助于我们观察由多空双方持续博弈所引起的市场价值的实时变化。

samples.source_text: Chart 4.2 is a chart of a stock in a downtrend, where price accelerated to the downside with the first gap lower (3) and had the heaviest trading volume to that point. The AVWAP from (3) acted as Resistance through the months of December and into January. There was another sizable price gap lower in January which occurred on even heavier volume (4). This is a great example of why not to buy stocks that appear to be “down too much.”
samples.translated_text: 图4.2展示了一只处于下跌趋势的股票，其价格在首次向下跳空缺口(3)处加速下跌，并伴随截至该点最大的成交量。从(3)点计算的AVWAP在整个12月直至1月期间均充当了阻力位。1月出现了另一个幅度较大的向下价格跳空，且成交量更为巨大(4)。这是一个绝佳示例，说明了为何不应买入那些看似“跌幅过大”的股票。

typst.markdown_text: AVWAP的独特优势在于能够从我们选择的任意起点开始客观地测量供需。我们不希望将AVWAP锚定在图表上的随机点位。AVWAP是一种精妙的工具，使我们能够从任意起点综合衡量价格、成交量与时间。问题在于，如何设置锚点才能更清晰地揭示市场力量，并最终实现更高收益？

samples.source_text: The unique benefit of the AVWAP comes from the ability to start our objective supply and demand measurement from any point that we choose. We don’t want to anchor AVWAPs to random points on the chart. The AVWAP is an elegant tool that allows us to measure the combination of price, volume, and time together from any point. The question is, where to set the anchor to help us see the market forces more clearly and ultimately make more money?

samples.source_text: Earnings Reports
samples.fit_text: Earnings Reports

samples.source_text: The releases of individual corporate earnings reports are consistently useful anchor points because they are when investors and traders will refine their beliefs and trading strategies about the companies whose stock they are holding, or are considering as trade candidates. Corporate results get reported every three months. At the end of each calendar quarter, companies prepare their financial documents for the results of the previous three months. The earnings reports are then reported to the public. The “earnings season” refers to the quarterly period where companies report their results. The earnings season begins one or two weeks after the last month of each quarter (March, June, September, and December) and continues for approximately six to eight weeks. In chart 4.3 below you can see three different anchor points set at the times that the earnings reports were released.
samples.translated_text: 单个公司财报的发布始终是有用的锚点，因为此时投资者和交易者会调整他们对所持股票或潜在交易标的公司的信念和交易策略。公司业绩每三个月报告一次。在每个日历季度末，公司会编制反映前三个月业绩的财务文件，随后向公众发布财报。“财报季”是指公司集中报告业绩的季度时段。财报季始于每季度末月（三月、六月、九月和十二月）结束后的1-2周，持续约6至8周。在下面的图表4.3中，可以看到在财报发布时设置的三个不同锚点。

typst.markdown_text: 其他可作为良好锚点的新闻相关事件包括美联储会议与讲话、美国食品药品监督管理局公告、围绕年度股东大会的维权活动、并购新闻与传闻、专利授予、二次股票发行、战略联盟、诉讼与产品召回、法院判决、新合同签订以及供应商问题。

samples.source_text: Chart 4.3 shows a stock with three earnings reports. Each report is a good anchor point for the start of a new AVWAP. Earnings 1 shows how the AVWAP (green) from the release date held as Support on two separate occasions. Earnings 2 (red AVWAP) shows a negative response to earnings and then two subsequent tests of the AVWAP as shares were sold into the market and provided Resistance. Earnings 3 (blue AVWAP) shows an acceleration of selling (notice the steeper slope) as sellers reacted to the contents of the report. Notice how the next four tests of the AVWAP showed Resistance.
samples.translated_text: 图表4.3展示了一只包含三份财报的股票。每份财报都是新AVWAP的起始锚点。财报1显示，自发布日期起算的AVWAP（绿色）在两次独立行情中均起到支撑作用。财报2（红色AVWAP）显示财报发布后市场反应消极，随后股价两次测试该AVWAP，因卖盘涌入市场而构成阻力。财报3（蓝色AVWAP）显示，随着卖方对报告内容作出反应，抛售加速（注意其更陡峭的斜率）。请注意后续四次对AVWAP的测试均显示出阻力作用。

typst.markdown_text: 公司事件，如新产品发布、管理层变动、股息公告和股票回购，也可作为锚点。此列表并非详尽无遗，也无意令您望而却步。您无需追踪所有这些事件来确定锚点。随着您在日常应用中更熟练地使用AVWAP，最佳的锚点将变得显而易见。

samples.source_text: Other news-related events that can become good anchor points include Federal Reserve meetings and speeches, FDA announcements, activist events around annual meetings, merger and acquisition news and rumors, patent awards, secondary stock offerings, strategic alliances, lawsuits and product recalls, court decisions, new contracts, and supplier issues.
samples.translated_text: 其他可作为良好锚点的新闻相关事件包括美联储会议与讲话、美国食品药品监督管理局公告、围绕年度股东大会的维权活动、并购新闻与传闻、专利授予、二次股票发行、战略联盟、诉讼与产品召回、法院判决、新合同签订以及供应商问题。

samples.source_text: Corporate events such as new products, a change in management, dividend announcements, and share buybacks can also be anchor points. I do not mean this list to be all-inclusive, nor is it meant to intimidate you. You do not have to follow all these events to know where to anchor. The best anchor points will become obvious to you as you become more practiced in the everyday application of AVWAP.

samples.source_text: On the macroeconomic side of the news, a few of the catalysts for price change that might be a good anchor point include economic numbers such as GDP, PPI, CPI, and jobs reports. Other events would include elections, wars, and weather related events. Chart 4.4 illustrates an AVWAP from a Federal Reserve announcement. Notice how that level became supported several days after the event.

typst.markdown_text: 图表4.4展示了一只主要市场ETF，其锚定点设定在美联储政策宣布日当日下午2点（1）。在联邦公开市场委员会（FOMC）声明发布后，1点至2点之间出现的初始波动属于正常现象，随后买方重新掌控局面（2），趋势继续上行。五天后，FOMC声明发布时形成的平均成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP）受到测试（3）并显示出支撑作用。我们可以利用该AVWAP水平作为设置止损或识别新买入机会的位置，因为股价从AVWAP反弹上行。

samples.source_text: Federal Reserve Announcement
samples.fit_text: Federal Reserve Announcement

samples.source_text: Chart 4.4 is a major market ETF with the anchor set at 2 p.m. (1) on the day of the Federal Reserve policy announcement. After the initial volatility between 1 and 2, which is normal with an FOMC release, the buyers regained control (2) and the trend continued higher. Five days later, the AVWAP from the FOMC release was tested (3) and showed Support. We can use this AVWAP level as a place to set stops or to identify new buying opportunities as the stock bounces higher from the AVWAP.

samples.source_text: Price-Based Events
samples.translated_text: 价格走势本身往往就是“新闻”，但媒体通常无法准确报道。他们试图为每一次价格变动寻找“原因”。价格变动源于新信息进入市场并被消化时产生的供需失衡。在后续章节深入探讨之前，我们将简要介绍一些基于价格的锚点以供参考。

typst.markdown_text: 价格走势本身往往就是“新闻”，但媒体通常无法准确报道。他们试图为每一次价格变动寻找“原因”。价格变动源于新信息进入市场并被消化时产生的供需失衡。在后续章节深入探讨之前，我们将简要介绍一些基于价格的锚点以供参考。

samples.source_text: When Price IS the News
samples.fit_text: When Price IS the News

samples.source_text: Price action often is “the news,” but it doesn’t get reported by the media accurately. They try to assign a “reason” for every move. Price movement is based on supply and demand imbalances as new information comes to and is absorbed by the market. We will briefly look at some of the price-based anchors to consider before we explore them in greater detail in the chapters to follow.
samples.translated_text: 价格走势本身往往就是“新闻”，但媒体通常无法准确报道。他们试图为每一次价格变动寻找“原因”。价格变动源于新信息进入市场并被消化时产生的供需失衡。在后续章节深入探讨之前，我们将简要介绍一些基于价格的锚点以供参考。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 449.34999999999997pt, height: 51.700000000000045pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p85_item_2_2_md, max_size: 8.9pt, min_size: 8.16pt, max_leading: 0.58em, min_leading: 0.52em, fit_height: 51.7pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 图表4.5展示了从显著高点绘制的AVWAP如何经常在短期反弹中充当股票的阻力位。当股票从先前的上升趋势向下突破时，将锚点设置在最近的高点是设置锚点的最佳位置之一。该锚点可能要到11月中旬（1）才会被设定，因为此时11月初的高点被确认为一个重要高点。只要股价低于该高点绘制的AVWAP，就应将其视为“有罪直至证明清白”。请注意，在始于锚点的长期AVWAP下降期间，向上的反弹在接下来的三个月内（2）经历了多次测试并遭遇阻力。总体而言，在短期反弹中买入该股票的投资者正在亏损，而空头占据主导地位。

samples.source_text: The obvious and important highs and lows are easy to recognize after the fact, but that doesn’t mean they aren’t useful. Highs where prices reversed lower are some of the best anchor points for the AVWAP, as the following tests of that AVWAP often prove to reveal the Resistance level.
samples.translated_text: 显而易见的重要高点和低点事后易于识别，但这并不意味着它们没有用处。价格反转下跌的高点是AVWAP的最佳锚点之一，因为后续对该AVWAP的测试通常能有效揭示阻力位。
samples.fit_text: 显而易见的重要高点和低点事后易于识别，但这并不意味着它们没有用处。价格反转下跌的高点是AVWAP的最佳锚点之一，因为后续对该AVWAP的测试通常能有效揭示阻力位。

samples.source_text: Chart 4.5 shows how an AVWAP from a significant high will often act as Resistance for the stock on short-term bounces. When a stock breaks downward from a previous uptrend, an anchor to the most recent high is one of the best places to set an anchor. The anchor would probably not be set until somewhere in mid-November (1), as that is when it became clear the early November high was an important one. While the stock is below the AVWAP from that high, consider it “guilty until proven innocent.” Notice how the upward rallies during the long period of declining AVWAP that started at the anchor point found Resistance during multiple tests over the next three months (2). On balance, buyers of the stock on the short-term rallies are losing money and short sellers have control.

typst.markdown_text: 当股价形成重要低点时，我们会在事后数日识别该低点（1），并以此已确认的低点（0）作为锚点计算AVWAP。AVWAP的数值与方向可能在数日至数周内并不明确，直至其被测试并（在本例中）为股价提供支撑（2）后，才得以清晰。

samples.source_text: An important low is also easy to recognize after the fact. Even if it takes a couple of days for the low to become obvious, it makes sense to monitor to see if buyers defend the average price from that low. Chart 4.6, shows how an AVWAP from a significant low will often act as Support for the stock on pullbacks.
samples.translated_text: 一个重要低点事后也易于识别。即使该低点需要数日才变得明显，监测买家是否从该低点起捍卫平均价格仍有意义。图表4.6展示了源自重要低点的AVWAP如何在股价回调时经常起到支撑作用。
samples.fit_text: 一个重要低点事后也易于识别。即使该低点需要数日才变得明显，监测买家是否从该低点起捍卫平均价格仍有意义。图表4.6展示了源自重要低点的AVWAP如何在股价回调时经常起到支撑作用。

typst.markdown_text: 当股价高于上升的AVWAP时，可视为“无罪推定”。这意味着买方占据主导，卖方处于劣势。

samples.source_text: When the stock makes an important low, we will recognize it a few days after the fact (1) and then anchor our AVWAP from that now-identified low (0). The value and direction of the AVWAP might not be clear for days to weeks later until after it is tested and (in this case) provides Support (2) for the stock.

typst.markdown_text: 第三类用于锚定VWAP的基准点是基于时间的。投资组合经理的许多决策都基于时间增量（日度、周度、月度甚至年度）。

samples.source_text: Anchoring to Specific Time-Based Events
samples.translated_text: 人们常说机构在交易执行中仅使用单日VWAP；这显然是不正确的。正如我们在第一部分第三章所了解的，机构可能设定短至数小时的买入或卖出程序。机构也常使用AVWAP程序来买卖个股或买卖广泛的市场，并对非常大的订单执行长达一个日历周、月、季度甚至一年的大额订单。
samples.fit_text: 人们常说机构在交易执行中仅使用单日VWAP；这显然是不正确的。正如我们在第一部分第三章所了解的，机构可能设定短至数小时的买入或卖出程序。机构也常使用AVWAP程序来买卖个股或买卖广泛的市场，并对非常大的订单执行长达一个日历周、月、季度甚至一年的大额订单。

samples.source_text: A third category of places to which to anchor the VWAP is time based. Many decisions of portfolio managers are based on time increments (daily, week- to-date, month-to-date, or even year-to-date).
samples.translated_text: 第三类用于锚定VWAP的基准点是基于时间的。投资组合经理的许多决策都基于时间增量（日度、周度、月度甚至年度）。
samples.fit_text: 第三类用于锚定VWAP的基准点是基于时间的。投资组合经理的许多决策都基于时间增量（日度、周度、月度甚至年度）。

samples.source_text: People often say that institutions only use VWAP for one day in their trade executions; that is simply not true. As we learned in Part One, Chapter 3 institutions may set buy or sell programs for as short as a couple of hours. It is also common for institutions to use AVWAP programs to buy or sell individual stocks or to buy or sell broad-based markets and to execute one large order for periods such as a calendar week, month, quarter, or even year, for very large orders.
samples.translated_text: 人们常说机构在交易执行中仅使用单日VWAP；这显然是不正确的。正如我们在第一部分第三章所了解的，机构可能设定短至数小时的买入或卖出程序。机构也常使用AVWAP程序来买卖个股或买卖广泛的市场，并对非常大的订单执行长达一个日历周、月、季度甚至一年的大额订单。

samples.source_text: The bottom line is that institutions have strategies and timeframes that are as varied as the techniques used by individuals. Why wouldn’t they? Below we will look at some examples of broad market ETFs and individual stocks where there is clear evidence on the chart that buy and sell programs are time based.

samples.source_text: It is always best to set your stop-loss orders AFTER the market is open. Doing so will save you from throwing your stocks away on a manipulated lower open in search of stop orders. The first few minutes of the trade day can be more volatile as deep-pocketed participants “hunt for stop orders.” To avoid the initial volatility, I generally use the low of the first five minutes of a new day as the new stop if the previous stop was traded through on a gap lower.
samples.translated_text: 最好在股市开盘后设置止损单。这样做可以避免因市场开盘时被操纵压低以寻找止损单而抛售股票。交易日最初几分钟可能波动更大，因为资金雄厚的参与者会“寻找止损单”。为避免初始波动，如果前一日止损因跳空低开被触发，我通常将新交易日最初五分钟的低点作为新的止损位。
samples.fit_text: 最好在股市开盘后设置止损单。这样做可以避免因市场开盘时被操纵压低以寻找止损单而抛售股票。交易日最初几分钟可能波动更大，因为资金雄厚的参与者会“寻找止损单”。为避免初始波动，如果前一日止损因跳空低开被触发，我通常将新交易日最初五分钟的低点作为新的止损位。

typst.markdown_text: 部分机构会按季度做出执行大宗订单的决策，这些订单需要时间来完成。应始终关注价格接近并测试AVWAP时的斜率。AVWAP的角度越陡峭，越表明买方比卖方更为活跃，且AVWAP提供支撑的可能性越大。因此，股价在触及AVWAP后反弹走高的可能性也更大。

samples.source_text: Chart 4.9 shows the price action for one calendar quarter. Notice how the AVWAP from the first day of the quarter (1) acted as Support on pullbacks (2).

samples.fit_text: 图4.9展示了一个日历季度的价格走势。请注意，该季度首日（1）的平均成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP）在价格回调时起到了支撑作用（2）。

samples.source_text: Some institutions make quarterly decisions to execute massive orders that need time to be filled. Always be aware of the slope of the AVWAP as prices approach and test it. The steeper the angle of the AVWAP, the greater the evidence that buyers are more active than sellers and the more likely it is that the AVWAP provides Support. Thus, it is more likely that stock will bounce higher after an AVWAP touch.

typst.body_expr: block(width: 445.54999999999995pt, height: 25.38000000000011pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p89_item_2_1_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.67pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.54em, fit_height: 25.38pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 了解某些机构投资组合经理如何使用年初至今平均成交量加权平均价格（YTD AVWAP）至关重要。简而言之，这关系到他们的薪酬构成。基金经理的薪酬不仅取决于其基金的绝对业绩，还取决于其相对于基准指数或投资组合自身表现的相对业绩。

samples.source_text: Year-to-Date (YTD)
samples.translated_text: 例如，一位管理半导体基金的经理会希望确保其基金所持半导体股票的平均成本至少不低于年初至今的平均成交量加权平均价格（YTD AVWAP）。若基金经理以高于YTD AVWAP的价格买入股票，将相对损害基金表现，从而导致其报酬减少。反之，若他能以低于YTD AVWAP的价格购入投资组合中的股票，该基金的表现便可能优于其他基金，其个人收入也会相应增加。
samples.fit_text: 例如，一位管理半导体基金的经理会希望确保其基金所持半导体股票的平均成本至少不低于年初至今的平均成交量加权平均价格（YTD AVWAP）。若基金经理以高于YTD AVWAP的价格买入股票，将相对损害基金表现，从而导致其报酬减少。反之，若他能以低于YTD AVWAP的价格购入投资组合中的股票，该基金的表现便可能优于其他基金，其个人收入也会相应增加。
