typst.markdown_text: 人们常将下跌趋势中的任何短期股价反弹称为“轧空”。下跌趋势中的大多数快速上涨都包含空头回补头寸，这在一定程度上推动并导致了上涨。并非所有“轧空”都会导致价格持续上涨。下文我们将探讨两种不同类型的轧空：短期膝跳反射式轧空和长期结构性轧空。

samples.source_text: Knee-Jerk Short Squeeze
samples.fit_text: Knee-Jerk Short Squeeze

samples.source_text: This squeeze occurs in a longer-term downtrend with a large short position. When a stock in an established Stage 4 Decline is accompanied by a large short position, short sellers are in control of the trend. Their accumulated profits make short sellers less likely to panic and cover at the first signs of a bounce. When prices are in a Stage 4 Decline, short sellers can dig their heels in and stay short until their bearish opinion changes or, in the most severe down market, the stock becomes delisted in a bankruptcy. Still, some short sellers will panic and cover their position after even a small bounce.
samples.translated_text: 这种挤压发生在长期下跌趋势中，且伴随着大量空头头寸。当一只处于确立的第四阶段下跌阶段的股票伴有大量空头头寸时，空头便控制了趋势。其累积的利润使得空头不太可能在反弹初现时恐慌性平仓。当价格处于第四阶段下跌时，空头可以坚守阵地，继续持有空头头寸，直到其看跌观点改变，或在最严重的下跌市场中，股票因破产而退市。尽管如此，一些空头仍会在即使小幅反弹后恐慌性平仓。
samples.fit_text: 这种挤压发生在长期下跌趋势中，且伴随着大量空头头寸。当一只处于确立的第四阶段下跌阶段的股票伴有大量空头头寸时，空头便控制了趋势。其累积的利润使得空头不太可能在反弹初现时恐慌性平仓。当价格处于第四阶段下跌时，空头可以坚守阵地，继续持有空头头寸，直到其看跌观点改变，或在最严重的下跌市场中，股票因破产而退市。尽管如此，一些空头仍会在即使小幅反弹后恐慌性平仓。

typst.markdown_text: 这种挤压发生在长期下跌趋势中，且伴随着大量空头头寸。当一只处于确立的第四阶段下跌阶段的股票伴有大量空头头寸时，空头便控制了趋势。其累积的利润使得空头不太可能在反弹初现时恐慌性平仓。当价格处于第四阶段下跌时，空头可以坚守阵地，继续持有空头头寸，直到其看跌观点改变，或在最严重的下跌市场中，股票因破产而退市。尽管如此，一些空头仍会在即使小幅反弹后恐慌性平仓。

samples.source_text: Chart 10.7 shows a knee-jerk short squeeze. Notice how, after the large gap down in November, short sellers covered some of their shares, which you can see in the declining short interest. As the stock continued lower over the next few months, short sellers became emboldened and nearly doubled their short position (green arrow). While many participants try to “buy the dip,” as a long position, short sellers know there is no such thing as “down too much.” Therefore, they continue to hold their short position.
samples.translated_text: 图10.7展示了一次典型的空头挤压现象。值得注意的是，在11月大幅跳空下跌后，空头回补了部分仓位，这可以从下降的空头头寸中看出。随后几个月，随着股价持续走低，空头信心增强，其空头头寸几乎翻倍（绿色箭头所示）。尽管许多市场参与者试图“逢低买入”作为多头头寸，但空头深知不存在“跌过头”的情况。因此，他们选择继续持有空头头寸。

samples.source_text: Stage 4 Decline stocks can experience quick and large rallies, but those short-term bursts typically fail as longer-term selling pressure is too strong to overcome.
samples.translated_text: 第四阶段下跌的股票可能出现快速大幅反弹，但这些短期上涨通常难以持续，因为长期卖压过强而无法克服。
samples.fit_text: 第四阶段下跌的股票可能出现快速大幅反弹，但这些短期上涨通常难以持续，因为长期卖压过强而无法克服。

typst.markdown_text: 第四阶段下跌的股票可能出现快速大幅反弹，但这些短期上涨通常难以持续，因为长期卖压过强而无法克服。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 447.45000000000005pt, height: 35.72000000000003pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p152_item_10_7_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.5pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.53em, fit_height: 35.72pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 处于下跌趋势的股票会吸引“动量卖空者”，他们在短期反弹后增加空头头寸。投资者可能因短期反弹能带来快速交易收益而试图买入，但当长期趋势向下时，做多交易风险极高。这些短期反弹最适合由专注于最短时间框架、风险承受能力最强的交易者参与。最佳策略是顺应主要趋势，而非与之对抗。

samples.source_text: Psychology: Trading against the trend
samples.fit_text: Psychology: Trading against the trend

samples.source_text: Stocks in a downtrend will attract “momentum short sellers” who pile on extra shares of short stock after the short-term rallies. You may be tempted to buy the short-term rallies because they can result in quick trading gains. However, when the longer timeframe is lower, long purchases are very risky trades. These short-term bounces are best left to the most risk-tolerant traders who specialize in the shortest timeframes. The best course of action is to stay with the primary trend rather than fight them.
samples.translated_text: 处于下跌趋势的股票会吸引“动量卖空者”，他们在短期反弹后增加空头头寸。投资者可能因短期反弹能带来快速交易收益而试图买入，但当长期趋势向下时，做多交易风险极高。这些短期反弹最适合由专注于最短时间框架、风险承受能力最强的交易者参与。最佳策略是顺应主要趋势，而非与之对抗。

typst.markdown_text: 第二种短期轧空现象，我称之为“结构性轧空”。它发生在处于确立的第二阶段上升趋势的股票中，存在大量“价外”空头头寸时。

samples.source_text: Structural Short Squeeze

samples.source_text: A second short squeeze is what I call a “structural short squeeze.” It occurs when a stock in an established Stage 2 Uptrend has a large short position that is “out of the money.”
samples.translated_text: 第二种短期轧空现象，我称之为“结构性轧空”。它发生在处于确立的第二阶段上升趋势的股票中，存在大量“价外”空头头寸时。

typst.markdown_text: 一旦平均空头头寸出现亏损，该股票便易受轧空影响，价格可能随之上涨。若多数空头头寸建立于较低价位，股价上涨导致的亏损扩大将促使空头重新评估其持仓并买入股票。

samples.source_text: When we combine the price data along with the short interest trend of the short sellers, we can determine the approximate price level where the short positions were initiated. We use this information to determine the “pain point” where the short sellers (in the aggregate) are in a losing position.

typst.markdown_text: 当空头回补时，额外的需求会推动市场，从而给平均空头带来更大压力。这种轧空行情（图表10.8）可能导致价格大幅上涨。其基础在于大量空头对股价下跌的判断错误。

samples.source_text: Once the average short seller is losing money, the stock is vulnerable to a squeeze, and a subsequent increase in price. If most short positions were initiated at lower levels, the growing losses in a rising stock motivate short sellers to reconsider their positions and purchase the stock.

samples.source_text: When they cover their shorts, additional demand fuels the market, which adds further pressure to the average short seller. This type of squeeze setup (Chart 10.8) can lead to substantial moves higher. This is based on the large number of short sellers who are wrong about the stocks price decline.

The stock still had 6 million shares of exposure with the stock at all-time highs. I wouldn’t want that position.

typst.markdown_text: 图10.9中的股票走势显示了空头持续坚守的时段。重点关注(B)点，此处买家明显掌控了股价。从(B)点股价突破基于高点(A)锚定的AVWAP时起，我们即可确知买家已占据主导。那些在(B)点或其附近将空头头寸从390万股增至1230万股的卖空者陷入困境，因为股价已高于该AVWAP。

samples.source_text: The interesting part is that the short position was at the highest level, 13.7 million shares, while the stock broke to an all-time high (after C). The AVWAP (green) from the last higher-low (C) was rising under the stock as it traded in a tight range. With buyers clearly in control and short sellers in a large losing position, this is the type of stock you want to identify as a structural short squeeze candidate.
samples.translated_text: 有趣的是，空头头寸处于最高水平，达1370万股，而股价却创下历史新高（C点之后）。自上一个更高的低点（C点）起，AVWAP（绿色线）在股价于窄幅区间内交易时持续上升。鉴于买方明显占据主导，且空头处于大幅亏损状态，这类股票正是需要识别出的结构性轧空候选标的。

samples.source_text: Here are some general points to help you determine the stocks that may become structural squeeze candidates:
samples.translated_text: 以下是一些有助于识别可能成为结构性轧空候选股的通用要点：
samples.fit_text: 以下是一些有助于识别可能成为结构性轧空候选股的通用要点：

typst.body_expr: block(width: 317.29999999999995pt, height: 9.399999999999977pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p154_item_2_2_md, max_size: 8.9pt, min_size: 8.46pt, max_leading: 0.26em, min_leading: 0.2em, fit_height: 9.399999999999977pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 以下是一些有助于识别可能成为结构性轧空候选股的通用要点：

samples.source_text: 1. Uptrend on the daily timeframe. At a minimum, the stock must be above the rising 50-day moving average. Stocks at or near all-time highs are best because there is no real motivated source of supply from holders looking to get out at break even, and all of the longs are in a winning position. If the stock is in a downtrend, it is not a good squeeze candidate, as short sellers are in control and have no reason for aggressive purchases.
samples.fit_text: 1. Uptrend on the daily timeframe. At a minimum, the stock must be above the rising 50-day moving average. Stocks at or near all-time highs are best because there is no real motivated source of supply from holders looking to get out at break even, and all of the longs are in a winning position. If the stock is in a downtrend, it is not a good squeeze candidate, as short sellers are in control and have no reason for aggressive purchases.

samples.source_text: 2. Absence of any hedging vehicles. Some common ways for short sellers to hedge bearish bets are with options; a different class of common stock, warrants, convertibles, preferred stock, or any other hedge products. If there is an inability to hedge against a short exposure, it will leave the short seller in a more vulnerable position.
samples.fit_text: 2. Absence of any hedging vehicles. Some common ways for short sellers to hedge bearish bets are with options; a different class of common stock, warrants, convertibles, preferred stock, or any other hedge products. If there is an inability to hedge against a short exposure, it will leave the short seller in a more vulnerable position.

samples.source_text: 3. Short interest should be high relative to average volume. The higher the SIR, the greater the difficulty short sellers will have in purchasing their shares.

typst.markdown_text: 具有双重需求来源（多头和空头）且供应紧张（尤其是当股价处于或接近历史高点时）的股票，可能带来极佳的上行交易机会。

typst.markdown_text: 卖空者通常是精明的投机者。然而，与任何市场参与者群体一样，他们并非总是正确。当空头对股票走势判断错误时，其平仓行为可能为能够识别轧空局面形成的交易者带来一些绝佳的趋势机会。

samples.source_text: Short sellers are usually savvy speculators. However, like any group of market participants, they aren’t always right. When shorts are wrong about the direction of a stock, their move to cover can lead to some excellent trend opportunities for traders who can recognize the squeeze situation develop.

samples.fit_text: KEY TAKEAWAYS

samples.source_text: • Short squeezes are motivated by short sellers who fear unlimited losses that can arise from being in a losing position.

typst.markdown_text: 首次公开募股为研究股票自上市以来的价格走势提供了独特机会。由于存在明确的起始点作为锚定AVWAP的基础，IPO能够从所有参与者共享的固定起点出发，最纯粹地展现供需关系。

samples.source_text: Initial Public Offerings are a unique opportunity to study price action from the inception of a stock as a publicly traded company. Because there is a defined starting point to anchor an AVWAP from, an IPO offers the purest look at supply and demand from a fixed starting point shared by all participants.
samples.translated_text: 首次公开募股为研究股票自上市以来的价格走势提供了独特机会。由于存在明确的起始点作为锚定AVWAP的基础，IPO能够从所有参与者共享的固定起点出发，最纯粹地展现供需关系。
samples.fit_text: 首次公开募股为研究股票自上市以来的价格走势提供了独特机会。由于存在明确的起始点作为锚定AVWAP的基础，IPO能够从所有参与者共享的固定起点出发，最纯粹地展现供需关系。

samples.source_text: Why Anchor to the IPO?
samples.translated_text: 所有首次公开募股（IPO）的股票在作为上市公司开始交易时，均以单笔交易启动。该首笔交易即为“开盘成交”，所有买单和卖单在此处完成匹配。在此首笔成交之后，股票便在公开市场中自由交易。开盘成交通常是1分钟时间框架内成交量最大的交易。开盘成交的巨大成交量是所有买单和卖单的汇总。开盘成交之后，该股票便如同其他股票一样，遵循供求规律进行交易。

typst.markdown_text: 所有首次公开募股（IPO）的股票在作为上市公司开始交易时，均以单笔交易启动。该首笔交易即为“开盘成交”，所有买单和卖单在此处完成匹配。在此首笔成交之后，股票便在公开市场中自由交易。开盘成交通常是1分钟时间框架内成交量最大的交易。开盘成交的巨大成交量是所有买单和卖单的汇总。开盘成交之后，该股票便如同其他股票一样，遵循供求规律进行交易。

samples.source_text: All IPOs start their life as a public company with a single trade. That first trade is the “opening print” where all of the buy and sell orders are matched. After that first print, the stock trades freely in the open market. The opening print is usually the largest volume of stock on a 1-minute timeframe. The large volume of the opening print is the aggregate of all the buy and sell orders. After the opening print, the stock trades like any other stock, subject to the laws of supply and demand.

samples.source_text: Chart 11.1 This is a blog post I wrote in 2007 about potential support at the AVWAP from the IPO. Note, this company no longer exists as a publicly traded stock. Chart: RealTick.com
samples.translated_text: 以下示例采用多个时间框架，以强调从新股首次交易到首次公开募股后数年期间，IPO AVWAP的重要性。
samples.fit_text: 以下示例采用多个时间框架，以强调从新股首次交易到首次公开募股后数年期间，IPO AVWAP的重要性。

typst.markdown_text: 以下示例采用多个时间框架，以强调从新股首次交易到首次公开募股后数年期间，IPO AVWAP的重要性。

samples.source_text: For the first couple of weeks, daily chart data is of little value without an AVWAP. If you trade from a chart with daily data, wait for more data to build to make sense of the chart.
samples.translated_text: 在最初几周，若无平均成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP），日线图数据价值有限。若基于日线图进行交易，应等待更多数据积累以使图表分析具有意义。
samples.fit_text: 在最初几周，若无平均成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP），日线图数据价值有限。若基于日线图进行交易，应等待更多数据积累以使图表分析具有意义。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 440.79999999999995pt, height: 16.920000000000073pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p157_item_8_5_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.54pt, max_leading: 0.57em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 16.92pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 图11.2为1分钟时间框架，起始于该股票作为上市公司首日交易开始时刻。图中显示两条(A)VWAP。第一条VWAP（紫色）锚定于交易的第一分钟。第二条AVWAP（蓝色）锚定于交易的第二分钟。

samples.source_text: If you want to use the 50-DMA, you need to wait until the fiftieth day the stock trades. If you want to trade the first day the stock comes to life, look at intraday charts and use VWAP/AVWAP.
samples.translated_text: 若欲使用50日移动平均线，需待股票交易满五十日。若希望在股票上市首日进行交易，则应观察日内图表并采用成交量加权平均价格/平均成交量加权平均价格。
samples.fit_text: 若欲使用50日移动平均线，需待股票交易满五十日。若希望在股票上市首日进行交易，则应观察日内图表并采用成交量加权平均价格/平均成交量加权平均价格。

typst.markdown_text: 该股票属于“热门首次公开募股”，意味着上市伊始便需求旺盛。这一点从股价迅速飙升并触发交易所暂停交易（1）即可看出，其快速上涨幅度达到$10 \\%$.

samples.source_text: Start Charting the Shortest Timeframes
samples.fit_text: Start Charting the Shortest Timeframes

samples.source_text: I start my analysis of IPOs on the first day with a 1-minute chart. The 1-minute chart allows me to see price action develop in real time. As the stock builds more price and volume data history, my analysis will move out from a 1-minute timeframe to 5-minutes, 15, 30, 65 timeframes and longer. Of course, it is always by using a multiple timeframe analysis where we find the most value.
samples.translated_text: 我使用1分钟图表开始分析IPO首日行情。1分钟图表使我能够实时观察价格走势的发展。随着股票积累更多价格和成交量数据历史，我的分析将从1分钟时间框架逐步扩展到5分钟、15分钟、30分钟、65分钟及更长的时间框架。当然，通过多时间框架分析才能获得最大价值。
samples.fit_text: 我使用1分钟图表开始分析IPO首日行情。1分钟图表使我能够实时观察价格走势的发展。随着股票积累更多价格和成交量数据历史，我的分析将从1分钟时间框架逐步扩展到5分钟、15分钟、30分钟、65分钟及更长的时间框架。当然，通过多时间框架分析才能获得最大价值。

samples.source_text: The first trades of an IPO are similar to gap trades. There is a sudden supply/demand shift, which becomes an important anchor point for the stock. There is no subjectivity about where to set the anchor in an IPO. We set it from the first trade.
samples.translated_text: 首次公开募股（IPO）的首笔交易类似于跳空交易。此时供需关系发生突变，成为该股票的重要锚定点。对于IPO而言，锚定点的设定不存在主观性。我们将其设定为首笔交易的价格。
samples.fit_text: 首次公开募股（IPO）的首笔交易类似于跳空交易。此时供需关系发生突变，成为该股票的重要锚定点。对于IPO而言，锚定点的设定不存在主观性。我们将其设定为首笔交易的价格。

samples.source_text: The following examples use several timeframes to emphasize how important the IPO AVWAP is from the first trade of a new stock to several years after the IPO.
samples.translated_text: 以下示例采用多个时间框架，以强调从新股首次交易到首次公开募股后数年期间，IPO AVWAP的重要性。
samples.fit_text: 以下示例采用多个时间框架，以强调从新股首次交易到首次公开募股后数年期间，IPO AVWAP的重要性。

samples.source_text: Chart 11.2 is a 1-minute timeframe, it begins the moment the stock traded through the end of its first day as a public company. There are two (A)VWAPs on this chart. The first VWAP (purple) is anchored to the first minute of trading. The second AVWAP (blue) is anchored to the second minute of trading.
samples.translated_text: 图11.2为1分钟时间框架，起始于该股票作为上市公司首日交易开始时刻。图中显示两条(A)VWAP。第一条VWAP（紫色）锚定于交易的第一分钟。第二条AVWAP（蓝色）锚定于交易的第二分钟。
samples.fit_text: 图11.2为1分钟时间框架，起始于该股票作为上市公司首日交易开始时刻。图中显示两条(A)VWAP。第一条VWAP（紫色）锚定于交易的第一分钟。第二条AVWAP（蓝色）锚定于交易的第二分钟。

samples.source_text: The stock was a “hot IPO,” which means there was a lot of demand right out of the gate. We know that because the stock immediately rallied so strongly that it triggered an exchange halt (1) after a quick rise of 1 0 \% .
samples.translated_text: 该股票属于“热门首次公开募股”，意味着上市伊始便需求旺盛。这一点从股价迅速飙升并触发交易所暂停交易（1）即可看出，其快速上涨幅度达到1 0 \%.
samples.fit_text: 该股票属于“热门首次公开募股”，意味着上市伊始便需求旺盛。这一点从股价迅速飙升并触发交易所暂停交易（1）即可看出，其快速上涨幅度达到1 0 \%.

samples.source_text: Chart 11.2 One-minute chart. When a stock first begins trading as an IPO it is helpful to anchor to the first and second minutes. Chart: TC2000.com
samples.translated_text: 图11.2 一分钟图。当一只股票首次作为IPO开始交易时，锚定其第一分钟和第二分钟的交易情况是有帮助的。图表来源：TC2000.com
samples.fit_text: 图11.2 一分钟图。当一只股票首次作为IPO开始交易时，锚定其第一分钟和第二分钟的交易情况是有帮助的。图表来源：TC2000.com

typst.markdown_text: 若等待价格回调至开盘AVWAP，则会错失行情，因其从未触及第一分钟的AVWAP（紫色）。反之，若知晓应从第二分钟开始锚定——该分钟未包含开盘成交的全部成交量——则可在股价两次测试并随后脱离第二分钟AVWAP（蓝色）时获得买入机会（位置2和3）。这些买入操作的止损位应设置在蓝色箭头所示的低点下方。

samples.source_text: Stocks are halted by the NASDAQ for a few minutes when a move of 1 0 \% or more occurs in a five-minute period. The AVWAP from the first minute trailed the stock significantly through the end of the day.
samples.translated_text: 当股价在五分钟内出现1 0 \%或以上的波动时，纳斯达克会暂停交易数分钟。从第一分钟开始的成交量加权平均价在当天剩余时间内显著落后于股价。
samples.fit_text: 当股价在五分钟内出现1 0 \%或以上的波动时，纳斯达克会暂停交易数分钟。从第一分钟开始的成交量加权平均价在当天剩余时间内显著落后于股价。

samples.source_text: If you waited for a pullback to the AVWAP from the open, you would have missed the move since it never touched the first minute AVWAP (purple). If instead, you knew to anchor from the second minute that does not include all of the volume from the open print, you would have had the opportunity to purchase the stock on two occasions (2 and 3) as the stock tested and then rallied away from the second minute AVWAP (blue). Stops for these buys would be placed below the low at the blue arrows.

typst.markdown_text: 你会惊讶地发现，即使在股票上市多年后，买卖双方仍经常聚集在IPO AVWAP附近。机构通常会在价格回调至IPO AVWAP时买入，因此我们也需要准备在该区域采取行动。IPO首日之后，由于除数增加且首分钟交易的影响减弱，便无需再以第二分钟作为锚定点。

samples.source_text: This is the same stock as Chart 11.2, two days after the IPO. This chart is constructed with 5-minute data. The circled area shows how the AVWAP from the IPO found buyers on the day three as the stock opened on a gap down.
samples.translated_text: 该股票与图表11.2所示相同，为首次公开募股（IPO）后两日。此图表基于5分钟数据构建。圆圈区域显示，在第三天股票开盘跳空下跌时，IPO当日的成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP）吸引了买家。
samples.fit_text: 该股票与图表11.2所示相同，为首次公开募股（IPO）后两日。此图表基于5分钟数据构建。圆圈区域显示，在第三天股票开盘跳空下跌时，IPO当日的成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP）吸引了买家。

samples.source_text: It will surprise you how often buyers or sellers congregate near the IPO AVWAP, even years after the stocks come public. Institutions will often buy on pullbacks to the IPO AVWAP, so we want to be ready to act in that area as well. After day one of an IPO, it is no longer necessary to anchor from the second minute as the divisor increases and the impact of the first-minute trades lessens.

typst.markdown_text: 图11.4 VWAP（紫色线）在此10分钟时间框架内为IPO的价格走势提供了结构。图表来源：TC2000.com。

samples.source_text: Chart 11.4 shows how the addition of AVWAP from the IPO adds structure to our analysis. The left chart shows the stock with price action only. On the right, we see the AVWAP from the IPO along with price action for the same stock. Notice how the initial rally failed to hold (1). As it made a lower-low under the AVWAP, it led to a quick selloff below (1).
samples.translated_text: 图11.4展示了首次公开募股（IPO）引入的AVWAP如何为我们的分析增添结构。左图仅显示股票的价格走势。右图则展示了同一只股票的IPO AVWAP及其价格走势。请注意，最初的上涨未能持续（1）。当价格在AVWAP下方创出新低时，随即引发了快速下跌至（1）下方。
samples.fit_text: 图11.4展示了首次公开募股（IPO）引入的AVWAP如何为我们的分析增添结构。左图仅显示股票的价格走势。右图则展示了同一只股票的IPO AVWAP及其价格走势。请注意，最初的上涨未能持续（1）。当价格在AVWAP下方创出新低时，随即引发了快速下跌至（1）下方。

typst.markdown_text: 两天后，股价反弹至首次公开募股平均成交量加权平均价格（2）。在突破该区域的供应压力后，股价创出高于平均成交量加权平均价格的新高（3）。这将成为一次低风险入场机会，初始止损位可设在最近且相关的高点低点下方（4）。

samples.fit_text: 图11.4 VWAP（紫色线）在此10分钟时间框架内为IPO的价格走势提供了结构。图表来源：TC2000.com。

samples.source_text: Two days later, the stock rallied to the IPO AVWAP (2). After it moved through the supply in that area it made a higher-high above the AVWAP (3). This would have been a low-risk entry with an initial stop below the most recent and relevant higher-low (4).
samples.translated_text: 两天后，股价反弹至首次公开募股平均成交量加权平均价格（2）。在突破该区域的供应压力后，股价创出高于平均成交量加权平均价格的新高（3）。这将成为一次低风险入场机会，初始止损位可设在最近且相关的高点低点下方（4）。
samples.fit_text: 两天后，股价反弹至首次公开募股平均成交量加权平均价格（2）。在突破该区域的供应压力后，股价创出高于平均成交量加权平均价格的新高（3）。这将成为一次低风险入场机会，初始止损位可设在最近且相关的高点低点下方（4）。

samples.source_text: As the stock rallied past $13/share, it would be time to raise the stop to under (5). At (6) the stop (5) would have been undercut and stopped out. Of course, someone with a longer-term goal might not have their stop so tight and more risk averse traders may have had an even tighter stop.
samples.translated_text: 随着股价上涨至每股13美元以上，此时应将止损位上移至(5)下方。在(6)处，止损位(5)已被跌破并触发止损。当然，具有长期目标的投资者可能不会设置如此严格的止损，而风险规避型交易者则可能设置更紧的止损。
samples.fit_text: 随着股价上涨至每股13美元以上，此时应将止损位上移至(5)下方。在(6)处，止损位(5)已被跌破并触发止损。当然，具有长期目标的投资者可能不会设置如此严格的止损，而风险规避型交易者则可能设置更紧的止损。

samples.source_text: Here is an example of the first three days of trades for an IPO with 10-minute data. We set the anchor to the first candle (1) on the chart. Notice how the buyers scooped up shares of the stock on the weak open on the second day (2) and the day after that (3). With the benefit of hindsight, we can see a purchase (2 or 3) would have worked, but the better buys were (A, B). These were better buy levels because the stock had upward momentum and a stop could go below the pullback low. A look at the one-minute chart on the open of day two and day three would have been the best opportunity to purchase this stock with a “second-day” entry technique.
samples.translated_text: 以下为使用10分钟数据的新股上市前三日交易示例。我们将锚点设为图表上的第一根蜡烛（1）。注意买方如何在第二日（2）及第三日（3）开盘弱势时吸纳股票。事后可见，在（2或3）位置买入本可获利，但更佳的买入点实为（A、B）。这些之所以是更优的买入价位，是因为股票具备上涨动能，且止损位可设于回调低点下方。若在第二日和第三日开盘时观察一分钟图表，将是运用“次日”入场技术买入该股票的最佳时机。
samples.fit_text: 以下为使用10分钟数据的新股上市前三日交易示例。我们将锚点设为图表上的第一根蜡烛（1）。注意买方如何在第二日（2）及第三日（3）开盘弱势时吸纳股票。事后可见，在（2或3）位置买入本可获利，但更佳的买入点实为（A、B）。这些之所以是更优的买入价位，是因为股票具备上涨动能，且止损位可设于回调低点下方。若在第二日和第三日开盘时观察一分钟图表，将是运用“次日”入场技术买入该股票的最佳时机。

samples.source_text: The IPO AVWAP anchored (1) in Chart 11.6 was briefly undercut (2) before the stock broke to a new high (3). A purchase (3) would make sense with a stop below the low (2). There are several higher-lows (discussed below), so the stop should have been raised to protect the gains over the following months.

samples.source_text: Regardless of where you may choose to set a stop, it would be unforgivable to allow the stock to come all the way down to breakeven (4) or worse, to allow it to turn into a loss.

samples.source_text: The stock broke and closed below the IPO AVWAP (10) five months after the stock came public. The stock then held below the AVWAP (11). A short sale would make sense here since the stock reached a lower-low below AVWAP. A stop on this short sale would have gone above the most recent and relevant lower-high (12).
samples.translated_text: 该股票在上市五个月后跌破并收于首次公开募股平均成交量加权平均价格（10）之下。随后，该股票维持在平均成交量加权平均价格（11）下方。由于股价在平均成交量加权平均价格下方创出新低，此时进行卖空操作是合理的。该卖空交易的止损点应设置在最近且相关的较低高点（12）之上。

samples.source_text: It broke below the IPO AVWAP (2) but recovered (3). At (3) it looked like buyers had control again, but it didn’t last long, and the stock broke below (2) and made a lower-low (4). This is where we have confirmation that sellers established their dominance (4).

typst.markdown_text: 图11.8展示了IPO AVWAP如何从阻力位翻转为支撑位，随后又转回阻力位。图11.8由周线蜡烛图构成。部分分析者倾向于观察更长的时间周期，因其数据点较少，可“减少噪音”。由于一周包含五个交易日，相同的数据由更少的蜡烛图表示。与所有技术分析一样，IPO AVWAP的概念在所有时间周期内均保持一致。

samples.source_text: Over the next 11 months, the stock rallied up to the IPO AVWAP (5, 6, 7, and 8) and sellers maintained control as the stock failed to close back above the IPO AVWAP. As downward pressure accelerated away from the AVWAP, there were excellent short sale opportunities (A, B, C) with protective stops above the preceding highs (5, 6, and 7).
samples.translated_text: 在接下来的11个月里，股价反弹至首次公开募股平均加权价格（5、6、7和8）附近，但由于未能收于该价格之上，卖方持续占据主导。随着股价加速远离平均加权价格，出现了绝佳的做空机会（A、B、C），止损位可设于前期高点（5、6和7）之上。

samples.source_text: Chart 11.8 shows how the IPO AVWAP flipped from Resistance to Support and then back to Resistance. Chart 11.8 is constructed of weekly candles. Some people prefer to look at longer timeframes with fewer data points for “less noise.” With one week being five days, there are fewer candles to represent the same data. As with all technical analysis, the IPO AVWAP concepts are the same on all timeframes.

samples.source_text: When the stock moved higher from that level, the stop should be raised to just under (4). It would have been stopped out with a good gain (5). The next couple of weeks the stock made a higher-low until (6), when it started to trend lower, below the low of the prior low. The break (6) occurred below the IPO AVWAP, so a short sale (7) made sense with a protective stop above the high (6).
samples.translated_text: 当股价从该水平上涨时，止损位应提高至略低于(4)的位置。这将使交易以良好收益(5)平仓。随后几周，股价形成更高的低点，直至(6)时开始下跌趋势，跌破前一个低点的低点。跌破(6)发生在IPO AVWAP下方，因此在(6)高点上方设置保护性止损的卖空操作(7)是合理的。

typst.markdown_text: 该多头头寸的止损位将设置在(A)点，随后在(3)点低点下方（即股价从IPO AVWAP反弹的位置）上移。当股价在(4)点创出新低时，多头头寸将被止损离场。

samples.source_text: The IPO AVWAP can be useful four years after the stock comes public. Chart 11.9 is constructed of weekly candles, showing nearly three years of trades for the stock. We set our anchor to the first week of the IPO (1). After five weeks below the IPO AVWAP, the stock was a good buy (2) when it cleared the IPO AVWAP and cleared the prior week high.
samples.translated_text: 首次公开发行(IPO)的平均成交量加权平均价格(IPO AVWAP)在股票上市四年后仍具参考价值。图11.9采用周K线构建，展示了该股票近三年的交易情况。我们将锚点设定在IPO首周(1)。在IPO AVWAP下方运行五周后，当股价突破IPO AVWAP并超越前一周高点时(2)，该股票呈现良好的买入时机。
samples.fit_text: 首次公开发行(IPO)的平均成交量加权平均价格(IPO AVWAP)在股票上市四年后仍具参考价值。图11.9采用周K线构建，展示了该股票近三年的交易情况。我们将锚点设定在IPO首周(1)。在IPO AVWAP下方运行五周后，当股价突破IPO AVWAP并超越前一周高点时(2)，该股票呈现良好的买入时机。

typst.markdown_text: 几周后，股价跌破前一周低点（5）。这将是建立空头头寸的良机，止损点可设在（B）上方。若交易者逐步将止损位下调至略低于前高点的位置，空头头寸将在（6）处被止损平仓。此时，股价突破IPO AVWAP并站上前一周高点。

samples.source_text: The stop on this long would be placed (A) and then raised up under the low of (3) where the stock bounced from the IPO AVWAP. At (4) the long position would be stopped out as the stock made a lower low.

typst.markdown_text: 直到六个月后，该股票才测试了首次公开募股的平均成交量加权平均价格（4）。对于尚未持有该股票的投资者而言，在股价从该平均成交量加权平均价格反弹时买入（5）是合理的。此次买入以及任何现有多头头寸的止损位应设在低点（4）下方。该股票已成为有史以来最出色的股票之一。

typst.markdown_text: • 若要在股票首次公开发行日进行交易，需采用成交量加权平均价格（VWAP）。

typst.markdown_text: • 在首次公开募股（IPO）首日，于交易第二分钟设定一个锚定成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP），以便进行客观分析，避免开盘第一分钟成交价带来的噪音干扰。

samples.source_text: There is no wrong way to use what I’ve laid out for you. I expect some readers will use the concepts and information to complement and fine-tune an approach that already works for them.

samples.source_text: Others will try to replicate the strategies exactly as they have been laid out. If so, I ask that you also follow my advice on managing risk aggressively. If you do both of these together, you have a good chance for success.

typst.markdown_text: 其他人将尝试完全按照已制定的策略进行复制。若如此，我建议您也遵循我关于积极管理风险的建议。若两者兼顾，您将有较大的成功机会。

samples.source_text: Any approach that works, and limits losses when it doesn’t work, creates the type of mindset that allows you to accept small losses. When armed with a winning strategy, you have the confidence to have an attitude of, “I loaned $ back to the market and I will get it back with interest.” A short-term loss can be part of the process. Do not get stuck in hindsight analysis. Instead, learn to use past data to anticipate what may be next for a market. This is the best way to create a logical plan that includes the possibility of being wrong and taking a small loss before causing major damage to your account.
samples.translated_text: 任何行之有效且能在失效时限制损失的方法，都能培养一种允许你接受小额亏损的心态。当拥有制胜策略时，你便有信心秉持这样的态度：“我将资金借给了市场，并会连本带利收回。”短期亏损可以是过程的一部分。不要陷入后见之明的分析。相反，学会利用过去的数据来预判市场下一步的可能动向。这是制定逻辑计划的最佳方式，该计划包含了犯错的可能性，以及在账户遭受重大损害之前承担小额损失的可能性。
samples.fit_text: 任何行之有效且能在失效时限制损失的方法，都能培养一种允许你接受小额亏损的心态。当拥有制胜策略时，你便有信心秉持这样的态度：“我将资金借给了市场，并会连本带利收回。”短期亏损可以是过程的一部分。不要陷入后见之明的分析。相反，学会利用过去的数据来预判市场下一步的可能动向。这是制定逻辑计划的最佳方式，该计划包含了犯错的可能性，以及在账户遭受重大损害之前承担小额损失的可能性。

samples.source_text: APPENDIX A:

samples.source_text: THE FOUR STAGES OF MARKET STRUCTURE
samples.fit_text: THE FOUR STAGES OF MARKET STRUCTURE

samples.source_text: All trade strategies should be based on a solid comprehension of market structure. There is a definite cyclical flow of money in and out of assets and we can observe this rhythm on charts of all timeframes. Graphic A-1 is a reference to the various stages of a stock’s life cycle. For a high-definition downloadable electronic copy of this please see page 234.
samples.translated_text: 所有交易策略都应建立在对市场结构的深刻理解之上。资金在不同资产间的流入与流出存在明确的周期性规律，我们可以在所有时间周期的图表中观察到这一节奏。图A-1展示了股票生命周期的各个阶段。如需获取此图的高清可下载电子版，请参见第234页。
samples.fit_text: 所有交易策略都应建立在对市场结构的深刻理解之上。资金在不同资产间的流入与流出存在明确的周期性规律，我们可以在所有时间周期的图表中观察到这一节奏。图A-1展示了股票生命周期的各个阶段。如需获取此图的高清可下载电子版，请参见第234页。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 437.95000000000005pt, height: 27.25999999999999pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p168_item_2_0_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.74pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 27.25999999999999pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 所有交易策略都应建立在对市场结构的深刻理解之上。资金在不同资产间的流入与流出存在明确的周期性规律，我们可以在所有时间周期的图表中观察到这一节奏。图A-1展示了股票生命周期的各个阶段。如需获取此图的高清可下载电子版，请参见第234页。

samples.source_text: Stage 1 Accumulation refers to the period when a stock has no trend, it trades sideways. It occurs after a drop in prices. It is the process of buyers gaining control from sellers. We know it is in Stage 1 only after the buyers have regained control of the stock and it enters a Stage 2 Uptrend. Until it is in uptrend, it is a trendless market that should be avoided by trend traders.
samples.translated_text: 第一阶段吸筹期指股票无趋势、横盘整理的阶段。该阶段出现在价格下跌之后，是买方从卖方手中逐步获得控制权的过程。只有在买方重新掌控股票并进入第二阶段上升趋势后，我们才能确认其处于第一阶段。在进入上升趋势之前，市场处于无趋势状态，趋势交易者应予以回避。
samples.fit_text: 第一阶段吸筹期指股票无趋势、横盘整理的阶段。该阶段出现在价格下跌之后，是买方从卖方手中逐步获得控制权的过程。只有在买方重新掌控股票并进入第二阶段上升趋势后，我们才能确认其处于第一阶段。在进入上升趋势之前，市场处于无趋势状态，趋势交易者应予以回避。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 437.95000000000005pt, height: 18.799999999999983pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p168_item_4_2_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.83pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.49em, fit_height: 18.799999999999983pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 第二阶段 拉升（上涨趋势）是股票或市场的看涨阶段。拉升阶段表现为上涨趋势，其定义为更高的高点和更高的低点。此时应持有多头头寸。处于上涨趋势中的股票被视为“无罪推定”。

samples.source_text: Stage 2 Markup (Uptrend) is the bullish phase for stocks or markets. The markup stage is an uptrend, as defined by higher-highs and higher-lows. This is the time to be long. Stocks in uptrends are considered to be “innocent until proven guilty.”
samples.translated_text: 第二阶段 拉升（上涨趋势）是股票或市场的看涨阶段。拉升阶段表现为上涨趋势，其定义为更高的高点和更高的低点。此时应持有多头头寸。处于上涨趋势中的股票被视为“无罪推定”。
samples.fit_text: 第二阶段 拉升（上涨趋势）是股票或市场的看涨阶段。拉升阶段表现为上涨趋势，其定义为更高的高点和更高的低点。此时应持有多头头寸。处于上涨趋势中的股票被视为“无罪推定”。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 447.45000000000005pt, height: 18.799999999999955pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p168_item_5_3_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.83pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.49em, fit_height: 18.799999999999955pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 第三阶段：派发。经过一段长时间的上涨后，卖方力量将开始压倒买方。这导致股票或市场进入横盘整理。我们只有在下跌开始后，才能确认其处于第三阶段。在下跌开始之前，这只是一个中性时期，趋势交易者应予以规避。

samples.source_text: Stage 3 Distribution. After a prolonged advance, sellers will start to overwhelm the buyers. This causes the stock or market to turn sideways. We know that it is Stage 3 only after a decline has begun. Until the decline begins, it is just a neutral period that should be avoided by trend traders.
samples.translated_text: 第三阶段：派发。经过一段长时间的上涨后，卖方力量将开始压倒买方。这导致股票或市场进入横盘整理。我们只有在下跌开始后，才能确认其处于第三阶段。在下跌开始之前，这只是一个中性时期，趋势交易者应予以规避。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 446.5pt, height: 17.860000000000014pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p168_item_6_4_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.69pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 17.86pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 阶段4下跌（下降趋势）是股票生命周期中的看跌阶段。此时应关注做空机会。下跌阶段以更低的低点和更低的高点为特征。处于下降趋势的股票被视为“有罪直至证明清白”。

samples.source_text: Stage 4 Decline (Downtrend) is the bearish phase of a stock’s life. This is when to be on the lookout for short sale opportunities. The Decline stage is defined by lower-lows and lower-highs. Stocks in downtrends are considered “guilty until proven innocent.”

samples.source_text: Trend Formation
samples.fit_text: Trend Formation

samples.source_text: The direction of the expansions and contractions forms trends on a chart. A move in the trend’s direction on a higher (longer-term) timeframe is referred to as an “impulse wave.” In the short-term, countertrend moves are called a “pullback 1 .”
samples.translated_text: 扩张与收缩的方向在图表上形成趋势。在更高（更长周期）时间框架上沿趋势方向的运动被称为“推动浪”。在短期，逆趋势运动被称为“回调浪”。
samples.fit_text: 扩张与收缩的方向在图表上形成趋势。在更高（更长周期）时间框架上沿趋势方向的运动被称为“推动浪”。在短期，逆趋势运动被称为“回调浪”。

samples.source_text: The rally high is called the “swing high” and the pullback low is known as the “swing low.” The trend’s “legs or thrusts” will see an expansion of the range higher, followed by a contraction of the range in the corrective leg. These are show in charts A-2 and A-3.
samples.translated_text: 反弹高点称为“摆动高点”，回调低点则称为“摆动低点”。趋势的“腿或推力”将表现为区间向上扩张，随后在修正腿中区间收缩。这些情况在图表A-2和A-3中均有展示。
samples.fit_text: 反弹高点称为“摆动高点”，回调低点则称为“摆动低点”。趋势的“腿或推力”将表现为区间向上扩张，随后在修正腿中区间收缩。这些情况在图表A-2和A-3中均有展示。

samples.fit_text: Trade with the Trend

samples.source_text: When a stock has higher-highs (HH) and higher-lows (HL) it is in an uptrend. Only in retrospect, after prices pull back from each peak, can we identify the HH.
samples.translated_text: 当一只股票呈现更高的高点（HH）和更高的低点（HL）时，它处于上升趋势中。只有在价格从每个峰值回落后，我们才能事后识别出HH。

samples.source_text: Chart A-2 shows three higher thrusts, represented by the green arrows. The red arrows show the “corrective waves” or pullbacks. Keep in mind, a pullback only occurs in a primary uptrend. Stocks in downtrends are considered “guilty until proven innocent,” and should be avoided by long traders.

typst.markdown_text: 注意，图表A-2上的最后一个更高低点（HL）旁有一个问号。随着趋势转变为修正模式，我们无法确定其将形成更高低点，还是会直接失败并逆转趋势。

samples.source_text: The very definition of a trend is what gives us the most compelling reason to be trend traders. An uptrend is marked by a series of HH’s and HL’s. This means the sum of the rallies will be greater than the sum of the declines. Therefore the best odds for profitability lie in trading in the direction of the primary trend.
samples.translated_text: 趋势的定义本身为我们提供了成为趋势交易者最令人信服的理由。上升趋势由一系列更高的高点（HH）和更高的低点（HL）所标识。这意味着上涨的总和将大于下跌的总和。因此，盈利的最佳概率在于顺应主要趋势的方向进行交易。
samples.fit_text: 趋势的定义本身为我们提供了成为趋势交易者最令人信服的理由。上升趋势由一系列更高的高点（HH）和更高的低点（HL）所标识。这意味着上涨的总和将大于下跌的总和。因此，盈利的最佳概率在于顺应主要趋势的方向进行交易。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 441.75pt, height: 17.8599999999999pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p169_item_9_7_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.69pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 17.8599999999999pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 与支撑位和阻力位类似，我们只能在事后确认是否形成更高的低点，因此需等待价格回升的证据才考虑买入。若股价确实回升，则最近的低点被称为“摆动低点”。

samples.source_text: Note, the last HL on Chart A-2 has a question mark next to it. As the trend changes to a corrective mode, we do not know if it will be a HL or if it will fail outright and reverse the trend.

samples.source_text: Just like Support and Resistance, we know only after the fact if it will be a higher-low, so we wait for evidence of a turn back higher before we consider a purchase. If the stock does turn back up, the most recent low is known as the “swing low.”

samples.source_text: Regardless of the timeframe, from years using the weekly chart down to just one day using a one-minute chart, the behavior of a stock in an uptrend is the same. The uptrend action looks like a staircase, with prior levels of Resistance that act as Support for price.
samples.translated_text: 无论时间框架如何，从使用周线图的数年周期到仅使用一分钟图的单日周期，股票在上升趋势中的行为模式是相同的。上升趋势的走势呈现为阶梯状，其中先前的阻力位会转变为价格的支持位。

samples.source_text: The opposite is also true for downtrends that are defined by a series of lower-highs and lower-lows. Chart A-3. illustrates how, in a downtrend, the sum of the declines will be greater than the sum of the rallies.

typst.markdown_text: 市场在上升趋势中的价格上涨期与下降趋势中的价格收缩期之间交替。价格区间的扩大消耗了大量能量，市场需要“休息”。这种休息期可以通过价格回调或横向盘整（即“时间修正”）来实现。

samples.source_text: Price and Time Corrections
samples.translated_text: 时间调整比价格调整更有可能使趋势延续上行。时间调整中缺乏更深的价格回撤表明买盘持续强劲。更深的价格调整则显示股票买盘意愿较弱。无论调整以何种方式发生，它都为股票提供了重建动能、接受或拒绝新价格的机会。
samples.fit_text: 时间调整比价格调整更有可能使趋势延续上行。时间调整中缺乏更深的价格回撤表明买盘持续强劲。更深的价格调整则显示股票买盘意愿较弱。无论调整以何种方式发生，它都为股票提供了重建动能、接受或拒绝新价格的机会。

typst.markdown_text: 时间调整比价格调整更有可能使趋势延续上行。时间调整中缺乏更深的价格回撤表明买盘持续强劲。更深的价格调整则显示股票买盘意愿较弱。无论调整以何种方式发生，它都为股票提供了重建动能、接受或拒绝新价格的机会。

samples.source_text: Markets alternate between periods of price advancement in an uptrend, and price contraction as it consolidates the decline in a downtrend. The expansion of the price range uses up a lot of energy and the market needs to “rest.” The rest period can occur by a price pullback or a sideways, “correction through time.”
samples.translated_text: 市场在上升趋势中的价格上涨期与下降趋势中的价格收缩期之间交替。价格区间的扩大消耗了大量能量，市场需要“休息”。这种休息期可以通过价格回调或横向盘整（即“时间修正”）来实现。

typst.markdown_text: 当股票通过时间进行修正（如图A-4中绿色阴影区域所示）时，其看涨信号被认为强于价格回调（由红色箭头指示）。只要形成更高的低点（HL），价格修正仍具看涨意义。应同时监测时间和价格修正，以寻找买方重获主导权的证据，从而我们可在主要趋势方向上进行低风险买入。

samples.source_text: A time correction is a stronger candidate for a trend to continue higher than a price correction. The lack of a deeper price pullback in a time correction indicates a continued strong bid for shares. A deeper price correction shows a less agressive bid for the stock. Regardless of how a correction occurs, it is a chance for the stock to rebuild energy and accept or reject new prices.
samples.translated_text: 时间调整比价格调整更有可能使趋势延续上行。时间调整中缺乏更深的价格回撤表明买盘持续强劲。更深的价格调整则显示股票买盘意愿较弱。无论调整以何种方式发生，它都为股票提供了重建动能、接受或拒绝新价格的机会。

samples.source_text: It is considered more bullish when a stock corrects through time (as shown in Chart A-4 with the green shaded areas) than a price pullback (indicated by the red arrows). A price correction is still bullish as long as a HL is formed. Both time and price corrections should be monitored for evidence of buyers regaining control so we can make a low-risk purchase in the direction of the primary trend.

typst.markdown_text: 在图A-5中，处于下跌趋势中的时间修正（红色阴影区域）被认为是脆弱的，因为它们显示出缺乏积极的买盘。该股票的弱势买方最终被压制，股价继续走低。价格修正（绿色箭头）是短期反弹，最终因长期下跌趋势占主导而失败。

samples.source_text: The opposite action unfolds for a stock in a downtrend, as seen in Chart A-5.
samples.translated_text: 对于处于下跌趋势的股票，其作用方向相反，如图A-5所示。
samples.fit_text: 对于处于下跌趋势的股票，其作用方向相反，如图A-5所示。

samples.source_text: On Chart A-5, time corrections in a downtrend (red shaded areas) are considered vulnerable because they show a lack of aggressive demand. Weak bidders for the stock are eventually overwhelmed and the stock continues lower. The price corrections (green arrows) are short-term rallies which end up in failure as the longer-term downtrend prevails.

samples.source_text: It may be tempting to buy stocks at perceived bargain prices. It has been a popular mantra to “buy the dip” but as we have seen in Part Two Chapter 7, buying dips is a flawed strategy. “Selling the rip” is another popular phrase amongst market participants who sell short the strength of a stock. If you have ever acted on this kind of advice, I’m glad you’re reading this book and hope you’ll change your strategy and make stronger trades as a result.
samples.translated_text: 以看似低廉的价格买入股票可能颇具诱惑力。“逢低买入”曾是一种流行的口号，但正如我们在第二部分第七章所见，逢低买入是一种有缺陷的策略。“逢高卖出”是市场参与者中另一个流行说法，指的是在股票走强时做空。如果你曾依据此类建议进行交易，我很高兴你正在阅读本书，并希望你能因此改变策略，进行更稳健的交易。

samples.source_text: APPENDIX B: MULTIPLE TIMEFRAMES
samples.fit_text: APPENDIX B: MULTIPLE TIMEFRAMES

samples.source_text: AVWAP is a primary part of my analysis, which I integrate into my multiple timeframe analysis. Time is one of the few variables that we can control in our pursuit of market profits. Of course, we cannot control time itself, but we can control our entries and exits and the length of time we choose to hold our trades or investments.
samples.translated_text: AVWAP是我分析中的一个主要组成部分，我将其整合到我的多时间框架分析中。时间是我们追求市场利润时少数可以控制的变量之一。当然，我们无法控制时间本身，但我们可以控制我们的入场和出场，以及我们选择持有交易或投资的时间长度。
samples.fit_text: AVWAP是我分析中的一个主要组成部分，我将其整合到我的多时间框架分析中。时间是我们追求市场利润时少数可以控制的变量之一。当然，我们无法控制时间本身，但我们可以控制我们的入场和出场，以及我们选择持有交易或投资的时间长度。

samples.source_text: The period of time that we allow our market activities to work for (or against) us is completely subjective. Whether you are a day trader, long-term investor, or somewhere between, the goal is to always cut our losers and hold our winners. The most effective way to achieve this goal is to listen objectively to the message of the market. This is accomplished through technical analysis.
samples.translated_text: 我们允许市场活动为我们（或对我们不利）发挥作用的时间段完全是主观的。无论您是日内交易者、长期投资者，还是介于两者之间，目标始终是截断亏损，让利润奔跑。实现这一目标最有效的方法是客观地倾听市场传递的信息。这通过技术分析来实现。
samples.fit_text: 我们允许市场活动为我们（或对我们不利）发挥作用的时间段完全是主观的。无论您是日内交易者、长期投资者，还是介于两者之间，目标始终是截断亏损，让利润奔跑。实现这一目标最有效的方法是客观地倾听市场传递的信息。这通过技术分析来实现。

typst.markdown_text: 我们允许市场活动为我们（或对我们不利）发挥作用的时间段完全是主观的。无论您是日内交易者、长期投资者，还是介于两者之间，目标始终是截断亏损，让利润奔跑。实现这一目标最有效的方法是客观地倾听市场传递的信息。这通过技术分析来实现。

samples.source_text: Often, the message of the market will be one of confusion, and that is an important message to recognize. If there is no clear trend or potential trade setup, the message of the market is clear, “be patient, cash is a position.” We may want to constantly be engaged in the market, but it is better to observe and wait for the “fat pitch” trade to set up before getting involved in a new trade.
samples.translated_text: 市场的信号常常是混乱的，而认识到这一点至关重要。若市场未呈现明确趋势或潜在交易机会，其传递的信息便很清晰：“保持耐心，持有现金也是一种头寸。”我们或许总想持续参与市场，但更好的做法是观察并等待“绝佳机会”出现，再介入新的交易。

typst.markdown_text: 市场的信号常常是混乱的，而认识到这一点至关重要。若市场未呈现明确趋势或潜在交易机会，其传递的信息便很清晰：“保持耐心，持有现金也是一种头寸。”我们或许总想持续参与市场，但更好的做法是观察并等待“绝佳机会”出现，再介入新的交易。

samples.source_text: Choose Your Timeframe
samples.fit_text: Choose Your Timeframe

samples.source_text: One of the most important aspects to your success in the markets is to identify and stick to a timeframe that is best suited to your personality. Some people are more attracted to day trading in-and-out of highly volatile stocks, while others may be more interested in a buy and hold approach.
samples.translated_text: 在市场中取得成功的关键之一，是确定并坚持最适合你个性的交易时间框架。有些人更倾向于日内交易波动性较高的股票，而另一些人可能对买入并持有的策略更感兴趣。
samples.fit_text: 在市场中取得成功的关键之一，是确定并坚持最适合你个性的交易时间框架。有些人更倾向于日内交易波动性较高的股票，而另一些人可能对买入并持有的策略更感兴趣。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 443.65000000000003pt, height: 17.860000000000014pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p173_item_5_3_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.69pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 17.86pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 在市场中取得成功的关键之一，是确定并坚持最适合你个性的交易时间框架。有些人更倾向于日内交易波动性较高的股票，而另一些人可能对买入并持有的策略更感兴趣。

samples.source_text: I am not the first person to observe that the number of trades one makes will typically decrease over the number of years that you are in the markets. The fact is some people are great day traders and that is where they should focus there energy. There are many excellent long-term investors and that should be their focus. The bottom line is to find the timeframe that works best for you.
samples.translated_text: 我并非第一个注意到，随着在市场中交易年数的增加，交易次数通常会减少的人。事实上，有些人擅长日内交易，这应是他们精力的集中点。也有许多优秀的长期投资者，这应是他们的专注领域。关键在于找到最适合自己的时间框架。
samples.fit_text: 我并非第一个注意到，随着在市场中交易年数的增加，交易次数通常会减少的人。事实上，有些人擅长日内交易，这应是他们精力的集中点。也有许多优秀的长期投资者，这应是他们的专注领域。关键在于找到最适合自己的时间框架。

typst.markdown_text: 我并非第一个注意到，随着在市场中交易年数的增加，交易次数通常会减少的人。事实上，有些人擅长日内交易，这应是他们精力的集中点。也有许多优秀的长期投资者，这应是他们的专注领域。关键在于找到最适合自己的时间框架。

samples.source_text: Many traders use a combination of timeframes for different strategies. This is how bond managers must work. They diversify their timeframes by the purchase of 2, 5, 10, 20-years and longer maturities. They are forced to trade various timeframes. Do what works for you and go slow until you figure out the timeframe that is most suitable for you.
samples.translated_text: 许多交易者会结合使用不同时间框架来实施多种策略。债券经理人必须采用这种方式运作。他们通过购买2年、5年、10年、20年及更长期限的债券来实现时间框架的分散化。他们不得不交易多种时间框架。选择适合您的方法，并逐步探索，直到找到最适合您的时间框架。

typst.markdown_text: 许多交易者会结合使用不同时间框架来实施多种策略。债券经理人必须采用这种方式运作。他们通过购买2年、5年、10年、20年及更长期限的债券来实现时间框架的分散化。他们不得不交易多种时间框架。选择适合您的方法，并逐步探索，直到找到最适合您的时间框架。

samples.source_text: Only you can know the best timeframe for your personality, and you will likely make a lot of mistakes as you figure it out. A long-term investor uses the longer-term market exposure to smooth out returns. This requires a tolerance for short-term volatility. Shorter-term traders attempt to exploit the volatile periods for quicker returns and need to make quick and accurate decisions. Because of their shorter timeframe focus, shorter-term traders can experience a full cycle of emotions in a single market session that will take longer-term investors years to experience. This leaves shorter-term traders more vulnerable to costly, emotional decisions.
samples.translated_text: 只有你自己才能确定最适合你个性的时间框架，并且在此过程中你可能会犯很多错误。长期投资者利用较长时间的市场敞口来平滑收益，这需要对短期波动有一定的容忍度。短期交易者则试图利用波动期获取更快的回报，需要做出快速而准确的决策。由于他们关注的时间框架较短，短期交易者可能在单个市场交易时段内经历一个完整的情绪周期，而这对于长期投资者来说可能需要数年才能经历。这使得短期交易者更容易做出代价高昂的情绪化决策。

samples.source_text: We need to use even-sized bars/candles for accurate analysis. A common mistake made in technical analysis is to use timeframes where there are an uneven number of periods each day.

samples.fit_text: 我们需要使用等长的柱线/蜡烛图进行准确分析。技术分析中一个常见的错误是使用每日周期数不均匀的时间框架。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 447.45000000000005pt, height: 50.75999999999999pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p173_item_17_14_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.11pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.5em, fit_height: 50.75999999999999pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 这种方法不公平地偏重某一时段而忽视其他时段，从而扭曲了我们分析中使用的任何技术工具。以60分钟数据（小时时间框架）构建的图表为例。市场每日交易时间为上午9:30至下午4:00，总计6.5小时，即390分钟。若使用小时柱线（蜡烛图）构建图表，图表上将显示7个时段，但这无法准确反映价格行为。若要对数据进行分析，必须确保数据集具有一致性：若第一个时段仅为30分钟（上午9:30-10:00），而其余时段均为60分钟（上午10:00-11:00等），则数据将存在缺陷。这如同用1厘米测量一次、再用英寸测量其余11次来衡量标准的12英寸英尺——将这12个单位相加，结果并不等于12英寸。

samples.source_text: This approach unfairly weighs one period over the others and therefore skews any technical tools we use in our analysis. Take the example with a chart constructed of 60-minute data (the hourly timeframe.) The market is open from 9:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m each day, which is a total of 6.5 hours, or 390 minutes. If you were to use a chart constructed of hourly bars (candles) you would see 7 periods on the chart, but this would not accurately reflect the price action. If we are to do any analysis of data, we need to make sure we have a consistent data set, if the first period was just 30 minutes (9:30-10:00 a.m.) and the rest were 60 minutes (10:00-11:00, etc.) the data would be flawed. It would be like measuring a standard 12-inch foot with 1 measurement of 1 centimeter and the other 11 measurements with inches. When you add those 12 units up, it does not equal 12 inches.

typst.markdown_text: 当看到一只股票处于强劲的上升趋势时，趋势交易似乎很容易。许多参与者未能理解，一只股票在不同时间框架下可能存在多重趋势。他们只看到股票的长期上升轨迹，而忽视了上升趋势中任何可能导致快速亏损的强劲回调。

samples.source_text: Timeframe Confusion

typst.markdown_text: 解决这一矛盾的方法是同时分析多个时间尺度，以获得更完整的图景。

samples.source_text: Trend trades may seem easy when you see a stock in a strong uptrend. Many participants fail to understand that a stock can have multiple trends on different timeframes. They only see the longer-term upward trajectory of the stock and overlook and any strong pullbacks in the uptrend that can result in quick losses.
samples.translated_text: 当看到一只股票处于强劲的上升趋势时，趋势交易似乎很容易。许多参与者未能理解，一只股票在不同时间框架下可能存在多重趋势。他们只看到股票的长期上升轨迹，而忽视了上升趋势中任何可能导致快速亏损的强劲回调。
samples.fit_text: 当看到一只股票处于强劲的上升趋势时，趋势交易似乎很容易。许多参与者未能理解，一只股票在不同时间框架下可能存在多重趋势。他们只看到股票的长期上升轨迹，而忽视了上升趋势中任何可能导致快速亏损的强劲回调。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 438.90000000000003pt, height: 26.32000000000005pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p174_item_4_3_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 7.65pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.48em, fit_height: 26.32pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 一只在过去一年处于长期上升趋势的股票，可能在最近两周处于下跌趋势，并可能刚刚开始在更短的时间框架内转向上行。这些相互冲突的趋势可能导致时间框架混淆，或在长期趋势已充分展开后情绪化地买入股票。买入近期已上涨的股票会使风险控制变得更加困难。

samples.source_text: The remedy to this conflict is to analyze multiple timeframes together for a more complete picture.
samples.translated_text: 解决这一矛盾的方法是同时分析多个时间尺度，以获得更完整的图景。
samples.fit_text: 解决这一矛盾的方法是同时分析多个时间尺度，以获得更完整的图景。

samples.source_text: A stock in a longer-term uptrend over the last year may be in a downtrend over the last 2 weeks and may have just begun to turn higher on a shorter-term timeframe. These conflicting trends can create timeframe confusion or an emotionally driven purchase of a stock after a longer-term move is well underway. The purchase of a stock that has already moved in the near term makes it more difficult to control risk.
samples.translated_text: 一只在过去一年处于长期上升趋势的股票，可能在最近两周处于下跌趋势，并可能刚刚开始在更短的时间框架内转向上行。这些相互冲突的趋势可能导致时间框架混淆，或在长期趋势已充分展开后情绪化地买入股票。买入近期已上涨的股票会使风险控制变得更加困难。

typst.markdown_text: 媒体总是关注我们是否处于“牛市或熊市”。而波段交易者的任务是在任何市场条件下都能获利。虽然大多数股票会与整体市场趋势同步交易，但总有例外。即使在最强劲的牛市中，也存在持续数周至数月的时期，此时整体环境并不利于新购入股票。

samples.source_text: Focus on the Trend, not the Label
samples.translated_text: 在牛市的“看跌阶段”，买入处于近期下跌趋势的股票并不明智。更好的策略是持有现金以保护资本，或进行战术性卖空操作。同样，在熊市中，一些最快的短期上涨行情出现在反弹阶段。交易这些反弹是一种高风险策略，只有经验最丰富的交易者才应尝试利用这些逆势波动。每当进行与主要趋势方向相反的交易时，应减小仓位风险并减少隔夜敞口。

samples.source_text: The media is always focused on whether we are “in a bull or bear market.” A swing trader’s job is to make money regardless of market conditions. While most stocks will trade in-line with the trends of the overall market, there are always exceptions. Even in the strongest bull markets there are periods of weeks to months where the overall environment does not favor new purchases of stocks.
samples.translated_text: 媒体总是关注我们是否处于“牛市或熊市”。而波段交易者的任务是在任何市场条件下都能获利。虽然大多数股票会与整体市场趋势同步交易，但总有例外。即使在最强劲的牛市中，也存在持续数周至数月的时期，此时整体环境并不利于新购入股票。

samples.source_text: In the “bearish-periods” of a bull market, it doesn’t make sense to buy stocks that are in near-term downtrends. It is better to either stay on the sidelines in cash and protect your capital or to make tactical short sales. Just the same, in a bear market, some of the fastest short-term moves occur on the upside. Trading these rallies is a risky strategy and only the most skilled traders should attempt to take advantage of these countertrend moves. Whenever a trade is taken against the direction of the primary trend, it should be done with smaller risk in position size and less overnight exposure.

samples.source_text: As we know, stocks in downtrends are considered “guilty until proven innocent,” which means we do not trust the rally attempts. It is common for inexperienced traders to get excited about a large move higher with a stock in a downtrend. These are typically “sucker rallies” as they make a lower-high in the short-term trend and then roll back over with a new wave of shares sold by fearful sellers.
samples.translated_text: 众所周知，处于下跌趋势中的股票被视为“有罪直至证明清白”，这意味着我们不信任其反弹尝试。缺乏经验的交易者常会对下跌趋势中的股票出现大幅上涨感到兴奋。这些通常是“诱多反弹”，因为它们会在短期趋势中形成更低的高点，随后因恐慌性卖盘涌现而再度回落。

typst.markdown_text: 一旦我判断出相对于感知风险存在盈利潜力，我倾向于不执着于特定的价格“目标”，而是“倾听”股票发出的退出信号。许多人过度关注上行目标，向市场索取他们期望的利润。结果，他们未能领会市场传递的信息。市场不会迎合目标要求，如果我们固执地等待股票达到预设目标，原本盈利的交易可能转为亏损。

samples.source_text: Price Targets
samples.translated_text: 一旦确定了风险/回报比，我的价格目标对于多头而言是“更高”，对于空头则是“更低”。我们的任务是倾听市场，进行调整，并在价格沿趋势发展时提高止损位以保护利润。
samples.fit_text: 一旦确定了风险/回报比，我的价格目标对于多头而言是“更高”，对于空头则是“更低”。我们的任务是倾听市场，进行调整，并在价格沿趋势发展时提高止损位以保护利润。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 444.59999999999997pt, height: 20.680000000000064pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p176_item_6_5_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.09pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.5em, fit_height: 20.68pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 一旦确定了风险/回报比，我的价格目标对于多头而言是“更高”，对于空头则是“更低”。我们的任务是倾听市场，进行调整，并在价格沿趋势发展时提高止损位以保护利润。

samples.source_text: Once I have determined if it looks like there is potential for profit relative to the perceived risk, I prefer not to stay focused on a price “target,” but to “listen” to the stock to tell me when to exit. Many people focus too much on an upside target and tell the market what they want for a profit. As a result, they fail to listen to the message of the market. The market does not honor target requests and a profitable trade can turn into a loser if we stubbornly wait for the stock to hit our target.

samples.source_text: Once I have determined my risk/reward ratio, my price targets are “higher” for longs and “lower” for shorts. Our job is to listen to the market, adjust, and raise our stops to protect our gains as the price progresses in the trend.

samples.source_text: Multiple Timeframe Analysis
samples.fit_text: Multiple Timeframe Analysis

samples.source_text: Our analysis should begin with a chart constructed of daily price history for the last year or so. This timeframe is simply to make sure our trade candidate is in a primary uptrend (for longs) or downtrend (for shorts). We want to be sure there aren’t any large potential levels of Resistance or Support nearby that might slow or halt the momentum.
samples.translated_text: 我们的分析应从构建过去一年左右的日价格历史图表开始。这一时间范围旨在确保交易候选标的处于主要上升趋势（对于多头）或下降趋势（对于空头）。我们需要确认附近不存在可能减缓或阻碍动量的任何大型潜在阻力位或支撑位。
samples.fit_text: 我们的分析应从构建过去一年左右的日价格历史图表开始。这一时间范围旨在确保交易候选标的处于主要上升趋势（对于多头）或下降趋势（对于空头）。我们需要确认附近不存在可能减缓或阻碍动量的任何大型潜在阻力位或支撑位。

typst.markdown_text: 我们的分析应从构建过去一年左右的日价格历史图表开始。这一时间范围旨在确保交易候选标的处于主要上升趋势（对于多头）或下降趋势（对于空头）。我们需要确认附近不存在可能减缓或阻碍动量的任何大型潜在阻力位或支撑位。

samples.source_text: Trend Alignment
samples.fit_text: Trend Alignment

samples.source_text: We want to get involved in the stock as the intermediate-term trend (days to weeks) comes “into alignment” with the longer, more powerful trend. This approach allows us to enter the longer-term trend as the intermediate-term trend develops. When the trends of various timeframes move in the same direction, they are “aligned.” The goal is to find the ideal point of entry, set our initial stop-loss, and then to manage our winners. Many of the strategies we have explored are guided by this very concept. The strategies section covered the use of shorter timeframes to fine tune our entry points.
samples.translated_text: 我们希望当股票的中期趋势（数天至数周）与更长期、更强劲的趋势“趋于一致”时介入。这种方法使我们能够在中期趋势发展的过程中进入长期趋势。当不同时间框架的趋势朝同一方向运动时，它们即“一致”。目标是找到理想的入场点，设置初始止损，然后管理我们的盈利头寸。我们探讨的许多策略正是以此概念为指导。策略部分涵盖了利用更短时间框架来微调入场点的方法。
samples.fit_text: 我们希望当股票的中期趋势（数天至数周）与更长期、更强劲的趋势“趋于一致”时介入。这种方法使我们能够在中期趋势发展的过程中进入长期趋势。当不同时间框架的趋势朝同一方向运动时，它们即“一致”。目标是找到理想的入场点，设置初始止损，然后管理我们的盈利头寸。我们探讨的许多策略正是以此概念为指导。策略部分涵盖了利用更短时间框架来微调入场点的方法。

typst.markdown_text: 我们希望当股票的中期趋势（数天至数周）与更长期、更强劲的趋势“趋于一致”时介入。这种方法使我们能够在中期趋势发展的过程中进入长期趋势。当不同时间框架的趋势朝同一方向运动时，它们即“一致”。目标是找到理想的入场点，设置初始止损，然后管理我们的盈利头寸。我们探讨的许多策略正是以此概念为指导。策略部分涵盖了利用更短时间框架来微调入场点的方法。

samples.source_text: Multiple timeframe trend alignment gives us a greater confidence of trend continuation and that allows us to be more aggressive in our position size. Determination of the size of a trade is based largely on where we enter a trend and how we can control our perceived risk. Besides the location of our trade entry, other reasons to adjust position size would include; overall market conditions, news risk such as upcoming earnings reports or a Fed meeting, and even our own personal well-being.
samples.translated_text: 多时间框架趋势对齐增强了我们对趋势延续的信心，从而允许我们采取更激进的仓位规模。交易规模的确定主要基于我们进入趋势的位置以及如何控制感知风险。除了交易入场点外，调整仓位规模的其他原因还包括：整体市场状况、新闻风险（如即将发布的财报或美联储会议），甚至我们自身的个人状态。
samples.fit_text: 多时间框架趋势对齐增强了我们对趋势延续的信心，从而允许我们采取更激进的仓位规模。交易规模的确定主要基于我们进入趋势的位置以及如何控制感知风险。除了交易入场点外，调整仓位规模的其他原因还包括：整体市场状况、新闻风险（如即将发布的财报或美联储会议），甚至我们自身的个人状态。

typst.markdown_text: 多时间框架趋势对齐增强了我们对趋势延续的信心，从而允许我们采取更激进的仓位规模。交易规模的确定主要基于我们进入趋势的位置以及如何控制感知风险。除了交易入场点外，调整仓位规模的其他原因还包括：整体市场状况、新闻风险（如即将发布的财报或美联储会议），甚至我们自身的个人状态。

samples.source_text: To become proficient at trend alignment strategies, make it a habit to look at stocks on multiple timeframes to gain a better comprehension of how the timeframes flow together. Observe how trends from shorter timeframes coexist, interact, and build upon, the longer-term market structure. One tenet of technical analysis is, “a trend once established is more likely to continue than reverse.” The long-term trend is the sum of many shorter-term trends, therefore, we need to wait for evidence the trends are back in alignment before we get involved.
samples.translated_text: 要熟练掌握趋势对齐策略，应养成在多时间框架下观察股票的习惯，以更好地理解不同时间框架如何协同作用。观察短期时间框架的趋势如何与长期市场结构共存、互动并相互构建。技术分析的一条原则是：“趋势一旦确立，延续的可能性大于反转。”长期趋势是多个短期趋势的总和，因此我们需要等待趋势重新对齐的证据出现后再参与。

typst.markdown_text: “如有疑问，请放大视野”

samples.source_text: ~J.C. Parets
samples.fit_text: ~J.C. Parets

samples.source_text: Always be aware of the longer timeframe, even if you do not plan to hold a stock for a long while. The longer-term trends should be respected as it takes a lot of energy in the opposite direction to reverse them. Defer to the longer and more powerful trend if in doubt. If the long-term trend is up, give the benefit of doubt to the bulls and vice versa. Longer timeframes offer greater opportunity for profit, while shorter-term timeframes offer the fastest (more seductive) opportunity to turn a quick profit.
samples.translated_text: 即使你不打算长期持有一只股票，也应始终关注更长的时间框架。长期趋势应予以尊重，因为逆转它们需要巨大的反向能量。如有疑问，应遵从更长、更强大的趋势。如果长期趋势向上，则倾向于看涨方，反之亦然。更长的时间框架提供了更大的盈利机会，而较短的时间框架则提供了最快（更具诱惑力）的快速获利机会。

typst.markdown_text: 即使你不打算长期持有一只股票，也应始终关注更长的时间框架。长期趋势应予以尊重，因为逆转它们需要巨大的反向能量。如有疑问，应遵从更长、更强大的趋势。如果长期趋势向上，则倾向于看涨方，反之亦然。更长的时间框架提供了更大的盈利机会，而较短的时间框架则提供了最快（更具诱惑力）的快速获利机会。

samples.source_text: If you cannot find clarity on a longer timeframe, interpret it as the market telling you to avoid the stock for now. Cash is a position. When uncertainty reigns, cash affords you the objective position to wait for your signals for a low-risk entry. As you get better at timelier entries with multiple timeframes, your profitability increase, and you will also find satisfaction from not buying a stock that continues lower.
samples.translated_text: 若无法在较长的时间框架内获得明确信号，应将其解读为市场当前建议回避该股票。现金本身即是一种持仓。当不确定性主导市场时，现金使你能够保持客观立场，等待低风险入场的信号。随着你运用多时间框架进行更及时入场的能力提升，盈利能力将随之增强，并且你会从避免买入持续下跌的股票中获得满足感。
samples.fit_text: 若无法在较长的时间框架内获得明确信号，应将其解读为市场当前建议回避该股票。现金本身即是一种持仓。当不确定性主导市场时，现金使你能够保持客观立场，等待低风险入场的信号。随着你运用多时间框架进行更及时入场的能力提升，盈利能力将随之增强，并且你会从避免买入持续下跌的股票中获得满足感。

typst.markdown_text: 若无法在较长的时间框架内获得明确信号，应将其解读为市场当前建议回避该股票。现金本身即是一种持仓。当不确定性主导市场时，现金使你能够保持客观立场，等待低风险入场的信号。随着你运用多时间框架进行更及时入场的能力提升，盈利能力将随之增强，并且你会从避免买入持续下跌的股票中获得满足感。

samples.source_text: A quick and easy reference point to determine the primary trend is the slope of the 50-day moving average (“DMA”). If the stock is above a rising 50- DMA, consider it “innocent until proven guilty” and therefore is a candidate for a long trade. If the stock is below a 50-DMA that is sloped lower, it is “guilty until proven innocent.” Avoid these latter stocks from the long side or consider them as a candidate for a short sale. When a stock crosses above and below a flat 50-DMA, it shows us a trendless stock that offers no advantage for a long or short sale. The message of the market is clear in these instances, these stocks should be avoided. The direction of the 50-DMA is an efficient way to be sure we know the trend of the stock on the longer timeframe.
samples.translated_text: 一个快速简便的参考点来确定主要趋势是50日移动平均线（“DMA”）的斜率。如果股价高于上升的50日移动平均线，则视为“无罪推定”，因此是潜在的多头交易候选。如果股价低于向下倾斜的50日移动平均线，则视为“有罪推定”。应避免在后一类股票上做多，或可考虑将其作为卖空候选。当股价在平坦的50日移动平均线上下穿梭时，表明该股处于无趋势状态，无论做多或做空均无优势。在这些情况下，市场的信号很明确：应避开这些股票。50日移动平均线的方向是一种有效方法，能确保我们了解股票在较长时间框架内的趋势。

samples.source_text: All moving averages should be used as a reference point to compare price trends. They should not be considered as automatic levels to do business when they are touched, but rather as a reason to observe the price action on a shorter-term timeframe, where we formulate a plan. We generally give the benefit of doubt to buyers when the 50-DMA trend is higher and to the sellers when the slope is lower. Note, the charts are for the same stock; on the left Chart B- 4 shows the weekly moving average over a 10-week period, while Chart B-5 is the 50-day moving average. The increased detail in Chart B-5 arises from that fact that there are are five trade days per week.
samples.translated_text: 所有移动平均线均应作为比较价格趋势的参考点。它们不应被视为触及即可自动交易的价位，而应作为观察更短期时间框架内价格走势、并据此制定交易计划的依据。当50日移动平均线趋势上行时，我们通常倾向于买方；当趋势下行时，则倾向于卖方。请注意，左右两图针对同一只股票：左侧图B-4显示10周期间的周移动平均线，而图B-5为50日移动平均线。图B-5中细节的增加源于每周包含五个交易日的事实。

typst.markdown_text: 一旦确定了日线时间框架的趋势，下一步就是分析中期时间框架，以寻找可能形成低风险、高概率交易机会的线索。

samples.translated_text: 图B-5 该日线图上的50日移动平均线显示了图B.4中最近四个月的数据。图表来源：TC2000.com

samples.source_text: Once we have identified the trend on the daily timeframe, the next step is to analyze the intermediate-term timeframe for clues of a possible low-risk, high- probability trade set up.
samples.translated_text: 一旦确定了日线时间框架的趋势，下一步就是分析中期时间框架，以寻找可能形成低风险、高概率交易机会的线索。
samples.fit_text: 一旦确定了日线时间框架的趋势，下一步就是分析中期时间框架，以寻找可能形成低风险、高概率交易机会的线索。

samples.source_text: Intermediate-Term and the 5-Day Moving Average
samples.translated_text: 中期与5日移动平均线
samples.fit_text: 中期与5日移动平均线

samples.source_text: An intermediate-term timeframe helps us to determine if there is a trade setting up in a low-risk manner. This timeframe allows us to enter the stock as price momentum comes into alignment with the longer timeframe. For the intermediate-term trend, I prefer to use a 30-minute timeframe (for a period of approximately 25-40 days) with a 5-day moving average (5-DMA) as a trend reference point in a similar way to how we use the 50-DMA on a daily timeframe.
samples.translated_text: 中期时间框架有助于我们以低风险方式判断是否存在交易机会。该时间框架允许我们在价格动量与更长期趋势一致时入场。对于中期趋势，我倾向于使用30分钟时间框架（约25-40天周期），并以5日移动平均线（5-DMA）作为趋势参考点，其作用类似于日线图上使用的50-DMA。
samples.fit_text: 中期时间框架有助于我们以低风险方式判断是否存在交易机会。该时间框架允许我们在价格动量与更长期趋势一致时入场。对于中期趋势，我倾向于使用30分钟时间框架（约25-40天周期），并以5日移动平均线（5-DMA）作为趋势参考点，其作用类似于日线图上使用的50-DMA。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 437.95000000000005pt, height: 32.900000000000006pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p179_item_0_0_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.26pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.51em, fit_height: 32.9pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 中期时间框架有助于我们以低风险方式判断是否存在交易机会。该时间框架允许我们在价格动量与更长期趋势一致时入场。对于中期趋势，我倾向于使用30分钟时间框架（约25-40天周期），并以5日移动平均线（5-DMA）作为趋势参考点，其作用类似于日线图上使用的50-DMA。

samples.source_text: Note: Do not confuse the 5-day moving average with a 5-period moving average. You cannot just set the moving average to “5” on any timeframe and see the 5-DMA. It is a 5-DMA only when the chart is constructed of daily candles. The only time you will observe a true 5-DMA on a daily timeframe is when the market is closed; prior to the close it will only be four full days plus whatever part of the day has elapsed since the open. To compensate for this, I never look at the 5-DMA on a daily timeframe, only on shorter (intraday) timeframes. Here is how we accomplish this.

typst.markdown_text: 如前文所述，该市场每日开放390分钟。五个交易日内总计开放1,950分钟。例如，若我们在65分钟时间框架下观察市场，这意味着我们需要使用30周期移动平均线来复现过去5天的平均值（每日1,950分钟除以我们的30分钟时间框架$= 65$周期）。在15分钟时间框架下，5日移动平均线对应130个周期$1950 / 15 = 130$）。通过简单的计算，我们可以在不同时间框架下保持与5日移动平均线分析的一致性。

samples.source_text: Notice that the location of the moving average for the same stock on different timeframes is in the same location and has the same slope. We want to keep our analysis consistent across various timeframes, so we convert the daily moving average to intraday timeframes with this handy table (B-7).

samples.source_text: As noted earlier in the chapter, the market is open 390 minutes per day. Over five days it is open for 1,950 minutes. For example, if we look at the market on a 65-minute timeframe, that means we want to use a 30-period moving average to replicate the average over the last 5 days (1,950 minutes per day/our 30-minute timeframe = 6 5 periods). On a 15-minute timeframe, the 5-DMA is represented by 130 periods 1 9 5 0 / 1 5 = 1 3 0 ). A little bit of simple math allows us to keep consistency of our analysis in relation to 5-DMA on various timeframes.

samples.source_text: Combine Timeframes for Longs
samples.fit_text: Combine Timeframes for Longs

samples.source_text: The breakdown below outlines how we want to analyze the intermediate-term trends to plan our trade. When a stock is in a longer-term uptrend, we then look to a shorter timeframe for trend alignment.
samples.translated_text: 以下分解概述了我们如何分析中期趋势以规划交易。当股票处于长期上升趋势时，我们随后会观察更短的时间框架以寻求趋势一致性。
samples.fit_text: 以下分解概述了我们如何分析中期趋势以规划交易。当股票处于长期上升趋势时，我们随后会观察更短的时间框架以寻求趋势一致性。

samples.source_text: Stage 1 Accumulation. This is where we plan our trades. We observe, analyze, and ANTICIPATE the point where buyers will take control. This is the time to assess our price objectives and stop levels to determine our plan of action for when the stock gives us a reason to get involved. This is when we want to answer; where has the stock come from and where it has the potential to go? Then we can determine if there is a sufficient risk/reward ratio setting up to justify the purchase of a new long trade. If the 5-DMA is in decline, or beginning to flatten out, that is a clue to leave our activities to analysis and not take action at this stage.
samples.translated_text: 第一阶段：积累。在此阶段，我们规划交易。我们观察、分析并预测买方将取得控制权的时点。此时应评估价格目标与止损位，以确定当股票给出介入信号时的行动计划。此时我们需要回答：该股票从何处来，其潜在去向如何？随后，我们可判断是否已形成足以证明新多头交易合理性的风险/回报比。若5日移动平均线处于下降趋势或开始走平，则提示我们在此阶段仅进行分析而不采取行动。
samples.fit_text: 第一阶段：积累。在此阶段，我们规划交易。我们观察、分析并预测买方将取得控制权的时点。此时应评估价格目标与止损位，以确定当股票给出介入信号时的行动计划。此时我们需要回答：该股票从何处来，其潜在去向如何？随后，我们可判断是否已形成足以证明新多头交易合理性的风险/回报比。若5日移动平均线处于下降趋势或开始走平，则提示我们在此阶段仅进行分析而不采取行动。

samples.source_text: Stage 2 Markup (Uptrend). As the stock makes a short-term higher-high, which is in alignment with the longer-term uptrend, we want to buy the stock. This is when we PARTICIPATE in what appears to be a continuation of the uptrend on the longer-term timeframe. Stage 2 is also where we manage our winners by raising the stop to just under successive higher-lows as the stock rallies. This is the fun part. The 5-DMA should be flat or starting to advance at this stage. We generally want to buy the stock as it makes it’s first higher-high on this timeframe.
samples.translated_text: 阶段2标记（上升趋势）。当股票创出短期新高，且与长期上升趋势一致时，我们应买入该股票。此时，我们参与看似在更长时间框架下延续的上升趋势。阶段2也是我们管理盈利仓位的地方，随着股价上涨，将止损位上移至连续更高的低点下方。这是有趣的部分。在此阶段，5日移动平均线应趋于平缓或开始上行。通常，我们希望在股票于该时间框架下首次创出新高时买入。

samples.source_text: Stage 3 Distribution. This is when the stock shows signs of fatigue and may precede a price pullback, or it may need a “correction through time.” This is a good point to EXIT the position, or at least sell some to lock in partial profits, and tighten the stop on the balance, rather than continue to expose our capital to risk. The angle of ascent of the 5-DMA will slow down or begin to turn sideways at this point.
samples.translated_text: 阶段3分布。此时股票显现疲态，可能预示价格回调，或需经历“时间修正”。这是退出头寸或至少部分卖出以锁定利润、收紧剩余头寸止损的良好时机，而非继续使资金暴露于风险之中。此时5日移动平均线的上升角度将放缓或开始转为横向。

typst.markdown_text: 在图B-8中，我们展示了日线图（含50日移动平均线），而在图B-9中，相同数据以65分钟时间框架（含5日/30周期移动平均线）呈现。不难看出，使用多个时间框架能显著提高判断日线与日内时间框架趋势何时趋于一致的准确性。图（B-9）上的数字代表短期趋势。注意在65分钟时间框架上，股票可能跳过某些子阶段；我们始终优先采信更长期时间框架的信号。

samples.source_text: Stage 4 Decline (Downtrend). Once the definition of a trend (higher-highs and higher-lows) no longer exists on the timeframe when we entered the stock, there is no reason for a swing trader to continue to hold. When the primary trend is higher, it is likely that the breakdown of price into an intermediate-term downtrend is the start of a pullback, not a longer-term reversal. This means that the odds do not favor profitable short sales. It is better to AVOID these stocks and look for better setups elsewhere.
samples.translated_text: 阶段4：下跌（下降趋势）。一旦在我们入场时的时间框架上不再存在趋势定义（更高的高点和更高的低点），波段交易者就没有理由继续持有。当主要趋势向上时，价格跌破进入中期下降趋势很可能是回调的开始，而非长期反转。这意味着获利的空头交易概率不高。最好避开这些股票，并在其他地方寻找更好的交易机会。

samples.source_text: In Chart B-8 we see the daily chart (with 50-DMA) and in Chart B-9 the same data on a 65-minute timeframe (with 5-Day/30-Period MA). It should be easy to see why the use of more than one timeframe allows us much greater accuracy to determine where the trend of the daily and intraday timeframes become aligned. The numbers on the chart (B-9) are the shorter-term trend. Notice on the 65-minute timeframe, substages can be skipped by the stock, we always give the benefit of the doubt to the longer-term timeframe.

samples.translated_text: 第一阶段：吸筹阶段。此时我们退出空头交易。当股票显现疲态时，即进入此阶段。这可能预示着价格反弹，或需要一段“时间修正”。这是退出头寸或至少部分回补以锁定部分利润并收紧剩余头寸止损的良机，而非继续使资金暴露于风险之中。此时，5日移动平均线的下降角度将放缓或开始转为横向。

typst.markdown_text: 第二阶段标记（上升趋势）。一旦在我们进入该股票的时间框架内，趋势的定义（更低的高点和更低的低点）不再成立，波段交易者就没有理由继续做空。当主要趋势向下时，价格突破进入中期上升趋势很可能是反弹的开始，而非长期反转。这意味着获利的空头交易概率不高。最好**避开**这些股票，寻找趋势在下跌方向上趋于一致的更佳交易机会。

samples.source_text: Stage 1 Accumulation. This is where we exit our short sale trades. This is when the stock shows signs of fatigue. It may precede a price bounce, or it may need a “correction through time.” This is a good point to EXIT the position or at least buy some back to lock in partial profits and tighten the stop on the balance, rather than continue to expose our capital to risk. The angle of descent of the 5-DMA will slow down or begin to turn sideways at this time.
samples.translated_text: 第一阶段：吸筹阶段。此时我们退出空头交易。当股票显现疲态时，即进入此阶段。这可能预示着价格反弹，或需要一段“时间修正”。这是退出头寸或至少部分回补以锁定部分利润并收紧剩余头寸止损的良机，而非继续使资金暴露于风险之中。此时，5日移动平均线的下降角度将放缓或开始转为横向。

typst.markdown_text: 阶段3：派发阶段。此时股票显现疲态，可能预示价格下跌。在短期阶段3中，我们观察、分析并预判卖方将取得控制权的时点。此时应评估价格目标与止损位，以确定当股票给出介入信号时的行动计划。此时需确保已明确：股票从何处来，以及其潜在去向？随后可判断是否已形成足够的风险/回报比，以支持建立空头交易。

samples.source_text: Stage 2 Markup (Uptrend). Once the definition of a trend (lower-highs and lower-lows) no longer exists on the timeframe we entered the stock, there is no reason for a swing trader to continue to be short. When the primary trend is lower, it is likely that the breakout of price into an intermediate term uptrend is the beginning of a bounce, not a longer-term reversal. This means the odds do not favor profitable short sales. It is better to AVOID these stocks and look for better setups where trends are coming into alignment on the downside.

samples.source_text: Stage 3 Distribution. This is when the stock shows signs of fatigue and may precede a price decline. In the short-term stage 3, we observe, analyze, and ANTICIPATE the point where sellers will take control. This is the time to assess our price objectives and stop levels to determine our plan of action when the stock gives us a reason to get involved. This is when we want to be sure we have answered; where has the stock come from and where it has the potential to go? We can then determine if there is sufficient risk/reward ratio setting up to justify a short sale trade.

samples.source_text: Stage 4 Decline (Downtrend). As the stock makes a short-term lower-low, which is in alignment with the longer-term down-trend, we want to sell the stock short. This is when we PARTICIPATE in what appears to be a continuation of the downtrend on the longer-term timeframe. Stage 4 is also where we manage our winners by lowering the stop just above successive lower highs as the stock declines. This is the fun part.

typst.markdown_text: 交易员往往倾向于认为必须持续持有活跃头寸，但当不同时间框架的趋势信号混杂时，最好采取更为谨慎的立场，直至趋势一致并显现出较低风险的入场机会。现金也是一种头寸。

samples.source_text: There is a tendency for traders to feel as if they must be continually engaged in an active position, but when there are mixed trend signals across various timeframes, it is best to have a more cautious stance until trends align and show lower-risk entries. Cash is a position.

samples.source_text: Timeframe Transition
samples.translated_text: 普遍观点认为，应坚持交易初始设定的时间框架，这有其充分理由。短期交易者常常会为延长持有亏损头寸的时间寻找借口，而原本计划长期持股的投资者也应克制获取快速利润的冲动。
samples.fit_text: 普遍观点认为，应坚持交易初始设定的时间框架，这有其充分理由。短期交易者常常会为延长持有亏损头寸的时间寻找借口，而原本计划长期持股的投资者也应克制获取快速利润的冲动。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 449.34999999999997pt, height: 27.25999999999999pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p184_item_4_3_md, max_size: 8.75pt, min_size: 8.57pt, max_leading: 0.6em, min_leading: 0.57em, fit_height: 27.25999999999999pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 普遍观点认为，应坚持交易初始设定的时间框架，这有其充分理由。短期交易者常常会为延长持有亏损头寸的时间寻找借口，而原本计划长期持股的投资者也应克制获取快速利润的冲动。

samples.source_text: Common wisdom says to stick to the timeframe you intended at the onset of your trade, and for good reason. All too often, short-term traders will find a reason to justify hanging onto an unprofitable trade longer than originally planned. Similarly, someone who intends to hold a stock for the long-term should fight the urge to take a quick profit.
samples.translated_text: 普遍观点认为，应坚持交易初始设定的时间框架，这有其充分理由。短期交易者常常会为延长持有亏损头寸的时间寻找借口，而原本计划长期持股的投资者也应克制获取快速利润的冲动。
samples.fit_text: 普遍观点认为，应坚持交易初始设定的时间框架，这有其充分理由。短期交易者常常会为延长持有亏损头寸的时间寻找借口，而原本计划长期持股的投资者也应克制获取快速利润的冲动。

typst.body_expr: block(width: 447.45000000000005pt, height: 35.72000000000003pt)[#{ pdftr_fit_markdown(p184_item_5_4_md, max_size: 8.85pt, min_size: 8.5pt, max_leading: 0.56em, min_leading: 0.53em, fit_height: 35.72pt) }]
typst.markdown_text: 只有当股票处于盈利状态且你仍能管理交易风险时，才可将短期交易转为较长时间框架。以日内交易开始是可接受的，当股票走势有利时，通过部分卖出降低风险，然后持有剩余仓位以获取次日或后续收益。对于长期投资者而言，提前结束交易的唯一理由是，若基本面发生变化或止损被触发，以保护资本。

samples.source_text: The only time you should allow a short-term trade to be transitioned to a longer timeframe is if the stock is in a profitable position and you can still manage risk for the trade. It is acceptable to start a trade as a day trade, and when the stock moves in your favor, take some of the risk off with the sale of a piece of it and then hold the balance for gains into the next day or coming days. For a longer-term investor, the only reason to cut the trade early is to protect capital if the story has changed or if the stop-loss is violated.
samples.translated_text: 只有当股票处于盈利状态且你仍能管理交易风险时，才可将短期交易转为较长时间框架。以日内交易开始是可接受的，当股票走势有利时，通过部分卖出降低风险，然后持有剩余仓位以获取次日或后续收益。对于长期投资者而言，提前结束交易的唯一理由是，若基本面发生变化或止损被触发，以保护资本。
samples.fit_text: 只有当股票处于盈利状态且你仍能管理交易风险时，才可将短期交易转为较长时间框架。以日内交易开始是可接受的，当股票走势有利时，通过部分卖出降低风险，然后持有剩余仓位以获取次日或后续收益。对于长期投资者而言，提前结束交易的唯一理由是，若基本面发生变化或止损被触发，以保护资本。

typst.markdown_text: 决定延长持仓时间应基于优势而非劣势。我们始终希望首先保护资本，然后在市场允许的情况下最大化收益。

samples.source_text: The decision to hold longer than expected should be made from a position of strength, not weakness. We always want to protect capital first and then maximize the gains if the market allows it.

typst.markdown_text: 俗话说，牛市和熊市都能赚钱，而贪婪者终将亏损。我将这句话更新为：“牛市和熊市都能赚钱，贪婪的猪被屠宰，而自律的猪则致富。”若你的目标是获取巨大收益，请以自律的方式实现。

samples.source_text: It is said that bulls and bears make money and pigs get slaughtered. I have updated that phrase to; “Bulls and bears make money, greedy pigs get slaughtered, and disciplined pigs get rich.” If your goal is large gains, do it in a disciplined way.

samples.source_text: When you have been involved in a business that you have a great passion for as long as I have, 3 0 + years, you pick up a lot of knowledge from books, articles, and conversations with people. I have done my best to acknowledge the large number of people who have had a positive influence on my career and have expanded my knowledge of the financial markets. It is my sincere hope that I have not missed anyone or that I have inadvertently given credit to the wrong person.

samples.source_text: I was quite naive when I thought my book was finished prior to handing it over to my wonderful editor Tamela Rich. I had never heard of “developmental editing” before seeing her in action. She helped me organize the manuscript in its present form, then brought together a great team of people who helped with the design, layout, proofing, and all the things that make a good book great.
samples.translated_text: 在我将书稿交给我出色的编辑塔梅拉·里奇之前，我曾天真地以为我的书已经完成了。在亲眼目睹她的工作之前，我从未听说过“发展性编辑”这一概念。她帮助我将手稿组织成现在的形式，然后组建了一个优秀的团队，协助完成设计、排版、校对以及所有使一本好书变得卓越的工作。

samples.source_text: The first time I mentioned I was going to write a book about the Anchored VWAP (pronounced Vee-Wap not Vee-Wop, wink) was in a conversation with Jay Woods at a CMT Symposium in NYC in 2016. He encouraged me and provided useful feedback along the way. Thank you Jay.
samples.translated_text: 我第一次提到要写一本关于锚定成交量加权平均价格（Anchored VWAP，发音为Vee-Wap而非Vee-Wop，眨眼）的书，是在2016年纽约市CMT研讨会上与Jay Woods的一次交谈中。他给予我鼓励，并在此过程中提供了宝贵的反馈。谢谢你，Jay。

samples.source_text: Chartered Market Technician, Brian Shannon has been a securities professional for over three decades. He developed and applied the little-known AVWAP theory to his daily trading and has used the AVWAP since the early 2000s. He has been instrumental in getting this analysis tool on nearly a dozen charting platforms.

samples.source_text: This subscription is an end-of-day video analysis of key market ETFs including; SPY, QQQ, IWM, SMH, and IBB. These daily videos are a daily “masterclass” in AVWAP, and other techniques described in this book. In addition to the educational aspect they offer actionable ETF trade ideas.
samples.translated_text: 该订阅服务提供每日收盘后对关键市场ETF（包括SPY、QQQ、IWM、SMH和IBB）的视频分析。这些每日视频是关于AVWAP及本书所述其他技术的“大师课”。除教育内容外，它们还提供可操作的ETF交易思路。

samples.source_text: Brian’s original book, Technical Analysis Using Multiple Timeframes, is the top book anyone can read to understand the structure of price and trend. He builds on those concepts, going in depth on the use of the AVWAP to find low-risk entries in alignment with the trend. Brian is the trend setter in using AVWAP in active trading. This is a must-read for anyone serious about incorporating it into their strategy.
samples.translated_text: Brian 的原创著作《Technical Analysis Using Multiple Timeframes》是理解价格与趋势结构的首选读物。他在此基础上深入探讨了如何运用 AVWAP 来寻找与趋势一致的低风险入场点。Brian 是将 AVWAP 应用于主动交易的趋势引领者。对于任何认真考虑将其纳入自身策略的读者而言，本书都是必读之作。

samples.source_text: Brian Shannon is what I consider a true technician, keeping his focus squarely on the raw data of the market: volume and price. His teaching of the VWAP tools and methodology are steeped in real-world examples and explanations of why it’s useful that are written in a clear and easy-to follow-format. After learning about VWAP from Brian, it’s become a tool I consistently use on my own charts, elevating my market analysis.
samples.translated_text: 布莱恩·香农是我心目中真正的技术分析师，他始终将注意力牢牢锁定在市场原始数据上：成交量与价格。他对VWAP工具及方法的讲解，植根于现实案例，并以清晰易懂的格式阐述了其应用价值。从布莱恩那里学习了VWAP之后，它已成为我图表分析中持续使用的工具，提升了我的市场分析水平。

samples.source_text: Price tells you one thing. Volume another. There isn’t a better person than Brian Shannon to help you understand the relationship between the two. Institutions leave footprints and whether you trade long or short timeframes, breakouts or pullbacks, or stocks or commodities, it’s critical to know who is in control of the instrument—buyers or sellers—in order to be on the right side of the bullish or bearish sentiment. Brian’s study on AVWAP is the best resource to help you understand this transition of ideology and to improve your trading in a very meaningful way.
samples.translated_text: 价格传递一种信息，成交量则传递另一种。要理解两者之间的关系，没有人比Brian Shannon更合适。机构会留下足迹，无论你交易的是长线还是短线，是突破还是回调，是股票还是大宗商品，了解谁在控制该工具——买方还是卖方——对于站在看涨或看跌情绪的正确一方至关重要。Brian对AVWAP的研究是帮助你理解这种理念转变、并以极具意义的方式提升交易水平的最佳资源。

typst.markdown_text: Michael Batnick，管理合伙人，Ritholtz Wealth Management @michaelbatnick

samples.source_text: From the viewpoint of a 20-year technical trader who has learned most of his craft from the school of hard knocks, Brian’s book was a welcomed read. I’ve attempted to read other books on the subject but found them tedious, boring and quite often hard to understand. Brian manages to engage the reader and teaches in plain language the merits of VWAP and how and why he believes it works so well for trading. Always there to remind you that this tool is not a crystal ball, he invites you to view VWAP as he does, but encourages you to find how it works best in your trading. The rookie trader who is looking to explore more about VWAP will find this book invaluable as well as the season vet who can apply some of Brian’s ideas to their repertoire. I am a devout VWAP trader and found the book validating some of my own ideas as well.
samples.translated_text: 作为一名从实战经验中汲取了大部分交易技巧的20年技术交易者，布莱恩的著作令人欣然阅读。我曾尝试阅读该主题的其他书籍，但发现它们冗长乏味、枯燥且常常难以理解。布莱恩成功吸引了读者，并以通俗易懂的语言阐述了成交量加权平均价格（VWAP）的优势，以及他为何认为其在交易中如此有效。他始终提醒读者，这一工具并非水晶球，他邀请你以他的视角看待VWAP，同时鼓励你探索其在自身交易中的最佳应用方式。无论是希望深入了解VWAP的新手交易者，还是能将布莱恩的部分理念融入自身策略的经验丰富者，都会发现本书极具价值。作为一名坚定的VWAP交易者，本书也印证了我个人的一些交易理念。

samples.source_text: This book is essential reading for anyone who uses charts/technicals to navigate markets. Stylistically, the Maximum Trading Gains with the Anchored VWAP separates itself from all the other tools in the trader’s toolbox. I find the reliability and consistency in searching for price is the most valuable part of the AVWAP. Brian easily explains how to use the AVWAP as a valid weapon in these book chapters. Price, time and volume are what you’ll learn in the basic section of this book, while using other important indicators to confirm the movement.
samples.translated_text: 本书对于任何使用图表/技术分析来驾驭市场的人来说都是必读之作。从风格上讲，《基于锚定VWAP实现最大交易收益》一书在交易者的工具箱中独树一帜。我认为，在寻找价格方面，其可靠性和一致性是AVWAP最有价值的部分。Brian在本书章节中轻松地解释了如何将AVWAP作为一种有效的工具来使用。价格、时间和成交量是您将在本书基础部分学到的内容，同时还会使用其他重要指标来确认市场动向。

samples.source_text: Brian provides an excellent breakdown of the markets both in terms of the science of technical analysis and the art of trading. His experience shines through as he breaks down price action and key AVWAP concepts in an easy to understand fashion and provides actionable, practical strategies that traders can adopt and excel with. I would highly recommend this book to any trader looking to add new tools to their repertoire.
samples.translated_text: Brian 对市场进行了出色的剖析，既涵盖了技术分析的科学性，也涉及了交易的艺术性。他的经验在剖析价格行为和关键AVWAP概念时熠熠生辉，方式易于理解，并提供了可操作、实用的策略，交易者可以采用并借此取得优异成绩。我强烈推荐这本书给任何希望为其技能库增添新工具的交易者。

samples.source_text: Brian warned me that this was a highly technical book. But it is also a very thoughtful and well-researched book. I spent a 9-year career on Wall Street without ever spending much time thinking of the implications of VWAP. Now I know.

samples.source_text: Brian Shannon does an excellent job of breaking down the complex topic of Anchored VWAP into its building blocks, a core component of any institutional trading strategy. But he does not stop there. Instead, he continues to delve into many AVWAP strategies to illustrate and educate the reader on how to employ them in their own trading. Another well done gem from Brian. Bravo.
samples.translated_text: Brian Shannon 出色地将机构交易策略的核心组成部分——锚定VWAP这一复杂主题——分解为基本构成要素。但他并未止步于此，而是继续深入探讨多种AVWAP策略，以阐释并指导读者如何将其应用于自身交易。这是Brian带来的又一佳作。值得称赞。

samples.source_text: When any trader or investor makes decisions based on inputs that actually matter to the markets, they instantly gain edge. AVWAP is one of those edge defining inputs and once again, Brian Shannon has given the trading and investing world a key to unlock its potential. AVWAP has been a game changer for me and the traders our firm develops. This book is soon to be a classic. If you want to make better trading and investing decisions and don’t buy this book…. I’m not sure how else to help you.
samples.translated_text: 当任何交易者或投资者基于对市场真正重要的输入做出决策时，他们便立即获得了优势。AVWAP 正是这类定义优势的输入之一，而 Brian Shannon 再次为交易和投资界提供了一把解锁其潜力的钥匙。AVWAP 对我本人以及我们公司所培养的交易员而言，一直是一个改变游戏规则的工具。这本书即将成为经典。如果你想做出更好的交易和投资决策，却不购买这本书……我不知道还能如何帮助你了。

samples.source_text: Brian Shannon shares many trading gems while sorting through the puzzle pieces of technical analysis to provide active traders with an edge using anchored volume-weighted average price (AVWAP) trends. The beauty of his approach is that it tunes out the noise all traders need to deal with and focuses on the undeniable and essential elements of price, volume, and time. Brian devotes a chapter to IPOs and shows how AVWAP can be applied to multiple timeframes when analyzing new issues with limited trading history. New and experienced traders alike are sure to find this book invaluable while honing their trading strategy.
samples.translated_text: Brian Shannon 在梳理技术分析的拼图时分享了许多交易智慧，为活跃交易者提供了利用锚定成交量加权平均价格（AVWAP）趋势的优势。其方法的精妙之处在于，它过滤了所有交易者都需要应对的市场噪音，专注于价格、成交量与时间这些不可否认且至关重要的要素。Brian 专门用一章讨论首次公开募股（IPO），并展示了在分析交易历史有限的新股时，如何将 AVWAP 应用于多个时间框架。无论是新手还是经验丰富的交易者，在打磨其交易策略时，都必将发现本书极具价值。

samples.source_text: The Anchored-VWAP is one of the most powerful discretionary indicators for traders. It very efficiently demonstrates the mass psychology of the market by putting in one place all the elements of trading: time, volume, and price. Brian Shannon has done an excellent job pioneering and developing this indicator, as well as teaching traders how to effectively use it.
samples.translated_text: Anchored-VWAP是交易者最强大的自主判断指标之一。它通过将交易的所有要素——时间、成交量和价格——整合于一处，极为高效地展示了市场的群体心理。Brian Shannon在开创和发展该指标，以及教导交易者如何有效运用它方面，做出了卓越的贡献。

Let Brian show you how to gain a serious edge in your trading by using the AVWAP.

samples.source_text: Brian has put together an easy-to-understand book that convincingly states the case for using AVWAP asa way to increase profitability while limiting risk.The book is written from the perspec- tivethat eachtrader isdifferentandshouldfindhisownapproachtousingAvwAP.After covering AVWAP basics,Brian dives right in with specific strategies.Highlyrecommended!
samples.translated_text: Brian撰写了一本通俗易懂的著作，有力地论证了使用AVWAP作为提高盈利能力同时限制风险的方法。该书基于每位交易者各不相同、应找到自身使用AVWAP方式的视角撰写。在介绍AVWAP基础知识后，Brian直接深入探讨了具体策略。强烈推荐！

samples.source_text: Innovation is rare intechnical analysisas the field has beenworkedintensivelyfora long time,and thelow-hangingfruitislong gone.Ifirstcameacross VolumeWeightedAveragePrice,VWAPas it isknown,manyyearsagoasatrading techniqueforlargeordersusedmostly byinstitutions.More recentlyit has surfacedas ananalytical technique,Anchored VWAP.To mymind that isareal innovationand Brian Shannon is theanalyst to tell you what it isand howto use it.
samples.translated_text: 技术创新在技术分析领域较为罕见，因为该领域已历经长期深入研究，易于取得的成果早已被发掘。我最初接触成交量加权平均价格（VWAP）是在多年前，当时它主要被机构用作大额订单的交易技术。近年来，它又以锚定VWAP这一分析技术的形式重新出现。在我看来，这是一项真正的创新，而Brian Shannon正是向您阐释其原理与应用方法的分析师。
